title: Switch>
cmd:
Switch>                # User EXEC mode
Switch> enable         # Privileged EXEC mode
Switch# configure terminal   # Global config mode
Switch(config)# interface fa0/1
Switch(config-if)# description Uplink-to-Router
desc: Configuration Modes

title: show running-config
cmd:
show running-config        # Displays the active configuration in RAM
show startup-config        # Displays the saved config in NVRAM (loaded on boot)
show vlan brief            # Lists VLANs, names, status, and assigned ports
show interfaces status     # Shows interface status (UP/DOWN, VLAN, speed, duplex)
show mac address-table     # Displays learned MAC addresses and their ports
show spanning-tree         # Shows STP information (root bridge, port roles/states)
desc: Important show Commands

title: show running-config | include vlan
cmd:
show running-config | include vlan        # Show only lines containing "vlan"
show interfaces | begin GigabitEthernet0/1   # Display output starting from Gi0/1
show mac address-table | exclude dynamic  # Hide all lines with the word "dynamic"
desc: Filtering Information from show Commands

title: interface range gi0/1 - 10
cmd:
interface range gi0/1 - 10     # Select multiple interfaces at once (Gi0/1 to Gi0/10)
switchport mode access         # Set all selected ports to access mode
switchport access vlan 20      # Assign VLAN 20 to all selected ports
desc: Managing Multiple Interfaces

title: vlan 10
cmd:
vlan 10              # Create VLAN 10
name VLAN10           # Assign a name ("VLAN10") to the VLAN
exit                 # Exit VLAN configuration mode
desc: VLANs > Creating VLANs

title: no vlan 10
cmd:
no vlan 10           # Delete VLAN 10
delete flash:vlan.dat   # Delete the entire VLAN database from flash
desc: VLANs > Deleting VLANs

title: interface gi0/1
cmd:
interface gi0/1             # Enter interface Gi0/1
switchport mode access       # Set port as access
switchport access vlan 10    # Assign VLAN 10 to the port
desc: VLANs > Assigning Interfaces to a VLAN (Access)

title: interface gi0/24
cmd:
interface gi0/24                          # Enter trunk interface (Gi0/24)
switchport mode trunk                     # Set port as trunk
switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30    # Allow only VLANs 10, 20, and 30
switchport trunk native vlan 99           # Set VLAN 99 as the native VLAN
desc: Trunks > Configuring Trunks

title: switchport mode dynamic desirable
cmd:
switchport mode dynamic desirable   # Actively try to form a trunk
switchport mode dynamic auto        # Passively form a trunk if other side is trunk/desirable
switchport nonegotiate              # Disable DTP negotiation
desc: Trunks > Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP)

title: show vlan brief
cmd:
show vlan brief             # Display all VLANs and their assigned ports
show interfaces trunk       # Verify trunk ports and allowed VLANs
show mac address-table      # View MAC addresses learned on the switch
show running-config         # Check VLAN and interface configurations
desc: VLAN Troubleshooting

title: interface gi0/5
cmd:
interface gi0/5              # Enter interface Gi0/5
switchport mode access       # Set port as access
switchport access vlan 10    # Assign VLAN 10 for data
switchport voice vlan 20     # Assign VLAN 20 for voice traffic (IP phones)
desc: Voice VLANs

title: hostname Switch1
cmd:
hostname Switch1                        # Set device hostname  
ip domain-name example.com              # Define domain name (needed for RSA key)  
crypto key generate rsa                 # Generate RSA keys for SSH  
username admin privilege 15 secret cisco123   # Create local admin user  
line vty 0 4                            # Enter VTY line configuration (remote access)  
 transport input ssh                    # Allow only SSH (disable Telnet)  
 login local                            # Use local user database for login
desc: SSH > Initial SSH Setup

title: ip ssh version 2
cmd:
ip ssh version 2                        # Enable SSH version 2 (more secure)  
ip ssh time-out 60                      # Set SSH idle timeout to 60 seconds  
ip ssh authentication-retries 3         # Allow max 3 login attempts
desc: SSH > Modifying SSH Config

title: interface gi0/2
cmd:
interface gi0/2                              # Enter interface  
switchport mode access                       # Set interface to access mode  
switchport port-security                     # Enable port security  
switchport port-security maximum 2           # Allow max 2 MAC addresses  
switchport port-security violation shutdown  # Shutdown port if violation occurs
desc: Port Security > Dynamic Port Security

title: interface gi0/2
cmd:
interface gi0/2                              # Enter interface  
switchport port-security mac-address sticky  # Learn & save MAC addresses dynamically
desc: Port Security > Sticky Port Security

title: show port-security interface gi0/2
cmd:
show port-security interface gi0/2           # Check security settings for interface  
show port-security address                   # View secure MAC addresses learned
desc: Port Security > Verifying Port Security

title: show interface status err-disabled
cmd:
show interface status err-disabled           # Show interfaces in error-disabled state  
errdisable recovery cause psecure-violation  # Enable automatic recovery for port-security violations  
errdisable recovery interval 300             # Set recovery interval (300 seconds)
desc: Port Security > Err-Disabled Interfaces

title: vtp mode server
cmd:
vtp mode server                 # Set switch as VTP server (can create/modify VLANs)  
vtp domain CCNA-Lab             # Define VTP domain name (must match across switches)  
vtp password cisco123           # Set a password for VTP updates
desc: VLAN Trunking Protocol > Configuring VTP

title: show vtp status
cmd: show vtp status                 # Display VTP mode, domain, and revision number
desc: VLAN Trunking Protocol > VTP Verification

title: CDevice(config)#no ip domain-lookup
desc: | disable DNS lookup

title: CDevice(config-line)#history size [lines]
desc: | specify the size (number of lines) for the history of executed commands (you can view your history with `R1#show history`)

title: CDevice(config-line)#end
desc: | exit to EXEC privileged mode, where the next command will be executed

title: CDevice#copy running-config startup-config
desc: | Saves the running configuration to the NVRAM

title: ena
cmd:
ena
conf t
no ip domain-lookup
cdp run
line con 0
logging syn
his size 50
exec-timeout 25 0
end
copy run start

desc: Important show commands | display config saved in RAM (and the currently running configuration in the device)

title: show startup-config
desc: Important show commands | display config saved in NVRAM

title: show history
desc: Important show commands |

title: ip route [network-address] [subnet-mask] ....
desc: Static routing

title: ip route [network-address] [subnet-mask] ([next-hop-ip] [exit-int])
desc: Static routing > Standard Static Route

title: ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 ([next-hop-ip] [exit-int])
desc: Static routing > Default Static Route

title: ip route [network-address] [subnet-mask] ([next-hop-ip] [exit-int])...
desc: Static routing > Floating Static Route

title: ip route [network-address] [subnet-mask*] ([next-hop-ip] [exit-int])
desc: Static routing > Summary Static Route

title: router ospf [PID]
desc: Dynamic routing OSPF > OSPF configuration | create OSPF process with Process ID [PID] (1 - 65535)

title: router-id [a.b.c.d]
desc: Dynamic routing OSPF > OSPF configuration | manually assign the router an ID, in an IPv4 address format.

title: show ip ospf interface brief
desc: Dynamic routing OSPF > OSPF verification | summary of router's OSPF-enabled interfaces. :warning: This command does NOT seem to work on Packet Tracer 7.3

title: show ip ospf interface
desc: Dynamic routing OSPF > OSPF verification | in-detail list of every OSPF-enabled interface

title: show ip ospf interface [int-id]
desc: Dynamic routing OSPF > OSPF verification | in-detail information for a particular OSPF-enabled interface

title: interface g0/0.[vlan-id]
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | create the `.[vlan-id]` **subinterface** on interface Gigabit Ethernet 0/0

title: encapsulation dot1q [vlan-id]
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | configure subinterface to operate on a specified VLAN

title: encapsulation dot1q [vlan-id] native
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | must be configured on the subinterfaace belonging to the **native** VLAN

title: ip address [ip-address] [subnet-mask]
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | :exclamation: don't forget to assign the subinterface an IP address!

title: interface g0/0
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | access the Gigabit0/0 interface (i.e., the actual physical interface) to enable it

title: no shutdown
desc: Configuring Router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing | enable the physical interface. This enables **all** configured subinterfaces **on that interface**

title: access-list [number] [permit/deny] [address] [wildcard mask]
desc: nopedestrians Access Control Lists (ACL) > Procedure for configuring Standard Numbered ACLs | create entry in standard IPv4 ACL with specified number

title: interface [int-id]
desc: nopedestrians Access Control Lists (ACL) > Procedure for configuring Standard Numbered ACLs | select the interface to which the ACL will be applied

title: ip access-group [number] [in/out]
desc: nopedestrians Access Control Lists (ACL) > Procedure for configuring Standard Numbered ACLs | activate the ACL on the selected interface

title: no access-list [number]
desc: nopedestrians Access Control Lists (ACL) > Procedure for configuring Standard Numbered ACLs | :warning: This **ERASES** the ACL, i.e., **EVERY ACE** (Access Control entry) **that makes it up**

title: ip access-list [standard] [name]
desc: nopedestrians Access Control Lists (ACL) > Procedure for configuring Standard Named ACLs | creates a standard named ACL. Will take you to standard (`std) named ACL (nacl`) configuration mode after entering it

title: ip dhcp excludded-address [start-address (end-address)]
desc: DHCPv4 > Configure a Cisco router as a DHCPv4 server | exclude a single or a range of IPv4 addresses, i.e., these addresses will not be available for DHCP assignment to hosts

title: ip dhcp pool [pool name]
desc: DHCPv4 > Configure a Cisco router as a DHCPv4 server | create and name the pool of available addresses to assign. Takes you to **DHCP config mode**.

title: R1(dhcp-config)#default-router [address]
desc: DHCPv4 > Configure a Cisco router as a DHCPv4 server | specify the IPv4 address of the default gateway (router) the hosts will use

title: ip helper address [DHCP-server-address]
desc: DHCPv4 > The ip helper address | indicates the router to relay (instead of discarding) DHCP messages to the DHCP server's address.

title: ip nat inside source static [local-add] [global-add]
desc: NAT > Configuring Static NAT | configure static nat, specifying the local address that will be mapped to a global address

title: ip nat inside
desc: NAT > Inside and outside interfaces |

title: ip nat outside
desc: NAT > Inside and outside interfaces |

title: ip nat pool [pool-name] [start-address] [end-address] {netmask [mas...
desc: NAT > Configuring Dynamic NAT | prefix-length [prefix-length]}``

title: access-list [number] {permit \
desc: NAT > Configuring Dynamic NAT | deny} [source] [wildcard mask]``

title: ip nat inside source list [acl-number] pool [pool name]
cmd: ip nat inside source list [acl-number] pool [pool name]
desc: NAT > Configuring Dynamic NAT | we now bind the pool to the ACL

title: standby version 2
desc: HSRP | (optional) specify HSRP version 2 will be used. Otherwise, your HSRP instance will be running in version 1 by default.

title: standby [group] ip [virtual-ip]
desc: HSRP | specify the router group and the virtual gateway's IP address

title: standby [group] priority [0-255]
desc: HSRP | (optional) specify each router a priority for **active router** election

title: standby [group] preempt
desc: HSRP | (optional) enables **preemption**, i.e., a router with higher priority will take the active role if current active router's priority is lower

title: standby [group] timers [hello-timer] [hold-timer]
desc: HSRP | specify hello and hold (dead) timers. HSRP automatically ensures that the hold timer value is greater than the hello timer value to avoid incorrect behavior.

title: router rip
desc: Dynamic routing RIP > RIPv1 configuration | enter router configuration mode, with RIP as the routing protocol

title: passive-interface [int-id]
desc: Dynamic routing RIP > RIPv1 configuration | (optional) - prevent an interface from sending RIP updates

title: no auto-summary
desc: Dynamic routing RIP > RIPv2 configuration | disable network auto summarization

title: router eigrp [AS-number]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Configuration | [AS-number] value range: 1-65535.

title: eigrp router-id [a.b.c.d]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Configuration | (optional) manually configure a **router ID**, in an IPv4 address format

title: show ip eigrp neighbors
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Verification | displays the nighbor table. Use it to verify the router recognizes its neighbors.

title: show ip route eigrp
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Verification | display **only EIGRP** entries in the routing table

title: show ip eigrp interface
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Verification | verifies which interface are enabled for EIGRP, number of peers, and transmit queues

title: ip hello-interval eigrp [AS-number] [time in seconds]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Fine tuning | modify hello interval on IPv4. :warning: this is done in **interface configuration mode**

title: ipv6 hello-interval eigrp [AS-number] [time in seconds]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Fine tuning | modify hello interval on IPv6. :warning: this is done in **interface configuration mode**

title: ip hold-time eigrp [AS-number] [time in seconds]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Fine tuning | modify hold timer length on IPv4 :warning: this is done in **interface configuration mode**

title: ipv6 hold-time eigrp [AS-number] [time in seconds]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP > EIGRP Fine tuning | modify hold timer length on IPv6 :warning: this is done in **interface configuration mode**

title: ipv6 unicast-routing
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Configuration | this **global configuration command** enables the router to forward IPv6 packets

title: ipv6 router eigrp [AS-number]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Configuration | enter router configuration mode for EIGRP for IPv6, specifying the AS number

title: ipv6 eigrp [AS-number]
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Configuration | use this command with the AS-number you used when configuring EIGRP for IPv6 on **each desired interface**

title: show ipv6 protocols
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Verification | verifies the current configured values for EIGRP (and any additional enabled routing protocol on the device)

title: show ipv6 eigrp neighbors
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Verification | displays the nighbor table. Use it to verify the router recognizes its neighbors.

title: show ipv6 route eigrp
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Verification | display **only EIGRP** entries in the routing table

title: show ipv6 eigrp interface
desc: Dynamic routing EIGRP for IPv6 > Verification | verifies which interface are enabled for EIGRP, number of peers, and transmit queues

title: S1#show running-config
desc: Important show commands | N/A

title: S1#show history
desc: Important show commands |

title: S1#show interface [int-id]
desc: Important show commands | useful to detect errors or verify packets are being sent and received

title: S1#show mac address-table
desc: Important show commands |

title: S1#show port-security
desc: Important show commands | displays Port Security configuration for all interfaces

title: S1#show port-security interface [int-id]
desc: Important show commands | display Port Security configuration of an interface

title: S1#show vlan
desc: Important show commands |

title: S1#show vlan brief
desc: Important show commands | **only** displays VLANs, statuses, names, and assigned ports

title: S1#show interface vlan [id]
desc: Important show commands |

title: S1#show interfaces trunk
desc: Important show commands |

title: S1(config)#vlan [vlan-ID]
desc: VLANs > Configuring VLANs | create VLAN and assign its VLAN number

title: S1(config-vlan)#name [someName]
desc: VLANs > Configuring VLANs | assign a name to the VLAN

title: S1(config)#interface [int-id]
desc: VLANs > Configuring VLANs | remember, `interface range` might be useful

title: S1(config-if)#switchport mode access
desc: VLANs > Configuring VLANs |

title: S1(config-if)#switchport access vlan [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Configuring VLANs | assign/change port VLAN

title: S1(config)#no vlan [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Deleting a VLAN | :warning: deletes specified VLAN

title: S1(config)#delete flash:vlan.dat
desc: VLANs > Deleting a VLAN | :warning: erases **the whole VLAN database**

title: S1(config-if)#no switchport access vlan [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Removing interface(s) from a VLAN | remove the VLAN from the port

title: S1(config-if)#switchport mode trunk
desc: VLANs > Configuring IEEE 802.1q trunk links |

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk native vlan [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Configuring IEEE 802.1q trunk links |

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk allowed vlan [vlan-list]
desc: VLANs > Configuring IEEE 802.1q trunk links | **All** allowed VLAN IDs.

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk allowed vlan remove [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Configuring IEEE 802.1q trunk links | :no_pedestrians: **PROHIBITS ONLY** the VLAN with the specified ID on the trunk interface

title: S1(config-if)#switchport mode dynamic auto
desc: VLANs > Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) | interface will convert into a **trunk interface** if its neighboring interface is in **mode `trunk or desirable` ONLY**

title: S1(config-if)#switchport mode dynamic desirable
desc: VLANs > Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) | interface will convert into a **trunk interface** if its neighboring interface is in **mode `trunk, dynamic auto, or dynamic desirable` ONLY**

title: S1(config-if)#switchport nonegotiate
desc: VLANs > Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) | :no_entry: stops DTP negotiation, in which interfaces may engage, as you saw above, i.e.,  an interface will NOT change its mode even if the neighboring interface could change it through negotiation

title: S1#show interfaces [int-id] switchport
desc: VLANs > Troubleshooting VLANs | helpful in verifying an inactive VLAN is assigned to a port

title: S1(config-if)#switchport voice vlan [vlan-id]
desc: VLANs > Voice VLANs | assign a voice VLAN to that port

title: S1#show ip ssh
desc: Configuring SSH | Use it to verify that the switch supports SSH

title: S1(config)#ip domain-name [domain-name]
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config)#crypto key generate rsa
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config)#line vty 0 15
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config-line)#transport input ssh
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config-line)#login local
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config-line)#exit
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config)#ip ssh version 2
desc: Configuring SSH | enable SSH version 2

title: S1(config)#crypto key zeroise rsa
desc: Configuring SSH | :warning: use to **delete** RSA key pair

title: S1(config)#ip ssh time-out [time]
desc: Configuring SSH > Modifying SSH configuration | Change timeout setting (time in seconds)

title: S1(config)#ip ssh authentication-retries [retries]
desc: Configuring SSH > Modifying SSH configuration | Change number of allowed authentication attempts

title: S1(config-if)#switchport port-security
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey Configuring Dynamic Port Security | Enable port security on the interface

title: S1(config-if)#switchport port-security violation [violation-mode]
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey Configuring Dynamic Port Security | set violation mode (`protect, restrict, shutdown`)

title: S1(config-if)#switchport port-security maximum [max-addresses]
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey Configuring Sticky Port Security | Set maximum number of secure MAC addresses allowed on port

title: S1(config-if)#switchport port-security mac-address sticky
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey Configuring Sticky Port Security | Enable sticky learning

title: S1#show port-security address
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey whitecheckmark Verifying Port Security  secure MAC addresses | displays secure MAC addresses configured on **all switch interfaces**

title: S1#show interface [int-id] status
desc: Port Security > closedlockwithkey whitecheckmark Verifying Port Security  secure MAC addresses | displays port status. Useful to verify if an interface is in `err-disabled` status.

title: S1#show vtp status
desc: VLAN trunking protocol (VTP) > VTP verification | verify your configuration and the status of VTP on the device

title: S1#show vtp password
desc: VLAN trunking protocol (VTP) > VTP verification | verify the configured VTP password

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree vlan [vlan-id] root primary
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Bridge ID configuration | ensures this switch has the lowest priority value

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree vlan [vlan-id] root secondary
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Bridge ID configuration | Use if the configuration of an alternative bridge is desired. Sets the switch priority value to ensure it becomes the root bridge if the primary root bridge fails.

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree vlan [vlan-id] priority [priority]
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Bridge ID configuration | manually configure the bridge's priority value

title: S1#show spanning-tree
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Bridge ID Verification | verify current spanning-tree instances and root bridges

title: S1(config)#interface range [int-type][lowest-id]-[highest-id]
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard | access a range of contiguous interfaces if necessary

title: S1(config-if)#spanning-tree portfast
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard | enables PortFast on the access port(s)

title: S1(config-if)#spanning-tree bpduguard enable
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard | enables BPDU Guard on the access port(s)

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree portfast default
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard | :warning: configures PortFast to be the default for all switch interfaces

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree bpduguard default
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard | :warning: configures BPDU Guard to be the default for all switch interfaces

title: S1#show running-config interface [int-id]
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > PortFast and BPDU guard verification | display the current configuration portion corresponding to the interface

title: S1(config)#spanning-tree mode rapid-pvst
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Configuring Rapid PVST | configure Rapid PVST+ as the STP mode on the switch

title: S1(config-if)#spanning-tree link-type point-to-point
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Configuring Rapid PVST | specify that a link is point-to-point

title: S1#clear spanning-tree detected-protocols (interface [int-id])
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > Configuring Rapid PVST | forces renegotiation with neighboring switches on all interfaces or the specified interface

title: S1#show spanning-tree active
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | display STP information for active interfaces only

title: S1#show spanning-tree brief
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | at-a-glance information for all STP instances running on the switch

title: S1#show spanning-tree detail
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | detailed information for all STP instances running on the switch

title: S1#show spanning-tree interface [int-id]
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | STP information for the specified interface

title: S1#show spanning-tree vlan [vlan-id]
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | STP information for the specified VLAN

title: S1#show spanning-tree summary
desc: Spanning Tree Protocol > General STP verification commands | summary of STP port states

title: S1(config)#interface range [start-int]-[end-int]
desc: EtherChannel | start by selecting the interfaces to be bundled into a **single logical link**, i.e., the EtherChannel.

title: S1(config)#interface port-channel [number]
desc: EtherChannel | enter the **port channel interface configuration mode** to change settings

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk native vlan [native-vlan-id]
desc: EtherChannel > PortChannel interface additional configuration | specify the link's native VLAN

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk allowed vlan [vlan-id-1 (,vlan-id-2,...
desc: EtherChannel > PortChannel interface additional configuration | specify allowed VLANs (VLAN IDs) on trunk link

title: S1(config-if)#switchport trunk allowed vlan add [vlan-id-1 (,vlan-i...
desc: EtherChannel > PortChannel interface additional configuration | **add** VLANs to the list of **already allowed** VLANs on the trunk link

title: Save config
cmd:
copy running-config startup-config
! short form: write memory
desc: Persist running config to NVRAM.

title: Reload device
desc: Reboot the device (with optional schedule).

title: Show inventory
desc: Chassis, line cards, SFP/transceiver inventory.

title: Show environment
desc: PSU, fans, temperature sensors.

title: Show CPU
desc: Top processes by CPU usage.

title: Show memory
desc: Memory pools and free/used.

title: Set hostname
desc: Configure device hostname.

title: Set clock / timezone
cmd:
clock timezone UTC 0
clock set 12:00:00 1 Jan 2026
desc: Configure system time and timezone.

title: Show file system
desc: List storage devices and contents.

title: Copy file (TFTP/SCP)
cmd:
copy tftp: flash:
copy scp: flash:
desc: Transfer files via TFTP/SCP.

title: Enable mode / privilege
desc: Privilege escalation and config mode.

title: Show boot variable
desc: Configured boot image and config-register.

title: Show license
desc: License status (Smart Licensing / classic).

title: Show interfaces
desc: Interface status, counters, errors.

title: Show interface description
desc: Per-interface descriptions and status.

title: Configure IP address
cmd:
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 description WAN-uplink
 ip address 10.0.0.1 255.255.255.252
 no shutdown
desc: Assign IPv4 address and enable interface.

title: Configure IPv6 address
cmd:
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ipv6 address 2001:db8::1/64
 ipv6 enable
desc: Assign IPv6 address.

title: Subinterface (802.1Q)
cmd:
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.10
 encapsulation dot1Q 10
 ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0
desc: VLAN-tagged subinterface for router-on-a-stick.

title: Loopback
desc: Logical loopback interface (router-id, BGP source).

title: MTU / IP MTU
cmd:
interface Gi0/0
 mtu 9216
 ip mtu 9000
 ipv6 mtu 9000
desc: L2 MTU and L3 MTU.

title: Speed / duplex
desc: Force speed and duplex.

title: Clear counters
desc: Reset interface statistics.

title: Shutdown / no shutdown
desc: Administratively disable/enable interface.

title: Etherchannel / Port-channel
cmd:
interface range Gi1/0/1-2
 channel-group 1 mode active
interface Port-channel1
 switchport mode trunk
desc: LACP bundle of two interfaces.

title: Show optics / transceiver
desc: SFP/QSFP DDM (RX/TX power, temp).

title: Create VLAN
desc: Create a VLAN with a name.

title: Access port
cmd:
interface Gi1/0/1
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 100
desc: Tag-untagged single-VLAN port.

title: Trunk port
cmd:
interface Gi1/0/24
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 100,200,300
desc: 802.1Q trunk allowing specific VLANs.

title: Voice VLAN
cmd:
interface Gi1/0/2
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 10
 switchport voice vlan 20
desc: Data + voice VLAN on the same port.

title: Show MAC address-table
cmd:
show mac address-table
show mac address-table dynamic vlan 100
desc: L2 forwarding table.

title: Spanning-tree show
desc: STP roles, state, root info.

title: Rapid-PVST / MST
cmd:
spanning-tree mode rapid-pvst
! or:
spanning-tree mode mst
desc: Select STP mode.

title: Portfast / BPDU guard
cmd:
interface Gi1/0/5
 spanning-tree portfast
 spanning-tree bpduguard enable
desc: Edge-port hardening.

title: Static route
desc: IPv4/IPv6 static routes.

title: Default route
desc: Quad-zero default.

title: Floating static
desc: Backup route with higher administrative distance.

title: Show IP route
cmd:
show ip route
show ip route bgp
show ip route 10.10.10.0
desc: Routing table by protocol or prefix.

title: Show route summary
desc: Counts of routes per protocol.

title: OSPF basic config
cmd:
router ospf 1
 router-id 1.1.1.1
 network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
 passive-interface default
 no passive-interface Gi0/0
desc: Single-process OSPF v2.

title: OSPF on interface (modern)
cmd:
interface Gi0/0
 ip ospf 1 area 0
 ip ospf network point-to-point
 ip ospf hello-interval 5
 ip ospf dead-interval 20
desc: Per-interface OSPF (preferred for clarity).

title: OSPFv3 (IPv6)
cmd:
ipv6 router ospf 1
 router-id 1.1.1.1
interface Gi0/0
 ipv6 ospf 1 area 0
desc: OSPFv3 for IPv6.

title: Show OSPF neighbors
cmd:
show ip ospf neighbor
show ipv6 ospf neighbor
desc: OSPF adjacencies.

title: Show OSPF database
desc: LSDB summary.

title: OSPF authentication
cmd:
interface Gi0/0
 ip ospf authentication message-digest
 ip ospf message-digest-key 1 md5 SECRET
desc: MD5 authentication.

title: EIGRP classic
desc: Classic EIGRP autonomous system.

title: EIGRP named
cmd:
router eigrp CORP
 address-family ipv4 unicast autonomous-system 100
  network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255
desc: Named EIGRP (recommended modern form).

title: Show EIGRP neighbors
cmd:
show ip eigrp neighbors
show ip eigrp topology
desc: Neighbors and topology table.

title: BGP basic config
cmd:
router bgp 65001
 bgp router-id 1.1.1.1
 neighbor 10.0.0.2 remote-as 65002
 address-family ipv4
  network 10.10.10.0 mask 255.255.255.0
  neighbor 10.0.0.2 activate
desc: Single eBGP peer.

title: BGP iBGP + loopback
cmd:
router bgp 65001
 neighbor 2.2.2.2 remote-as 65001
 neighbor 2.2.2.2 update-source Loopback0
 neighbor 2.2.2.2 next-hop-self
desc: iBGP peering sourced from loopback.

title: Show BGP summary
cmd:
show ip bgp summary
show bgp ipv4 unicast summary
desc: Neighbor uptime + prefix counts.

title: Show BGP routes
cmd:
show ip bgp
show ip bgp 10.10.10.0/24
show ip bgp neighbors 10.0.0.2 advertised-routes
desc: BGP table and advertised/received per neighbor.

title: Clear BGP neighbor (soft)
cmd:
clear ip bgp 10.0.0.2 soft
clear ip bgp * soft in
desc: Soft-reset to re-apply policy without dropping session.

title: BGP prefix-list filter
cmd:
ip prefix-list ALLOW seq 5 permit 10.10.10.0/24
router bgp 65001
 neighbor 10.0.0.2 prefix-list ALLOW out
desc: Outbound prefix filter.

title: BGP route-map (set local-pref)
cmd:
route-map PREF permit 10
 set local-preference 200
router bgp 65001
 neighbor 10.0.0.2 route-map PREF in
desc: Influence inbound path selection.

title: BGP MD5 auth
desc: TCP MD5 authentication.

title: Standard ACL
cmd:
access-list 10 permit 10.10.10.0 0.0.0.255
access-list 10 deny any log
desc: Numbered standard ACL.

title: Extended (named) ACL
cmd:
ip access-list extended WEB
 permit tcp any host 10.10.10.10 eq 443
 deny ip any any log
interface Gi0/0
 ip access-group WEB in
desc: Named L3/L4 ACL applied to an interface.

title: Show ACL hit counts
cmd:
show access-lists
show ip access-lists WEB
desc: Per-line counters.

title: VTY access control
cmd:
line vty 0 4
 access-class MGMT in
 transport input ssh
 login local
desc: Restrict management plane access.

title: Static NAT (1:1)
cmd:
ip nat inside source static 10.10.10.10 198.51.100.10
interface Gi0/0
 ip nat outside
interface Gi0/1
 ip nat inside
desc: 1-to-1 mapping insideâoutside.

title: PAT (overload)
cmd:
access-list 1 permit 10.10.10.0 0.0.0.255
ip nat inside source list 1 interface Gi0/0 overload
desc: Many-to-one with port reuse.

title: Show NAT translations
cmd:
show ip nat translations
show ip nat statistics
desc: Active translations and counters.

title: Class-map / policy-map
cmd:
class-map match-any VOICE
 match dscp ef
policy-map WAN-OUT
 class VOICE
  priority percent 30
interface Gi0/0
 service-policy output WAN-OUT
desc: MQC: classify, mark, queue.

title: Show policy-map
desc: Per-class counters and drops.

title: HSRP
cmd:
interface Vlan10

title: action bash
desc: The action bash command specifies a Bash shell command to be run when an event handler is triggered.

title: alias
cmd:
alias alias_name command_name
no alias alias_name
default alias alias_name
desc: The alias command creates an alias for a CLI command.

title: bash
desc: The bash command starts the Linux Bash shell.

title: comment (various configuration modes)
cmd:
comment comment_text EOF
no comment
default comment !! comment_text
desc: The comment command adds a comment for the active configuration mode to running-config.

title: configure (configure terminal)
desc: The configure command places the switch in the Global Configuration mode to configure features at the system level.

title: configure checkpoint
desc: The configure checkpoint command saves the running configuration to a checkpoint file.

title: configure convert
desc: The configure convert command converts the current configuration syntax to the specified syntax.

title: configure network
desc: The configure network command is deprecated.

title: copy running-config
desc: The current operating configuration of the switch is stored in a virtual file called running-config.

title: daemon
cmd:
daemon daemon_name
no daemon daemon_name
default daemon daemon_name
desc: The daemon command accesses daemon configuration mode for adding or removing external daemons and scripts, which are then managed by ProcMgr.

title: delay
desc: The delay command specifies the time in seconds the system will delay between a triggering event and the execution of an event handler action.

title: dir
desc: The dir command displays a list of files on a file system.

title: disable
desc: The disable command exchanges the session’s current command mode with the specified privilege level.

title: enable
desc: The enable command places the switch in Privileged EXEC mode.

title: event-handler DropCountersHandler
cmd:
event-handler DropCountersHandler
no event-handler DropCountersHandler
default event-handler DropCountersHandler
desc: The event-handler DropCountersHandler command enables the adverse drop counters monitor with event handlers.

title: event-handler
cmd:
event-handler name
no event-handler name
default event-handler name
desc: An event handler executes a Linux Bash shell command in response to a specific system event.

title: ip ftp client source-interface
cmd:
ip ftp [client] source-interface INTERFACE [vrf vrf_name]
no ip ftp [client] source-interface
default ip ftp [client] source-interface
desc: By default, the FTP (File Transfer Protocol) source IP address is selected by the switch (the IP address of the source interface if one is assigned).

title: ip http client local-interface
cmd:
ip http client local-interface INTERFACE [vrf vrf_name]
no ip http client local-interface
default ip http client local-interface
desc: The ip http client local-interface command specifies the source IP address for hypertext transfer protocol (HTTP) connections.

title: ip ssh client source-interface
cmd:
ip ssh [client] source-interface INTERFACE [vrf vrf_name]
no ip ssh [client] source-interface
default ip ssh [client] source-interface
desc: The ip ssh client source-interface command specifies the source IP address for secure shell (SSH) connections.

title: ip tftp client source-interface
cmd:
ip tftp [client] source-interface INTERFACE [vrf vrf_name]
no ip tftp [client] source-interface
default ip tftp [client] source-interface
desc: The ip tftp client source-interface command specifies the source IP address for Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) connections.

title: platform arad lag mode
desc: The platform arad lag mode command allows configuration of LAGs with more than 16 members.

title: platform sand fabric mode (7500 and 7500E Series)
cmd:
platform sand fabric mode [MODE_SETTING]
no platform sand fabric mode
default platform sand fabric mode
desc: The platform sand fabric mode command specifies the fabric mode under which the switch operates after the next system reload.

title: platform sand forwarding mode (7500 and 7500E Series)
cmd:
platform sand forwarding mode [MODE_SETTING]
no platform sand forwarding mode
default platform sand forwarding mode
desc: The platform sand forwarding mode command specifies the forwarding compatibility mode under which the switch operates after the next system reload.

title: platform sand lag hardware-only
cmd:
platform sand lag hardware-only
no platform sand lag hardware-only
default platform sand lag hardware-only
desc: The platform sand lag hardware-only command specifies that all LAGs will use hardware resources including single member LAGs.

title: platform sand lag mode
desc: The platform sand lag mode configures two Link Aggregation Groups (LAG) partitions, Partition A and Partition B, to support twice as many available Port Channels.

title: pwd
desc: The pwd command displays the working directory.

title: schedule config
desc: The schedule config command sets configuration parameters to the CLI scheduler.

title: schedule
cmd:
schedule schedule_name PERIOD {max-log-files count | timeout timeout_interval}{command cmd | logging
verbose | loglocation flash:}
no schedule schedule_name
default schedule schedule_name
desc: The schedule command facilitates the periodic execution of a specified CLI command.

title: show (various configuration modes)
desc: The show command, when executed within a configuration mode, can display data in running-config for the active configuration mode.

title: show event-handler DropCountersHandler
desc: The show event-handler command displays details of the DropCountersHandler event handler.

title: show event-handler
desc: The show event-handler command displays the contents and activation history of a specified event handler or all event handlers.

title: show module
desc: The show module command displays information that identifies the supervisor, fabric, and linecard modules in a modular switch, including model number, serial number, hardware version number, software version

title: show platform sand compatibility
desc: The show sand platform compatibility command displays the fabric and forwarding modes.

title: show platform sand lag hardware-only
desc: The show platform sand lag hardware-only command displays whether or not LAGs are hardware-only.

title: show schedule
desc: The show schedule command displays logging output on the terminal during the current terminal session.

title: show version
desc: The show version command displays information that identifies the switch, including its model number, serial number, and system MAC address.

title: terminal length
cmd:
terminal length lines
no terminal length
default terminal length
desc: The terminal length command overrides automatic pagination and sets pagination length for all show commands on a terminal.

title: terminal monitor
cmd:
terminal monitor
no terminal monitor
default terminal monitor
desc: The terminal monitor command enables the display of logging output on the terminal during the current terminal session.

title: trigger
desc: The trigger command specifies what event will trigger the event handler.

title: Boot Loader Aboot
desc: Aboot is the boot loader for Arista switches.

title: boot-config
desc: The boot-config file is an ASCII file that Aboot uses to configure console communication settings, locate the EOS flash image, and specify initial network configuration settings.

title: boot-config File Structure
desc: Each line in the boot-config file specifies a configuration setting and has this format: NAME=VALUE • NAME is the parameter label.

title: boot-config Command Line Content
desc: Aboot configuration commands that boot-config files can contain include: • SWI specifies the location and file name of the EOS image file that Aboot loads when booting, using the same format as the boot command to designate a local or network path.

title: running-config
desc: The running-config is a virtual file that contains the system’s operating configuration, formatted as a command sequence.

title: startup-config
desc: The startup-config file is stored in flash memory and contains the configuration the switch loads when booting.

title: Redundancy Supervisor Protocols
desc: There are three available supervisor redundancy protocols.

title: System Reset
desc: When a reset condition exists, Aboot can either reset the switch without user intervention or facilitate a manual reset through the Aboot shell.

title: CONSOLESPEED
desc: The CONSOLESPEED command in the boot-config file specifies the console baud rate when the switch is booted.

title: NET Commands
desc: Aboot uses NET commands in the boot-config file during switch booting to configure the network interface used for switch configuration.

title: install bios source
cmd: install bios source SOURCE [active | standby] [reload | now]
desc: install bios source command is used to install an Aboot Update File (AUF) to upgrade Aboot firmware.

title: PASSWORD (ABOOT)
desc: PASSWORD specifies the Aboot password, as described in Accessing the Aboot Shell.

title: SWI
desc: SWI specifies the location and file name of the EOS image file that Aboot loads when booting, using the same format as boot-config to designate a local or network path.

title: boot console
desc: The boot console command configures terminal settings for serial devices connecting to the console port.

title: boot secret
cmd:
boot secret [ENCRYPT_TYPE] password
no boot secret
default boot secret
desc: The boot secret command creates or edits the Aboot shell password and stores the encrypted string in the PASSWORD command line of the boot-config file.

title: boot system
desc: The boot system command specifies the location of the EOS software image that Aboot loads when the switch boots.

title: delete startup-config
desc: The delete startup-config command erases or deletes the startup-config file.

title: protocol
desc: The protocol command configures how the supervisors on a modular switch will handle switchover events.

title: redundancy
desc: The redundancy command places the switch in redundancy configuration mode.

title: redundancy manual switchover
desc: The redundancy manual switchover command immediately switches control of the switch to the standby supervisor.

title: reload
desc: The reload command power cycles the switch and resets it under Aboot control.

title: reload (scheduled)
cmd:
reload cancel
no reload
default reload
desc: The reload (scheduled) command configures the switch to reset at a specified time or after a specified interval.

title: securebootctl
cmd:
securebootctl secureboot [-enable | -disable | -display]
securebootctl cert [[-add path] -delete | -display]
securebootctl aristacert [-disable | -enable | -display]
securebootctl abootpassword [-disable | -enable | -display]
desc: The securebootctl command in the Privileged EXEC Mode manages and displays the status of Secure Boot on Arista switches.

title: show platform bios
desc: The show platform bios [history | detail] command displays the BIOS versions on the switch.

title: show redundancy file-replication
desc: The show redundancy file-replication command displays the status and last synchronization date of file replication between the supervisors on the switch.

title: show redundancy status
desc: The show redundancy status command displays the current status (active or standby) and the configured redundancy protocol of both supervisors, and summary information about the latest switchover event.

title: show redundancy switchover sso
desc: The show redundancy switchover sso command displays the number of stateful switchovers since the last reload and a log of the events in the latest stateful switchover.

title: show reload
desc: The show reload command displays the time and reason for any pending reload operation.

title: show reload cause
desc: The show reload cause command displays the reason for the most recent reload operation.

title: show reload fast-boot
desc: The show reload fast-boot command verifies that the switch configuration is valid for Smart System Upgrade (SSU).

title: commit timer
desc: The commit timer command can automatically roll back changes performed during a configuration session if you haven't confirmed them within a preset time interval.

title: configure replace
desc: The configure replace command replaces the current configuration with a new configuration from the specified source.

title: configure session
cmd:
configure session [session_name] [abort | commit | [description description]]
no configure session session_name
default configure session
desc: The configure session command allows a series of temporary configuration changes and commits to running-config later by issuing the commit command.

title: idle-timeout (Console Management)
cmd:
idle-timeout idle_period
no idle-timeout
default idle-timeout
desc: The idle-timeout (Console Management) command configures the idle-timeout period for console connection sessions.

title: management accounts
desc: The management accounts command enters the Accounts Management Configuration Mode and allows the creation of user accounts on the switch.

title: management api eos-sdk-rpc
cmd:
management api eos-sdk-rpc
no management api eos-sdk-rpc
default management api eos-sdk-rpc
desc: The management api eos-sdk-rpc command places the switch in EOS SDK RPC API management configuration mode.

title: management api external-services
cmd:
management api external-services
no management api external-services
default management api external-services
desc: The management api external-services command places the switch in External Services API configuration mode.

title: management api gnmi
cmd:
management api gnmi
no management api gnmi
default management api gnmi
desc: The management api gnmi command places the switch in GNMI API Management configuration mode.

title: management api gnsi
cmd:
management api gnsi
no management api gnsi
default management api gnsi
desc: The management api gnsi command places the switch in GNSI API management configuration mode.

title: management api gribi
cmd:
management api gribi
no management api gribi
default management api gribi
desc: The management api gribi command places the switch in gRIBI API Management configuration mode.

title: management api http-commands
cmd:
management api http-commands
no management api http-commands
default management api http-commands
desc: The management api http-commands command places the switch in HTTP Commands API Management Configuration Mode.

title: management api models
cmd:
management api models
no management api models
default management api models
desc: The management api models command places the switch in Models API Management configuration mode.

title: management api netconf
cmd:
management api netconf
no management api netconf
default management api netconf
desc: The management api netconf command places the switch in Netconf API Management configuration mode.

title: management api restconf
cmd:
management api restconf
no management api restconf
default management api restconf
desc: The management api restconf command places the switch in Restconf API Management configuration mode.

title: management archive
desc: The management archive command enters the Archive Management Configuration Mode and allows you to manage log archives and archive space.

title: management cli
desc: The management cli command places the switch in CLI Management Configuration Mode and allows you to configure command line options.

title: management client
cmd: management client api gnmi [group group_name member server_name] [server
desc: The management client command enters the Client Management Configuration Mode and configures remote applications or scripts that utilize the switch powerful eAPI (EOS API) to programmatically send configuration and show commands.

title: management console
cmd:
management console
no management console
default management console
desc: The management console command places the switch in Console Management Configuration Mode to adjust the idle-timeout period for console connection sessions.

title: management data source
cmd:
management data source [disable] [sensor sensor_name [disabled | ip address
ip_address port port] [traffic-policies [disabled] [field-set [ipv4 prefix
desc: The management data source command enters the Data Source Management Configuration Mode and allows defining the rules and actions taken on specific data streams.

title: management defaults
cmd: management defaults secret hash [md5 | scrypt | sha-512 | yescrypt]
desc: The management defaults command enters the Defaults Management Configuration Mode and configures storing secrets on the switch.

title: management dmf
cmd:
managment dmf [disabled] [controller address ip_address1 controller address
ip_address2
desc: The management dmf command in the DANZ Monitoring Fabric (DMF) Configuration Mode allows the configuration of up to two DMF controllers on the network.

title: management file-systems
desc: The management file-systems command in the File Systems Management Configuration Mode allows the configuration and management of the file system on the switch.

desc: This section contains the following topics: • Temperature • Fans • Power

title: Power
desc: Arista switches contain power supplies that provide power to internal components.

title: Power Status
desc: The show environment power command displays the status of the power supplies.

title: System Status
desc: The show system environment all command lists the temperature, cooling, fan, and power supply information the individual show environment commands display, as described in Temperature Status, Fans, and Power.

title: Environment Commands
desc: Environment Control Configuration Commands • environment fan-speed • environment insufficient-fans action • environment overheat action • locator-led Environment Display Commands • show environment power

title: environment fan-speed
cmd:
environment fan-speed ACTION
no environment fan-speed
default environment fan-speed
desc: The environment fan-speed command determines how to control the speed of the switch fans.

title: environment insufficient-fans action
cmd:
environment insufficient-fans action REMEDY
no environment insufficient-fans action
default environment insufficient-fans action
desc: The environment insufficient-fans command controls the switch response to the insufficient fan condition.

title: environment overheat action
cmd:
environment overheat action REMEDY
no environment overheat action
default environment overheat action
desc: The environment overheat command controls the switch's response to an overheat condition.

title: locator-led
cmd:
locator-led {fantray tray_num | interface interface | module module_num | powersupply supply_num}
no locator-led {fantray tray_num | interface interface | module module_num | powersupply supply_num}
desc: When a component requires service, the locator-led command activates a locator to assist a technician in finding the component.

title: show environment power
desc: The show environment power command displays the status of all power supplies in the switch.

title: show environment temperature
desc: The show environment temperature command displays the operating temperature of all sensors on the switch.

title: show locator-led
desc: The show locator-led command displays the status of locator LEDs enabled on the switch.

title: show system environment cooling
desc: The show system environment cooling command displays fan status, air flow direction, and ambient temperature on the switch.

title: show system environment power budget
desc: The show system environment power budget command displays the configured power budget for PoE switches and a table to show the current consumption.

title: Reload
desc: After updating the boot-config file, reset the switch to activate the new image.

title: Install the New EOS Image
desc: After the EOS image has been copied to the primary supervisor's flash drive, use the install command to update the boot-config, copy the new image to the secondary supervisor, and reload both supervisors.

title: install
desc: The install command copies the specified EOS image onto the switch if the source is external, configures the boot-config file to point to the specified EOS image, copies the image to the standby supervisor on dualsupervisor switches, and optionally reloads the switch to run the n

title: reload fast-boot
cmd:
reload fast-boot
also recommends that an additional 240MB be available to store diagnostic information.
desc: Smart System Upgrade (SSU) allows critical switches to be upgraded with minimal downtime and packet loss by optimizing the reload procedure and leveraging protocols capable of graceful restart.

title: reload hitless
desc: The reload hitless command is a legacy command now identical to the reload fast-boot command.

title: AAA Configuration
desc: This section describes Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA), and contains these topics: • Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting Overview • Configuring the Security Services • Server Groups

title: RADIUS
desc: Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service (RADIUS) consists of a networking protocol that provides centralized AAA services for computers connecting to a network and then using network resources.

title: AAA with LDAP
desc: The switches support AAA with the LDAP protocol for authentication and authorization.

title: Default Roles
cmd: aaa authorization policy local default-role sysuser
desc: Users that are not explicitly assigned a role are assigned the default role.

title: Radius Servers
cmd: aaa authentication login default group radius
desc: A role can be assigned to a remote user authenticated through a RADIUS server.

title: AAA and X.509 Authentication
desc: Configure AAA to support X.

title: AAA Time-based Lockout
desc: AAA time-based lockout enables managing remote user unsuccessful login attempts for a specified period.

title: AAA Accounting for OpenConfig Remote Procedure Call (RPC) Messages Overview
cmd: AAA Accounting for OpenConfig Remote Procedure Call (RPC) Messages Overview
desc: OpenConfig allows network engineers to collaboratively develop programming interfaces and tools to manage networks dynamically and in a vendor-neutral manner.

title: AAA Commands
desc: Local Security File Commands • aaa root • enable password • show privilege • show users • show users accounts • username • username ssh-key Accounting, Authentication, and Authorization Commands • aaa accounting

title: aaa accounting dot1x
cmd:
aaa accounting dot1x default [METHOD_1][METHOD_2][METHOD_N]
no aaa accounting dot1x default
default aaa accounting dot1x default
desc: The aaa accounting dot1x command enables the accounting of requested 802.

title: aaa accounting system
cmd:
aaa accounting system default [METHOD_1][METHOD_2] ... [METHOD_N]
no aaa accounting system default
default aaa accounting system default
desc: The aaa accounting system command performs accounting for all system-level events.

title: aaa accounting
cmd:
aaa accounting TYPE CONNECTION MODE [METHOD_1][METHOD_2] ... [METHOD_N]
no aaa accounting TYPE CONNECTION
default aaa accounting TYPE CONNECTION
desc: The aaa accounting command configures accounting method lists for a specified authorization type.

title: aaa authentication dot1x
desc: The aaa authentication dot1x command configures the default authentication list of requested 802.

title: aaa authentication enable
cmd:
aaa authentication enable [console | default] METHOD_1 [METHOD_2] ... [METHOD_N]
no aaa authentication enable [console | default]
default aaa authentication enable [console | default]
desc: The aaa authentication enable command configures the service list that the switch references to authorize access to Privileged EXEC command mode.

title: aaa authentication login
cmd:
aaa authentication login CONNECTION SERVICE_1 [SERVICE_2] ... [SERVICE_N]
no aaa authentication login CONNECTION
default aaa authentication login CONNECTION
desc: The aaa authentication login command configures service lists the switch references to authenticate usernames.

title: aaa authentication policy local allow-nopassword-remote-login
cmd:
aaa authentication policy local allow-nopassword-remote-login
no aaa authentication policy local allow-nopassword-remote-login
default aaa authentication policy local allow-nopassword-remote-login
desc: The aaa authentication policy local allow-nopassword-remote-login command permits usernames without passwords to log in from any port.

title: aaa authentication policy lockout failure
cmd:
aaa authentication policy lockout failure failure_count duration duration_time {window
no aaa authentication policy lockout failure
default aaa authentication policy lockout failure
desc: The aaa authentication policy lockout failure command configures the switch to block the remote user from access after a specified number of unsuccessful login attempts within a lockout period.

title: aaa authentication policy log
cmd:
aaa authentication policy {on-failure | on-success} log
no aaa authentication policy {on-failure | on-success} log
default aaa authentication policy {on-failure | on-success} log
desc: The aaa authentication policy log command configures the switch to generate syslog messages for login authentication success or failure events.

title: aaa authorization commands
cmd:
aaa authorization commands PRIV default SERVICE_1[SERVICE_2] ... [SERVICE_N]
no aaa authorization commands PRIV default
default aaa authorization commands PRIV default
desc: The aaa authorization commands command configures the service list that authorizes CLI command access.

title: aaa authorization config-commands
cmd:
aaa authorization config-commands
no aaa authorization config-commands
default aaa authorization config-commands
desc: The aaa authorization config-commands command enables authorization of commands in any configuration mode, such as Global Configuration and all interface configuration modes.

title: aaa authorization exec
cmd:
aaa authorization exec default METHOD_1 [METHOD_2] ... [METHOD_N]
no aaa authorization exec default
default aaa authorization exec default
desc: The aaa authorization exec command configures the service list that the switch references to authorize access to open an EOS CLI shell.

title: aaa authorization policy local default-role
cmd:
aaa authorization policy local default-role role_name
no aaa authorization policy local default-role
default aaa authorization policy local default-role
desc: The aaa authorization policy local command specifies the name of the default role.

title: aaa authorization serial-console
cmd:
aaa authorization serial-console
no aaa authorization serial-console
default aaa authorization serial-console
desc: The aaa authorization serial-console command configures the switch to authorize commands entered through the console.

title: aaa group server radius
cmd:
aaa group server radius group_name
no aaa group server radius group_name
default aaa group server radius group_name
desc: The aaa group server radius command enters the Server-group-RADIUS Configuration Mode for the specified group name.

title: aaa group server tacacs+
cmd:
aaa group server tacacs+ group_name
no aaa group server tacacs+ group_name
default aaa group server tacacs+ group_name
desc: The aaa group server tacacs+ command enters Server-group-TACACS+ Configuration Mode for the specified group name.

title: aaa root
cmd:
aaa root SECURITY_LEVEL [ENCRYPT_TYPE] [password]
no aaa root
default aaa root
desc: The aaa root command specifies the password security level for the root account and can assign a password to the account.

title: auto-certificate protocol connection
cmd:
auto-certificate protocol est profile_name connection retry [count retries |
interval seconds
desc: The auto-certificate protocol connection command configures parameters for Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST) profiles used for Automatic Certificate Management.

title: auto-certificate protocol credentials
cmd: auto-certificate protocol est profile_name credentials [enroll | re-enroll]
desc: The auto-certificate protocol credentials command configures the enrollment and reenrollment usernames and pairs or JSON Web Tokens (JWTs).

title: auto-certificate protocol disabled
desc: The auto-certificate protocol disabled command temporarily pauses retrieving certificates from the server by disabling the profile and prevents sending unnecessary requests to the server.

title: auto-certificate protocol server
cmd: auto-certificate protocol est profile_name server [ssl ssl_profile] [url server_url]
desc: The auto-certificate protocol server command configures EST server behavior and options such as SSL profiles, server URLs, and VRFs.

title: auto-certificate profile
desc: The auto-certificate profile command allows configuration of Automatic Certificate Management and profiles for X.

title: auto-certificate profile parameters
cmd:
auto-certificate profile profile_name parameters [distinguished-name [commonname common_name] [country country_code] [email email_address ] [locality
auto-certificate profile profile_name parameters [subject-alternative-name [dns
desc: The auto-certificate profile parameters command configures the certificate attributes including distinguished name and subject alternative name.

title: clear aaa authentication lockout
desc: The clear aaa authentication lockout command clears the locked status of a user and allows access within a lockout period.

title: clear aaa counters radius
desc: The clear aaa counters radius command resets the counters tracking the statistics for the RADIUS servers that the switch accesses.

title: clear aaa counters tacacs+
desc: The clear aaa counters tacacs+ command resets the counters tracking the statistics for the TACACS+ servers that the switch accesses.

title: clear aaa counters
desc: The clear aaa counters command resets the counters tracking the number of service transactions performed by the switch since the last reset of the counters.

title: clear radius proxy counters client group
cmd: clear radius proxy client group group_name
desc: The clear radius proxy client group command clears RADIUS proxy client packet counters.

title: deny (Role)
cmd:
no deny [MODE_NAME] command command_name
default deny [MODE_NAME] command command_name
desc: The deny command adds a deny rule to the configuration mode role.

title: enable password
cmd:
enable password [ENCRYPT_TYPE] password
no enable password
default enable password
desc: The enable password command creates a new enable password or changes an existing password.

title: ip radius source-interface
cmd:
ip radius [vrf_inst] source-interface slot_number
no ip radius [vrf_inst] source-interface
default ip radius [vrf_inst] source-interface
desc: The ip radius source-interface command specifies the interface from which the IPv4 or IPv6 address is derived for use as the source for outbound RADIUS packets.

title: ip tacacs source-interface
cmd:
ip tacacs [VRF_INST] source-interface INT_NAME
no ip tacacs [VRF_INST] source-interface
default ip tacacs [VRF_INST] source-interface
desc: The ip tacacs source-interface command specifies the interface from which the IPv4 or IPv6 address is derived for use as the source for outbound TACACS+ packets.

title: no <sequence number> (Role)
desc: The no <sequence number> command removes the rule with the specified sequence number from the configuration-mode role.

title: monitor server radius
cmd:
monitor server radius
no monitor server radius
desc: The monitor server radius command enters the Monitor Server RADIUS Configuration Mode and allows the configuration of RADIUS server monitoring on the switch.

title: probe interval
cmd:
probe interval period seconds
no probe interval
desc: The probe interval command in the Monitor Server RADIUS Configuration Mode configures EOS to send probes to the RADIUS server at specific time intervals to ensure the availability of the server.

title: probe method
cmd:
probe method [[access-request username username password password ] | [statusserver]]
no probe method
desc: The probe method command in the Monitor Server RADIUS Configuration Mode determines the type of probe sent to the RADIUS server.

title: probe threshold
cmd:
probe threshold failure number
no probe threshold failure
desc: The probe threshold command in the Monitor Server RADIUS Configuration Mode configures sets the number of consecutive failed probes before determining a RADIUS server has become nonresponsive.

title: radius proxy
desc: The radius proxy command enters the RADIUS Proxy Configuration Mode on a switch.

title: radius proxy client group client
cmd:
radius proxy client group group_name client [host host_name] | [ipv4 ipv4_addr] | [ipv6
ipv6_addr]
desc: The radius proxy client group client command configures RADIUS Proxy client groups on the switch.

title: radius proxy client group server
cmd: radius proxy client group group_name server group_name
desc: The radius proxy client group server command configures RADIUS Proxy server groups on the switch.

title: radius proxy client key
desc: The radius proxy client key command enters the RADIUS Proxy Configuration Mode on a switch and allows the configuration of a global client key.

title: radius proxy client session
cmd: radius proxy client session idle-timeout seconds
desc: The radius proxy client session command enters the RADIUS proxy configuration mode on a switch and allows the configuration of a client session idle-timeout parameter.

title: radius proxy dynamic-authorization
desc: The radius proxy client key command enters the RADIUS proxy configuration mode on a switch and enables dynamic authorization for RADIUS proxy server groups.

title: radius-server deadtime
cmd:
radius-server deadtime dead_interval
no radius-server deadtime
default radius-server deadtime
desc: The radius-server deadtime command defines global deadtime period, when the switch ignores a non-responsive RADIUS server.

title: radius-server host
cmd:
radius-server host ADDR [VRF_INST][AUTH][ACCT][TIMEOUT][DEAD][RETRAN][ENCRYPT]
no radius-server host [ADDR][VRF_INST][AUTH][ACCT]
default radius-server host [ADDR][VRF_INST][AUTH][ACCT]
desc: The radius-server host command sets parameters for communicating with a specific RADIUS server.

title: radius-server key
cmd:
radius-server key [ENCRYPT_TYPE] encrypt_key
no radius-server key
default radius-server key
desc: The radius-server key command defines the global encryption key the switch uses when communicating with any RADIUS server for which a key is not defined.

title: radius-server retransmit
cmd:
radius-server retransmit count
no radius-server retransmit
default radius-server retransmit
desc: The radius-server retransmit command defines the global retransmit count, which specifies the number of times the switch attempts to access the RADIUS server after the first timeout expiry.

title: radius-server timeout
cmd:
radius-server timeout time_period
no radius-server timeout
default radius-server timeout
desc: The radius-server timeout command defines the global timeout the switch uses when communicating with any RADIUS server for which a timeout is not defined.

title: resequence (Role)
desc: The resequence command assigns sequence numbers to rules in the configuration mode role.

title: permit (Role)
cmd:
no permit [MODE_NAME] command ] command_name
default permit [MODE_NAME] command command_name
desc: The permit command adds a permit rule to the configuration mode role.

title: role

no role role_name
default role role_name
desc: The role command places the switch in Role Configuration Mode, which is a group-change mode that modifies a role.

no server LOCATION [VRF_INST][AUTH][ACCT]
default server LOCATION [VRF_INST][AUTH][ACCT]

no server LOCATION [VRF_INST][PORT]
default server LOCATION [VRF_INST][PORT]
desc: The server (server-group-TACACS+ configuration mode) command adds the specified TACACS+ server to the configuration-mode group.

title: service dot1x
desc: The service dot1x command in the Monitor Server RADIUS Configuration Mode applies monitoring globally to the RADIUS servers used for 802.

title: show aaa

desc: IP NAT Commands • clear ip nat flow translation • ip address • ip nat destination static • ip nat pool • ip nat source dynamic • ip nat source static • ip nat translation counters • ip nat translation low-mark

desc: The clear ip nat flow translation command clears all or the specified NAT table entries.

connections exceed 275, it can cause unpredictable behavior including restart of FocalPointV2 agent. The

title: Port Channels and LACP
desc: This chapter describes channel groups, port channels, port channel interfaces, and the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

title: Port Channel Introduction
desc: Arista’s switching platforms support industry-standard link aggregation protocols.

title: Channel Groups and Port Channels
desc: A port channel is a communication link between two switches supported by matching channel group interfaces on each switch.

title: Port Channel Subinterfaces
desc: Port channel subinterfaces divide a single port channel interface into multiple logical L3 interfaces based on the 802.

desc: EOS supports Port Channels as mirroring destinations for both ingress and egress source directions.

title: MLAG Introduction
desc: High availability data center topologies typically provide redundancy protection at the expense of oversubscription by connecting Top-Of-Rack (TOR) switches and servers to dual aggregation switches.

title: MLAG Conceptual Overview
desc: Multi-Chassis Link Aggregation

title: MLAG Operation Process
desc: A Multi-chassis Link Aggregation Group (MLAG) is a pair of links that terminate on two cooperating switches and appear as an ordinary Link Aggregation Group (LAG).

title: MLAG Interoperability with Other Features
desc: The following sections describe MLAG interaction with other switch features.

title: LACP
desc: Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) should be used on all MLAG interfaces, including the peer-link.

title: Static MAC Addresses
desc: A static MAC address configured on an MLAG interface is automatically configured on the peer’s corresponding interface.

title: Spanning Tree Protocol (STP)
desc: When implementing MLAG in a spanning tree network, spanning tree must be configured globally and on port-channels configured with an MLAG ID.

title: Port Mirroring
desc: A port channel which is a member of an MLAG must not be used as the destination port for a port mirroring (port monitoring) session.

title: IPv6 Flow Label Hashing
desc: Arista switches use the hashing algorithm to load-balance traffic among LAG members and Layer 3 ECMP (equal cost multipath) paths.

title: MLAG Maintenance
desc: These sections describe tasks required for MLAG to operate on the switch: • Ensuring Control Plane ACL Compatibility • MLAG Availability through a Single Functional Peer • Upgrading MLAG Peers • Forced Periodic ARP and NDP Refresh on IPv4 and IPv6 Ne…

title: MLAG Availability through a Single Functional Peer
cmd: MLAG Availability through a Single Functional Peer
desc: MLAG high availability advantages are fully realized when all devices that connect to one MLAG switch also connect to the peer switch.

title: Reload Delay
desc: If an MLAG peer reboots, all ports except those in the peer-link port-channel remain in errdisabled state for a specified time, called the reload-delay period.

title: MLAG Configuration Mode
desc: Peer connection parameters are configured in MLAG-configuration mode.

title: Peer Address
desc: The peer address is the destination address on the peer switch for MLAG control traffic.

title: Peer Link
desc: An MLAG is formed by connecting two switches through an interface called a peer link.

title: Reload Delay Period
desc: The reload delay period specifies the interval that non-peer links are disabled after an MLAG peer reboots.

title: Shutdown
desc: The shutdown (MLAG) command disables MLAG operations without disrupting the MLAG configuration.

title: EVPN - MLAG Single-homed Hosts
desc: Add a local VTEP to each MLAG peer for the control plane to advertise singly connected hosts directly behind a specific local VTEP-MLAG peer.

title: MLAG Implementation Example
desc: This example creates an MLAG Domain, then configures MLAG connections between the peer switches and four Network Attached Devices (NADs).

desc: The show spanning-tree mst command displays configuration and state information for Multiple Spanning Tree protocol (MST) instances.

title: clear lldp counters
desc: The clear lldp counters command resets the LLDP counters to zero.

title: clear lldp table
desc: The clear lldp table command clears neighbor information from the LLDP table.

title: lldp hold-time
cmd:
lldp hold-time period
no lldp hold-time
default lldp hold-time
desc: The lldp hold-time command specifies the amount of time a receiving device should maintain the information sent by the device before discarding it.

title: lldp management-address
cmd:
lldp management-address [INTERFACE]
no lldp management-address [INTERFACE]
default lldp management-address [INTERFACE]
desc: The lldp management-address command enables the user to add the IP management address used for LLDP Type-Length-Value (TLV).

title: lldp management-address vrf
cmd:
lldp management-address vrf VRF_INSTANCE
no lldp management-address vrf VRF_INSTANCE
default lldp management-address vrf VRF_INSTANCE
