title: lldp receive
cmd:
lldp receive
no lldp receive
default lldp receive
desc: The lldp receive command enables LLDP packets on an interface.

title: lldp receive packet tagged drop
desc: The lldp receive packet tagged drop command is a global configuration command and when configured, the LLDP ignores all the packets with VLAN-tag.

title: lldp run
cmd:
lldp run
no lldp run
default lldp run
desc: The lldp run command enables LLDP on the switch.

title: lldp timer reinitialization
cmd:
lldp timer reinitialization delay
no lldp timer reinitialization
default lldp timer reinitialization
desc: The lldp timer reinitialization command sets the time delay in seconds for LLDP to initialize.

title: lldp timer
cmd:
lldp timer transmission_time
no lldp timer
default lldp timer
desc: The lldp timer command specifies the amount of time a receiving device should maintain the information sent by the device before discarding it.

title: lldp tlv transmit
cmd:
lldp tlv transmit TLV_NAME
no lldp tlv transmit TLV_NAM
default lldp tlv transmit TLV_NAME
desc: The lldp tlv transmit command allows the user to specify the Type-Length-Values (TLVs) to include in LLDP packets.

title: lldp transmit
cmd:
lldp transmit
no lldp transmit
default lldp transmit
desc: The lldp transmit command enables the transit of LLDP packets on an interface.

title: poe negotiation lldp disabled
cmd:
poe negotiation lldp disabled
no poe negotiation lldp disabled
default poe negotiation lldp disabled
desc: Power Via MDI TLVs are included by default in LLDP packets sent to Power over Ethernet (PoE) Powered Devices (PDs) to allow dynamic negotiation of power levels.

title: show lldp counters
desc: The show lldp counters command displays LLDP traffic information for the switch.

title: show lldp local-info
desc: The show lldp local-info command displays LLDP errors and overflows.

title: show lldp neighbors
desc: The show lldp neighbors command displays information about the switch’s LLDP neighbors.

title: show lldp
desc: The show lldp command displays LLDP information.

title: VLAN Introduction
desc: Arista switches support industry standard 802.

title: VLAN Conceptual Overview
desc: Virtual LANs (VLANs)

title: VLAN Definition
desc: A Virtual Local Area Network (VLAN) allows a group of devices to communicate as if they were in the same network regardless of their physical location.

title: VLAN Switching
desc: Ethernet and port channel interfaces are configured as switched ports by default.

title: VLAN Trunking and Trunk Groups
desc: Trunking extends multiple VLANs beyond the switch through a common interface or port channel.

title: VLAN Routing
desc: Each VLAN can be associated with a Switch Virtual Interface (SVI), also called a VLAN interface.

title: VLAN Interfaces
desc: A Switched Virtual Interface (SVI) connects to the VLAN segment on the switch to provide Layer 3 processing for packets from the VLAN.

title: VLAN Translation
desc: VLAN translation allows you to map packets from one VLAN to another.

title: VLAN Configuration Procedures
desc: These sections describe basic VLAN configuration tasks.

title: VLAN Policy
desc: The VLAN policy configuration command enables a switch to configure a VLAN policy when it receives a packet with unknown destination MAC address on a VLAN.

title: Port VLAN Scaling on DCS-7160
desc: Port VLAN scaling allows the user to configure a subset of ports in the scale mode.

title: autostate
desc: When autostate is enabled, the VLAN interface will be up when: • the corresponding VLAN exists and is in the active state.

title: encapsulation dot1q vlan
cmd:
encapsulation dot1q vlan vlan_id
no encapsulation dot1q vlan
default encapsulation dot1q vlan
desc: Routed Port VLAN Translation In the configuration mode for an Ethernet or port channel interface, the encapsulation dot1q vlan translates packets with a dot1q header to the internal VLAN for a routed port.

title: interface vlan
cmd:
interface vlan v_range
no interface vlan v_range
default interface vlan v_range
desc: The interface vlan command places the switch in VLAN-interface configuration mode for modifying parameters of the Switch Virtual Interface (SVI).

title: l2-protocol encapsulation dot1q vlan
cmd:
l2-protocol encapsulation dot1q vlan vlan_id
no l2-protocol encapsulation dot1q vlan
default l2-protocol encapsulation dot1q vlan
desc: The l2-protocol encapsulation dot1q vlan command enables Layer 2 802.

title: mac address forwarding
cmd:
mac address forwarding [unicast | multicast] miss action [drop | flood | log]
no mac address forwarding [unicast | multicast] miss action [drop | flood | log]
default mac address forwarding [unicast | multicast] miss action [drop | flood | log]
desc: The mac address forwarding command enables a switch to configure a VLAN policy when it receives a packet with an unknown destination MAC address on a VLAN.

title: pvlan mapping
desc: The pvlan mapping command maps a Switch Virtual Interface (SVI) available in the primary VLAN to the secondary VLAN or VLANs in the VLAN configuration mode.

title: show dot1q-tunnel
desc: The show dot1q-tunnel command displays the ports that are configured in dot1q-tunnel switching mode.

title: show interfaces switchport backup-link
cmd:
show interfaces [INTERFACE] switchport backup-link
show interfaces switchport backup-link [module {Fabric f_num | Linecard lc_num | Supervisor
svr_num | Switchcard | 1-2 | 3-6 }]
desc: The show interfaces switchport backup-link command displays interfaces that are configured as switchport backup pairs and the operational status of each interface.

title: show interfaces switchport vlan mapping
desc: The show interfaces switchport vlan mapping command displays mapping information of the configured VLANs in an interface mode.

title: show interfaces switchport
desc: The show interfaces switchport command displays the switching configuration and operational status of the specified ports.

title: show interfaces trunk
desc: The show interfaces trunk command displays configuration and status information for interfaces configured in switchport trunk mode.

title: show interfaces vlans
desc: The show interfaces vlans command displays a table that lists the VLANs that are carried by the specified interfaces.

title: show pvlan mapping interfaces
desc: The show pvlan mapping interfaces command displays information about the private VLAN mapping interfaces.

title: show vlan
desc: The show vlan command displays the VLAN ID, name, status, and member ports of all configured VLANs.

title: show vlan brief count
desc: The show vlan brief count command displays the number of VLANs that are configured on the switch.

title: show vlan counters
desc: Displays information about configured ingress and egress VLAN counters on the switch.

title: show vlan dynamic
desc: The show vlan dynamic command displays the source and quantity of dynamic VLANs on the switch.

title: show vlan internal allocation policy
desc: The show vlan internal allocation policy command displays the method the switch uses to allocate VLANs to routed ports.

title: show vlan internal usage
desc: The show vlan internal usage command shows the VLANs that are allocated as internal VLANs for routed ports.

title: show vlan trunk group
desc: The show vlan trunk group command displays the trunk group membership of the specified VLANs.

title: state
cmd:
state OPERATION_STATE
no state
default state
desc: The state command configures the VLAN transmission state of the configuration mode VLAN.

title: switchport access vlan
cmd:
switchport access vlan v_num
no switchport access vlan
default switchport access vlan
desc: The switchport access vlan command specifies the access VLAN of the configuration mode interface.

title: switchport dot1q ethertype
cmd:
switchport dot1q ethertype ethertype
no switchport dot1q ethertype
default switchport dot1q ethertype
desc: The switchport dot1q ethertype command configures the tag protocol identifier (TPID, also known as a dot1q ethertype), of the configuration mode interface.

title: switchport mode
cmd:
switchport mode MODE_TYPE priority tag transmit
no switchport mode
default switchport mode
desc: The switchport mode command specifies the switching mode of the configuration mode interface.

title: switchport trunk allowed vlan
cmd:
switchport trunk allowed vlan EDIT_ACTION
no switchport trunk allowed vlan
default switchport trunk allowed vlan
desc: The switchport trunk allowed vlan command creates or modifies the list of VLANs for which the configuration mode interface, in trunk mode, handles tagged traffic.

title: switchport trunk group
cmd:
switchport trunk group [ group_name ]
no switchport trunk group [ group_name ]
default switchport trunk group [ group_name ]
desc: The switchport trunk group command assigns the configuration mode interface to the specified trunk group.

title: switchport trunk native vlan
cmd:
switchport trunk native vlan VLAN_ID
no switchport trunk native vlan
default switchport trunk native vlan
desc: The switchport trunk native vlan command specifies the trunk mode native VLAN for the configuration mode interface.

title: switchport vlan forwarding
desc: The switchport vlan forwarding command forwards packets between the ports belonging to VLAN in the interface configuration mode.

title: switchport vlan translation
cmd:
switchport vlan translation [DIRECTION] incoming_vlanid new_vlanid
no switchport vlan translation incoming_vlanid new_vlanid
no switchport vlan translation DIRECTION incoming_vlanid
default switchport vlan translation incoming_vlanid new_vlanid
default switchport vlan translation DIRECTION incoming_vlanid
desc: The switchport vlan translation command allows you to map packets from one VLAN to another using VLAN translation.

title: switchport vlan translation required
desc: On routed ports, the switchport vlan translation required command (permitted only on routed ports) configures the VLAN on the interface to act as the native VLAN.

title: trunk group
cmd:
trunk group [name]
no trunk group [name]
default trunk group [name]
desc: The trunk group command assigns the configuration mode VLAN to a specified trunk group.

title: vlan
cmd:
vlan vlan_range
no vlan vlan_range
default vlan vlan_range
desc: The vlan command places the switch in VLAN configuration mode to configure a set of virtual LANs.

title: vlan internal order
cmd:
vlan internal order DIRECTION [RANGE_VLAN]
no vlan internal order
default vlan internal order
desc: The vlan internal order command specifies the range that the switch can allocate as internal VLANs when configuring routed ports and the order of their allocation.

title: Set the Priority Rank to the Traffic Class
desc: The dcbx application priority command assigns a priority rank to the specified traffic class in the application priority table.

title: Set the Priority Flow Control Priority
desc: The priority-flow-control priority command in DCBX mode creates a priority group that pauses priority.

title: which means the PFC watchdog is active.
desc: switch# show priority-flow-control The hardware supports PFC on priorities 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 The PFC watchdog default timeout is 3.

title: dcbx application priority
cmd:
dcbx application [ APPLICATION_TYPE priority ] rank
no dcbx application [ APPLICATION_TYPE priority ]
default dcbx application [ APPLICATION_TYPE priority ]
desc: The dcbx application priority command assigns a priority rank to the specified traffic class in the application priority table.

title: dcbx ets
cmd: dcbx ets [ qos map cos value traffic-class value | traffic-class value bandwidth value ]
desc: The dcbx ets command configures the enhanced transmission selection (ETS) to the specified QoS group, and sets the traffic class priority and the bandwidth percentage for the packets in the traffic class.

title: dcbx mode
cmd:
dcbx mode MODE_NAME
no dcbx mode
default dcbx mode
desc: The dcbx mode command enables DCBX mode on the configuration mode interface.

title: no priority-flow-control
desc: The no priority-flow-control and default priority-flow-control commands disable the priority flow control (PFC) on the configuration mode interface and restore the default packet drop setting on the interface, which takes effect when PFC is re-enabled.

title: priority-flow-control
cmd:
priority-flow-control on
no priority-flow-control on
default priority-flow-control on
desc: The priority-flow-control command enables Priority Flow Control (PFC) on the configuration mode interface to pause selected traffic classes.

title: platform fm6000 pfc-wm
cmd:
platform fm6000 pfc-wm [ RX-PRIVATE_SIZE ][ PFC-ON_WM ][ PFC-OFF_WM ]
no platform fm6000 pfc-wm
default platform fm6000 pfc-wm
desc: The platform fm6000 pfc-wm command configures the hardware buffer space allocated to the Priority Flow Control (PFC) RX-Private buffer.

title: priority-flow-control pause watchdog action
cmd:
priority-flow-control pause watchdog action
no priority-flow-control pause watchdog action
desc: The priority-flow-control pause watchdog action command either drops the traffic on a stuck queue, or error disables the port which has a stuck queue, or notifies if there is no action on the stuck queue.

title: priority-flow-control pause watchdog default
cmd:
priority-flow-control pause watchdog default
no priority-flow-control pause watchdog default
desc: The priority-flow-control pause watchdog default command monitors all the egress queues which have guaranteed bandwidth enabled and for the priorities on which PFC is enabled.

title: priority-flow-control pause watchdog hardware
cmd:
priority-flow-control pause watchdog hardware
no priority-flow-control pause watchdog hardware
desc: The priority-flow-control pause watchdog hardware command configures specific PFC priorities as non-disruptive.

title: priority-flow-control priority
cmd:
priority-flow-control priority pack-drop
no priority-flow-control priority
default priority-flow-control priority
desc: The priority-flow-control priority command configures the packet resolution setting on the configuration mode interface.

title: show dcbx
desc: The show dcbx command list DCBX status and the interfaces on which DCBX is enabled.

title: show dcbx application-priority-configuration
desc: The show dcbx application-priority-configuration command displays the DCBX peer application priority configuration.

title: show dcbx priority-flow-control-configuration
desc: The show dcbx priority-flow-control-configuration command displays the IEEE DCBX peer priority flow control configurations.

title: show dcbx status
desc: The show dcbx status command displays the DCBX status on the interfaces on which DCBX is enabled.

title: show interfaces priority-flow-control
desc: The show interfaces priority-flow-control command displays the status of PFC on all interfaces.

title: show platform fm6000 pfc-wm
desc: The show platform fm6000 pfc-wm command displays the buffer space allocated to the RX-Private buffer and buffer levels that trigger PFC frame transmission activities.

title: show priority-flow-control
desc: The show priority-flow-control command displays the status and other PFC and PFC watchdog information on all interfaces if no specific interface is specified.

title: IP Address Locking
desc: EOS provides IP Address Locking capabilities when configured on an Ethernet Layer 2 port.

title: IP Address Locking Configuration
desc: This section describes IP Locking configuration tasks.

title: IP Address Locking Commands
desc: IP Address Locking Configuration Commands • address locking deny • address locking dhcp • address-locking disable • address locking lease query • address locking local-interface IP Address Locking Clear Commands

title: address locking deny
desc: Use the address locking command to block IPv4 and ARP packets with specific IPv4 addresses on the switch.

title: address locking dhcp
cmd: address locking dhcp server ipv4 ip_address
desc: Use the address locking command to enter address locking mode and then dhcp to configure the DHCP server.

title: address-locking disable
desc: The address-locking disable command disables IP Address Locking on a switch.

title: address locking lease
desc: Use the address locking command to enter the IPv4 and IPv6 locking mode and then on the switch, use the lease to configure DHCP leases.

title: address locking lease query
desc: Use the address locking command to enter the IPv4 and IPv6 locking mode and then use the lease query retry interval to configure DHCP leases.

title: address locking local-interface
cmd: address locking local-interface [Ethernet | Loopback | Management | Port Channel | Tunnel | Vlan]
desc: Use the address locking local-interface command to configure a local interface for IPv4 and IPv6 locking IP addresses on the switch.

title: clear address locking lease
desc: Use the clear address locking lease command to remove lease bindings at different granularities.

title: locked-address expiration mac disabled
cmd:
locked-address expiration mac disabled
no locked-address expiration mac disabled
default locked-address expiration mac disabled
desc: IP Address Locking, by default, removes authorization from leases after the corresponding MAC addresses age out.

title: locked-address ipv4 enforcement disabled
cmd:
locked-address ipv4 enforcement disabled
no locked-address ipv4 enforcement disabled
default locked-address ipv4 enforcement disabled
desc: The locked-address ipv4 enforcement disabled command disables address filtering for all ports with IPv4 Locking enabled.

title: locked-address ipv6 enforcement disabled
cmd:
locked-address IPv6 enforcement disabled
no locked-address IPv6 enforcement disabled
default locked-address IPv6 enforcement disabled
desc: The locked-address ipv6 enforcement disabled command disables address filtering for all ports with IPv6 Locking enabled.

title: show address locking
desc: Use the show address locking command to display the status of IP and IPv6 locking.

title: show address locking counters
desc: The show address locking counters command displays DHCP lease query messages sent, received, and dropped.

title: any
desc: Ethernet49/1 IP DROP

title: c4:01:32:58:00:00
desc: Ethernet49/1 IP PERMIT

title: show address locking table ipv4
desc: Use the show address locking table ipv4 command to display all DHCP leases with IP Locking, and the interfaces with authorized the IP addresses.

title: switch# show interface et1.1-2 status
cmd: mac address-table static 0000.000a.000a vlan 200 interface et1.1
desc: Port Name Status Vlan Duplex Speed Et1.

title: ARP Inspection
desc: The Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inspection command ip arp inspection vlan activates a security feature that protects the network from ARP spoofing.

title: show arp agent
cmd: show arp agent [ipv4 | ipv6] [cache | dynamic | capacity | [interface interface]
desc: The show arp agent command displays the aggregate of all ARP entries that the CLI and other switch agents requested the ARP agent to install in EOS.

title: show ip arp inspection statistics
cmd: show ip arp inspection statistics [vlan [vid]|[interface] interface intf_slot | intf_port]
desc: The show ip arp inspection statistics command displays the statistics of inspected ARP packets.

title: show ip arp inspection vlan
desc: The show ip arp inspection vlan command displays the configuration and operation state of ARP inspection.

title: show ip dhcp relay counters
desc: The show ip dhcp relay counters command displays the number of DHCP packets received, forwarded, or dropped on the switch and on all interfaces enabled as DHCP relay agents.

title: show ip dhcp relay
desc: The show ip dhcp relay command displays the DHCP relay agent configuration status on the switch.

title: show ip dhcp snooping counters
desc: The show ip dhcp snooping counters command displays counters that track the quantity of DHCP request and reply packets received by the switch.

title: show ip dhcp snooping hardware
desc: The show ip dhcp snooping hardware command displays internal hardware DHCP snooping status on the switch.

title: show ip dhcp snooping
desc: The show ip dhcp snooping command displays the DHCP snooping configuration.

title: show ip interface
desc: The show ip interface command displays the status of specified interfaces that are configured as routed ports.

title: show ip interface brief
desc: Use the show ip interface brief command output to display the status summary of the specified interfaces that are configured as routed ports.

title: show ip route
desc: The show ip route command displays routing table entries that are in the Forwarding Information Base (FIB), including static routes, routes to directly connected networks, and dynamically learned routes.

title: show ip route age
desc: The show ip route age command displays the time when the route for the specified network was present in the routing table.

title: show ip route gateway
desc: The show ip route gateway command displays IP addresses of all gateways (next hops) used by active routes.

title: Static and Default IPv4 Routes
desc: Static routes are entered through the CLI and are typically used when dynamic protocols cannot establish routes to a specified destination prefix.

title: Dynamic IPv4 Routes
desc: Dynamic routing protocols establish dynamic routes.

title: BGP Functions Supported for Arista Switches
cmd: BGP Functions Supported for Arista Switches
desc: Arista switches support these BGP functions: • A single BGP instance • Simultaneous internal (IBGP) and external (EBGP) peering • Multiprotocol BGP • BGP Confederations

title: Route Management
desc: When enabling routing, the switch discovers the best route to a packet destination address by exchanging routing information with other devices.

title: Route Redistribution
desc: Route redistribution advertises connected (static) routes or routes established by other routing protocols into a dynamic routing protocol routing domain.

title: Show Commands
desc: • The show ip hardware fib summary command displays the statistics of this RECMP deduping:

title: uRPF Operation
desc: Configure uRPF on interfaces.

title: uRPF Configuration
desc: Enable Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) for IPv4 packets ingressing the configuration mode interface using the ip verify command.

title: Default VRF
desc: EOS creates the default VRF automatically and you cannot renamed or configure it.

title: rd (VRF configuration mode)
desc: The rd command issued in VRF Configuration Mode is a legacy command supported for backward compatibility.

title: Show Commands
cmd: show platform arad ip route summary
desc: Display the IPv4 route scale summary using the show platform arad ip route summary command in the Global Configuration Mode.

title: IP Source Guard
desc: IP Source Guard (IPSG) prevents IP spoofing attacks.

title: Nexthop Group Commands
desc: Nexthop Commands • entry (Next-hop Group) • ip route nexthop-group • nexthop-group • size (Nexthop Group) • ttl (Next-hop Group) • tunnel-source (Next-hop Group) Nexthop Show Command • show nexthop-group

title: entry (Next-hop Group)
cmd:
entry index tunnel-destination ipv4_address
no entry index
default entry index
desc: The entry command defines a next-hop entry in the nexthop group configuration mode .

title: ip route nexthop-group
cmd:
ip route [VRF_INST dest_net nexthop-group nhgp_name [dist][TAG_OPTION][RT_NAME]
no ip route [VRF_INST] dest_net [nexthop-group nhgroup_name][distance]
default ip route [VRF_INST] dest_net [nexthop-group nhgroup_name][distance]
desc: The ip route nexthop-group command creates a static route.

title: nexthop-group
cmd:
nexthop-group group_name type TUNNEL_TYPE
no nexthop-group [group_name][type TUNNEL_TYPE]
default nexthop-group [group_name][typeTUNNEL_TYPE]
desc: The nexthop-group command places the switch in nexthop-group configuration mode, through which next-hop groups are created or modified.

title: show nexthop-group

title: size (Nexthop Group)
cmd:
size entry_size
no size entry_size
default size entry_size

title: ttl (Next-hop Group)

ttl hop_expiry
no ttl hop_expiry
default ttl hop_expiry
desc: The ttl command specifies the number entered into the TTL (time to live) encapsulation field of packets transmitted to the address designated by the configuration mode next-hop group.

title: tunnel-source (Next-hop Group)
cmd:
tunnel-source SOURCE
no tunnel-source SOURCE
default tunnel-source SOURCE
desc: The tunnel-source command specifies the address that is entered into the source IP address encapsulation field of packets that are transmitted as designated by the nexthop group configuration mode .

title: tunnel nexthop-group unresolved
cmd:
tunnel nexthop-group unresolved invalid
no tunnel nexthop-group unresolved invalid
default tunnel nexthop-group unresolved invalid
desc: The tunnel nexthop-group unresolved command in the Router General Configuration Mode installs a nexthop-group tunnel only if a viable nexthop group exists.

title: sub-option 2 type string data “vendor"
desc: Configuring DHCP Server Subnets DHCP Server settings can also be configured per subnet and overrides the DHCP Server global mode configurations.

title: agent SandL3Unicast terminate
desc: The agent SandL3Unicast terminate command restarts the platform Layer 3 agent to ensure optimized IPv4 routes.

title: arp
cmd:
arp [vrf_instance] ipv4_addr mac_addr arpa
no arp [vrf_instance] ipv4_addr
default arp [vrf_instance] ipv4_addr
desc: The arp command adds a static entry to an Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) cache.

title: arp aging timeout
cmd:
arp aging timeout arp_time
no arp aging timeout
default arp aging timeout
desc: The arp aging timeout command specifies the duration of dynamic address entries in the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) cache for addresses learned through the configuration mode interface.

title: arp cache dynamic capacity
cmd:
ipv6 nd cache dynamic capacity capacity
no arp cache dynamic capacity
default arp cache dynamic capacity
desc: AARP and IPv6 Neighbor Discovery store neighbor address resolutions in a neighbor cache.

title: arp cache persistent
cmd:
arp cache persistent
no arp cache persistent
default arp cache persistent
desc: The arp cache persistent command restores the dynamic entries in the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) cache after reboot.

title: arp gratuitous accept
cmd:
arp gratuitous accept
no arp gratuitous accept
default arp gratuitous accept
desc: The arp gratuitous accept command configures the configuration mode interface to accept gratuitous ARP request packets received on that interface.

title: arp proxy max-delay
desc: The arp proxy max-delay command enables delaying proxy ARP requests on the configuration mode interface.

title: balance factor
cmd:
balance factor factor_value
no balance factor factor_value
desc: The balance factor command in the Port Group Host Configuration Mode configures port balancing for Cluster Load Balancing on the network.

title: clear arp inspection statistics
desc: The clear arp inspection statistics command clears ARP inspection statistics.

title: clear arp
desc: The clear arp command removes the specified dynamic ARP entry for the specified IP address from the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) table.

title: clear arp-cache
desc: The clear arp-cache command refreshes dynamic entries in the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) cache.

title: clear ip dhcp relay counters
desc: The clear ip dhcp relay counters command resets the DHCP relay counters.

title: clear ip dhcp snooping counters
desc: The clear ip dhcp snooping counters command resets the DHCP snooping packet counters.

title: clear ip multicast count
desc: The clear ip multicast count command clears all counters associated with the multicast traffic.

title: clear snapshot counters ecmp
desc: The clear shapshot counters ecmp deletes previous snapshots.

title: cli vrf
desc: The cli vrf command specifies the context-active VRF.

title: compress

ip hardware fib optimize prefix-length prefix-length expand prefix-length compress
no ip hardware fib optimize prefix-length prefix-length expand prefix-length compress
desc: The compress command increases the hardware resources available for the specified prefix lengths.

no destination groupings [bgp field-set] [prefix length length] [vtep]
desc: The destination grouping command in the Cluster Load Balancing Configuration mode allows the configuration of destination grouping settings with BGP, prefix, or VTEP groupings for cluster load balancing.

title: dhcp relay

title: dhcp server
desc: The dhcp server command places the switch in the DHCP relay mode.

title: dhcp server client
cmd: dhcp server client class [ipv4|ipv6] definition client_class assignments [defaultgateway|dns|lease|option|private-option|tftp]
desc: The dhcp server client command configures client options for the DHCP server.

desc: The dhcp server debug log command configures DHCP server debugging configuration.

title: dhcp server subnet
desc: The dhcp server subnet command configures DHCP server subnet options.

desc: The dhcp server subnet [ipv4 | ipv6] client command configures client options for the DHCP server.

title: dhcp server tftp
cmd: dhcp server tftp server [file [ipv4|ipv6] file_name] [option [150|66]] ipv4

title: dhcp server vendor-option

desc: The dhcp server vendor-option command configures the DHCP server vendor identifier options.

title: dhcp server vendor-option ipv4 sub-option
desc: The dhcp server vendor-option command configures the DHCP server vendor identifier options.

title: fib route limit
desc: The fib route limit command in the Router General Configuration Mode limits the number of routes added to the Forwarding Information Database (FIB) and also suppresses BGP routes when exceeding the table limit.

title: flow
cmd:
flow [counters] [match encapsulation [none | vxlan] ipv4] [monitor]
no flow [counters] [match encapsulation [none | vxlan] ipv4] [monitor]
desc: The flow command in the Cluster Load Balancing Configuration Mode allows the configuration of flow settings for Cluster Load Balancing including counters, matching, monitoring, sources, and warnings.

title: flow exhaustion
cmd:
flow exhaustion action [dscp dscp_value] [traffic-class class_value
no flow exhaustion action [dscp dscp_value] [traffic-class class_value
desc: The flow exhaustion command in the Port Group Host Configuration Mode configures flow-related settings for cluster load balancing on the switch.

title: flow source learning
cmd:
flow source learning
no flow source learning
desc: The flow source learning command enters the Flow Source Learning Configuration Mode and configures cluster load balancing to learn flow sources.

title: flow limit
cmd:
flow limit max_flows learning max_flows
no flow limit max_flows learning max_flows
desc: The flow limit command in the Port Group Host Configuration Mode configures flow-related settings for cluster load balancing on the switch.

title: flow warning
cmd:
flow warning threshold_flows
no flow warning threshold_flows
desc: The flow warning command in the Port Group Host Configuration Mode configures flow warning thresholds for cluster load balancing on the switch.

title: forwarding type
cmd:
forwarding type routed
no forwarding type routed
desc: The forwarding type command configures the encapsulation and mode used to deliver packets between TORs over the uplinks.

title: interface tunnel
cmd:
interface tunnel number
no interface tunnel number
desc: The interface tunnel command places the switch in Interface-Tunnel Configuration Mode.

title: ip arp inspection limit
cmd:
ip arp inspection limit [ rate pps] [burst_interval sec | none]
no ip arp inspection limit [ rate pps] [burst_interval sec | none]
default ip arp inspection limit [ rate pps] [burst_interval sec | none]
desc: The ip arp inspection limit command disables the interface if the incoming ARP rate exceeds the configured value rate limit of the incoming ARP packets on an interface.

title: ip arp inspection logging
cmd:
ip arp inspection logging [rate pps ][burst_interval sec | none]
no ip arp inspection logging [RATE pps ][burst_interval sec | none]
default ip arp inspection logging [RATE pps ][burst_interval sec | none]
desc: The ip arp inspection logging command enables logging of incoming ARP packets on the interface if the rate exceeds the configured value.

title: ip arp inspection trust
cmd:
ip arp inspection trust
no ip arp inspection trust
default ip arp inspection trust
desc: The ip arp inspection trust command configures the trust state of an interface.

title: ip arp inspection vlan
desc: The ip arp inspection vlan command enables ARP inspection.

title: ip dhcp relay all-subnets
cmd:
ip dhcp relay all-subnets
no ip dhcp relay all-subnets
default ip dhcp relay all-subnets
desc: The ip dhcp relay all-subnets command configures the DHCP smart relay status in the Interface Configuration Mode.

title: ip dhcp relay all-subnets default
cmd:
ip dhcp relay all-subnets default
no ip dhcp relay all-subnets default
default ip dhcp relay all-subnets default
desc: The ip dhcp relay all-subnets default command configures the global DHCP smart relay setting.

title: ip dhcp relay always-on
cmd:
ip dhcp relay always-on
no ip dhcp relay always-on
default ip dhcp relay always-on
desc: The ip dhcp relay always-on command enables the DHCP relay agent on the switch regardless of the DHCP relay agent status on any interface.

title: ip dhcp relay information option (Global)
cmd:
ip dhcp relay information option
no ip dhcp relay information option
default ip dhcp relay information option
desc: The ip dhcp relay information option command configures the switch to attach tags to DHCP requests before forwarding them to the DHCP servers designated by the ip helper-address commands.

title: ip dhcp relay information option circuit-id
cmd:
ip dhcp relay information option circuit-id id_label
no ip dhcp relay information option circuit-id
default ip dhcp relay information option circuit-id
desc: The ip dhcp relay information option circuit-id command specifies the content of tags that the switch attaches to DHCP requests before forwarding them from the configuration mode interface to DHCP server addresses specified by ip helper-address commands.

title: ip dhcp snooping
cmd:
ip dhcp snooping
no ip dhcp snooping
default ip dhcp snooping
desc: The ip dhcp snooping command enables DHCP snooping globally on the switch.

title: ip dhcp snooping bridging
cmd:
ip dhcp snooping bridging
no ip dhcp snooping bridging
desc: The ip dhcp snooping bridging command enables the DHCP snooping bridging configuration.

title: ip dhcp snooping information option
cmd:
ip dhcp snooping information option
no ip dhcp snooping information option
default ip dhcp snooping information option
desc: The ip dhcp snooping information option command enables the insertion of option-82 DHCP snooping information in DHCP packets on VLANs where you have DHCP snooping enabled.

title: ip dhcp snooping vlan
cmd:
ip dhcp snooping vlan v_range
no ip dhcp snooping vlan v_range
default ip dhcp snooping vlan v_range
desc: The ip dhcp snooping vlan command enables DHCP snooping on specified VLANs.

title: ip hardware fib ecmp resilience
cmd:
ip hardware fib ecmp resilience net_addr capacity nhop_max redundancy duplicates
no ip hardware fib ecmp resilience net_addr
default ip hardware fib ecmp resilience net_addr
desc: The ip hardware fib ecmp resilience command enables resilient ECMP for the specified IP address prefix and configures a fixed number of next hop entries in the hardware ECMP table for that prefix.

title: ip hardware fib load-balance distribution
cmd:
ip hardware fib load-balance distribution [dynamic | hash] average-trafficweight average_traffic_weight_value flow-set-size flow_set_size_value inactivity
inactivity_value sampling-period sampling_period seed hash_seed member-selection
desc: The ip hardware fib load-balance distribution command allows the configuration of dynamic load balancing (DLB) on ECMP Groups.

title: ip hardware fib next-hop resource optimization
cmd:
ip hardware fib next-hop resource optimization options
no ip hardware fib next-hop resource optimization options
desc: The ip hardware fib next-hop resource optimization command enables or disables the resource optimization features on the switch.

title: ip hardware fib optimize
cmd: ip hardware fib optimize exact-match prefix-length prefix-length prefix-length
desc: The ip hardware fib optimize command enables IPv4 route scale.

title: ip hardware fib optimize prefixes
cmd:
ip hardware fib optimize vrf vrf_name prefixes minimum count num_prefixes
no ip hardware fib optimize vrf vrf_name prefixes minimum count num_prefixes
desc: The ip hardware fib optimize prefixes command in the Global Configuration Mode reserves IPv4 optimized prefixes on the default and non-default VRFs.

title: ip helper-address
cmd:
ip helper-address ipv4_addr [vrf vrf_name][source-address ipv4_addr | source-interface interfaces]
no ip helper-address [ipv4_addr]
default ip helper-address [ipv4_addr]
desc: The ip helper-address command enables the DHCP relay agent on the Interface Configuration Mode and specifies a forwarding address for DHCP requests.

title: as the source IP address when relaying IPv4 DHCP messages to the server at 1.1.1
cmd: as the source IP address when relaying IPv4 DHCP messages to the server at 1.1.1.1.
desc: switch(config)# interface ethernet 1/2 switch(config-if-Et1/2)# ip helper-address 1.

title: ip icmp redirect
cmd:
ip icmp redirect
no ip icmp redirect
default ip icmp redirect
desc: The ip icmp redirect command enables the transmission of ICMP redirect messages.

title: ip load-sharing
cmd:
ip load-sharing hardware seed
no ip load-sharing hardware
default ip load-sharing hardware
desc: The ip load-sharing command provides the hash seed to an algorithm the switch uses to distribute data streams among multiple equal-cost routes to an individual IPv4 subnet.

title: ip local-proxy-arp
cmd:
ip local-proxy-arp
no ip local-proxy-arp
default ip local-proxy-arp
desc: The ip local-proxy-arp command enables local proxy ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) in the Interface Configuration Mode.

title: ip multicast count
cmd:
ip multicast count [group_address [source_address] | bytes | packets]
no ip multicast count [group_address [source_address] | bytes | packets]
default ip multicast count [group_address [source_address] | bytes | packets]
desc: The ip multicast count command enables the IPv4 multicast route traffic counter of group and source addresses in either bytes or packets.

title: ip proxy-arp
cmd:
ip proxy-arp
no ip proxy-arp
default ip proxy-arp
desc: The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP in the Interface Configuration Mode.

title: ip route
cmd:
ip route [vrf_instance] dest_net next-hop [distance][tag_varname][rt_name]
no ip route [vrf_instance] dest_net [next-hop][distance]
default ip route [vrf_instance] dest_net [next-hop][distance]
desc: The ip route command creates a static route.

title: ip routing
cmd:
ip routing [vrf_instance]
no ip routing [delete_routes][vrf_instance
default ip routing [delete_routes][vrf_instance]
desc: The ip routing command enables IPv4 routing.

title: ip source binding
cmd:
ip source binding [ip_address][mac_address] vlan [vlan_range] interface [interface]
no ip source binding [ip_address][mac_address] vlan [vlan_range] interface [interface]
default ip source binding [ip_address][mac_address] vlan [vlan_range] interface [interface]
desc: Layer 2 Port-Channels support IP source guard (IPSG), not member ports.

title: ip verify source
cmd:
ip verify source vlan [vlan_range]
no ip verify source [vlan_range]
default ip verify source
desc: The ip verify source command configures IP source guard (IPSG) applicable only to Layer 2 ports.

title: ip verify
cmd:
ip verify unicast source reachable-via rpf_mode
no ip verify unicast
default ip verify unicast
desc: The ip verify command configures Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) for inbound IPv4 packets on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv4 routable 240.0.0.0/4
cmd:
ipv4 routable 240.0.0.0/4
no ipv4 routable 240.0.0.0/4
default ipv4 routable 240.0.0.0/4
desc: The ipv4 routable 240.

title: load-balance cluster
desc: The load-balance cluster command enters the Cluster Load Balancing (CLB) Configuration Mode and configure parameters for cluster load-balancing on a network.

title: load-balance method
cmd:
load-balance method [flow round-robin] [spine port-index]
no load-balance method [flow round-robin] [spine port-index]
desc: The load-balance command in the Cluster Load Balancing Configuration Mode configures the method of load-balancing traffic on the cluster.

title: member Ethernet
cmd:
member Ethernet interface_number
no member Ethernet interface_number
desc: The member Ethernet command in the Port Group Host Configuration Mode configures per port hardware interfaces for cluster load balancing on the switch.

title: platform barefoot bfrt vrf
desc: The platform barefoot bfrt vrf command configures the forwarding plane agent on supported platforms to restart and listen on the configured VRF for connections.

title: platform trident forwarding-table partition
cmd:
platform trident forwarding-table partition size
no platform trident forwarding-table partition
default platform trident forwarding-table partition
desc: The platform trident forwarding-table partition command provides a shared table memory for L2, L3 and algorithmic LPM entries that can be partitioned in different ways.

title: platform trident routing-table partition
cmd:
platform trident routing-table partition size
no platform trident routing-table partition
default platform trident routing-table partition
desc: The platform trident routing-table partition command manages the partition sizes for the hardware LPM table that stores IPv6 routes of varying sizes.

title: port group host
cmd:
port group host word
no port group host word
desc: The port group host command enters the Port Group Host Configuration mode and configures additional port parameters for Cluster Load Balancing by identifying the ports connected to the GPU server.

title: rib fib policy
cmd:
rib [ipv4 | ipv6] fib policy name
no rib [ipv4 | ipv6] fib policy name
default rib [ipv4 | ipv6] fib policy name
desc: The rib fib policy command enables FIB policy for a particular VRF under router general configuration mode.

title: show arp
desc: The show arp command displays all ARP tables.

title: show dhcp server
cmd: show dhcp server [ipv4 | ipv6 | leases | vrf]
desc: Use the show dhcp server command to display DHCP server information.

title: show dhcp server leases
desc: Use the show dhcp server leases command to display DHCP server lease information.

title: show hardware capacity
desc: The show hardware capacity command displays the utilization of the hardware resources:

title: show hardware resource DlbEcmpGroupTable agent *
desc: The following platforms use the show hardware resource DlbEcmpGroupTable agent * command: • DCS-7050CX4 • DCS-7050DX4-32S-F • DCS-7050PX4-32S-F • DCS-7050SDX4 • DCS-7050SPX4 • 7358X4-SC

title: show hardware resource l3 summary
desc: The show hardware resource l3 summary command displays a summary of used hardware entries and the total available capacity for Layer 3 features such as next-hops and ECMP groups.

title: show interface tunnel
desc: The show interface tunnel command displays the interface tunnel information.

title: show ip arp
desc: The show ip arp command displays ARP cache entries that map an IPv4 address to a corresponding MAC address.

title: show ip route host
desc: The show ip route host command displays all host routes in the host forwarding table.

title: via 172.17.254.105 on Vlan3912 switch>
desc: Vlan3992 switch>

title: show ip route match tag
cmd: show ip route [vrf_instance] address match tag
desc: The show ip route match tag command displays the route tag assigned to the specified IPv4 address or subnet.

title: show ip route summary
desc: The show ip route summary command displays the number of routes, categorized by destination prefix, in the routing table.

title: show ip verify source
desc: The show ip verify source command displays the IP source guard (IPSG) configuration, operational states, and IP-MAC binding entries for the configuration mode interface.

title: show platform arad ip route
desc: The show platform arad ip route command shows resources for all IPv4 routes in hardware.

title: show platform barefoot bfrt
desc: The show platform barefoot bfrt command displays information about the current BfRuntime server configuration.

title: show platform fap eedb ip-tunnel gre interface tunnel
cmd: show platform fap eedb ip-tunnel gre interface tunnel number
desc: The show platform fap eedb ip-tunnel gre interface tunnel command verifies the tunnel encapsulation programming for the tunnel interface.

title: show platform fap tcam summary
desc: The show platform fap tcam summary command displays information about the TCAM bank that is allocated for GRE packet termination lookup.

title: show platform trident forwarding-table partition
cmd:
show platform trident forwarding-table partition
show platform trident forwarding-table partition flexible
desc: The show platform trident forwarding-table partition command displays the size of the L2 MAC entry tables, L3 IP forwarding tables, and Longest Prefix Match (LPM) routes.

title: show platform trident l3 shadow dlb-ecmp-group-control
cmd: show platform trident l3 shadow dlb-ecmp-group-control
desc: The show platform trident l3 shadow dlb-ecmp-group-control displays information about Dynamic Load Balancing with ECMP groups.

title: show rib route ip
desc: The show rib route ip command displays a list of IPv4 Routing Information Base (RIB) routes.

title: show rib route fib policy excluded
cmd: show rib route [ipv4 | ipv6] fib policy excluded
desc: The show rib route fib policy excluded command displays the RIB routes filtered by FIB policy.

title: show rib route summary
desc: The show rib route summary command displays information about the routes present in the Routing Information Base.

title: show routing-context vrf
desc: The show routing-context vrf command displays the context-active VRF.

title: show snapshot counters ecmp history
desc: The show snapshot counters ecmp history displays information about the AGM configuration.

title: show tunnel fib static interface gre
cmd: show tunnel fib static interface gre number
desc: The show tunnel fib static interface gre command displays the Forwarding Information Base (FIB) information for a static interface GRE tunnel.

title: show vrf
desc: The show vrf command displays the VRF name, RD, supported protocols, state and included interfaces for the specified VRF or for all VRFs on the switch.

title: start snapshot counters
cmd:
start snapshot counters ecmp poll interval interval [milliseconds | microseconds] duration duration
seconds destination_url
desc: The start snapshot counters ecmp allows the monitoring of packets and bytes traversing the members of the configured ECMP groups on the switch with a high time resolution.

title: tcp mss ceiling
desc: The tcp mss ceiling command configures the Maximum Segment Size (MSS) limit in the TCP header on the configuration mode interface and enables TCP MSS clamping.

title: tunnel
desc: The tunnel command configures options for protocol-over-protocol tunneling.

title: vrf (Interface mode)
desc: The vrf command adds the configuration mode interface to the specified VRF.

title: vrf instance
cmd:
vrf instance [vrf_name]
no vrf instance [vrf_name]
default vrf instance [vrf_name]
desc: The vrf instance command places the switch in VRF configuration mode for the specified VRF.

title: IPv6
desc: EOS supports Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4) and Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6) for routing packets across network boundaries.

title: IPv6 Description
desc: Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6) consists of a communications protocol used for relaying network packets across a set of connected networks using the Internet Protocol suite.

title: IPv6 Address Format
desc: IPv6 addresses have 128 bits, represented by eight 16-bit hexadecimal numbers separated by colons.

title: IPv6 DHCP Snooping
desc: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) snooping is a Layer 2 feature that is configured on LAN switches.

title: Neighbor Discovery Protocol
desc: The Neighbor Discovery Protocol (RFC 4861) operates with IPv6 to facilitate the following tasks for nodes within a specified prefix space: • Auto-configuring a node IPv6 address.

title: IPv6 Duplicate Address Detection Proxy
desc: Overview IPv6 Duplicate Address Detection Proxy ensures that all IPv6 addresses assigned on a network segment are unique.

title: IPv6 ECMP
cmd: ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience 2001:db8:0::/64 capacity 5 redundancy 3
desc: Multiple routes configured to the same destination with the same administrative distance comprise an Equal Cost Multi-Path (ECMP) route.

title: IPv6 Neighbor Discovery
desc: The IPv6 Neighbor Discovery protocol defines a method for nodes to perform the following network maintenance tasks: • Determine Layer 2 addresses for neighbors known to reside on attached links.

title: Router Advertisement Interval
desc: The ipv6 nd ra interval command configures the interval between IPv6 RA transmissions from the configuration mode interface.

title: Router Advertisement Flag Configuration
desc: The following commands set the specified configuration flag in IPv6 RAs transmitted from the configuration mode interface: • The ipv6 nd managed-config-flag command sets the managed address configuration flag.

title: IPv6 DHCP Relay Status
desc: The show ip dhcp relay command displays the status of DHCP relay agent parameters on the switch and each interface where at least one feature parameter is listed.

title: IPv6 DHCP Relay Counters
desc: The show ipv6 dhcp relay counters command displays the number of DHCP packets received, forwarded, or dropped on the switch and on all interfaces enabled as DHCP relay agents.

title: IPv6 Commands
desc: Global Configuration Commands • ipv6 dhcp relay always-on • ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address • ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address • ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience • ipv6 hardware fib nexthop-index

title: clear ipv6 dhcp relay counters
desc: The clear ipv6 dhcp relay counters command resets the DHCP relay counters.

title: clear ipv6 dhcp snooping counters
desc: The clear ipv6 dhcp snooping counters command resets the DHCP snooping packet counters.

title: clear ipv6 neighbors
desc: The clear ipv6 neighbors command removes the specified dynamic IPv6 neighbor discovery cache entries.

title: ipv6 address
desc: The ipv6 address command assigns a global IPv6 address to the IPv6 interface, and defines the prefix length.

title: ipv6 dhcp relay always-on
cmd:
ipv6 dhcp relay always-on
no ipv6 dhcp relay always-on
default ipv6 dhcp relay always-on
desc: The iv6p dhcp relay always-on command enables the switch DHCP relay agent on the switch regardless of the DHCP relay agent status on any interface.

title: ipv6 dhcp relay destination
cmd:
ipv6 dhcp relay destination [ipv6_addr][source-address ipv6_addr]
no ipv6 dhcp relay destination [ipv6_addr]
default ipv6 dhcp relay destination [ipv6_addr]
desc: The ipv6 dhcp relay destination command enables the DHCPv6 relay agent and sets the destination address on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address
cmd:
ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address
no ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address
default ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address
desc: The ipv6 dhcp relay option link-layer address command enables the DHCPv6 relay agent to configure the client link layer address option to solicit and request messages.

title: ipv6 dhcp relay option remote-id
cmd:
ipv6 dhcp relay option remote-id format {%m:%h:%p | %m:%i | %m:%p}
no ipv6 dhcp relay option remote-id format {%m:%h:%p | %m:%i | %m:%p}
desc: The ipv6 dhcp relay option command configures DHCPv6 Relay Option 37, Remote-ID, to add the MAC address, interface ID or name, and hostname as the remote-id format.

title: ipv6 enable
cmd:
ipv6 enable
no ipv6 enable
default ipv6 enable
desc: The ipv6 enable command enables IPv6 on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address
cmd:
ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address ipv6_prefix summary-only software-forward
no ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address ipv6_prefix
default ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address ipv6_prefix
desc: The ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address command specifies the routing table repository of specified IPv6 route.

title: ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience
cmd:
ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience net_prfx capacity nhop_max redundancy duplicates
no ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience net_addr
default ipv6 hardware fib ecmp resilience net_addr
desc: The ip hardware fib ecmp resilience command configures a fixed number of next hop entries in the hardware ECMP table for the specified IPv6 address prefix.

title: ipv6 hardware fib nexthop-index
cmd:
ipv6 hardware fib nexthop nxthop_index [PREFIX]
no ipv6 hardware fib nexthop
default ipv6 hardware fib nexthop
desc: The ipv6 hardware fib nexthop-index command deterministically selects the next hop used for ECMP routes.

title: ipv6 nd dad proxy
desc: IPv6 DAD Proxy can be configured on an interface, and you must configure an IPv6 address on the interface and then enable IPv6 on the interface.

title: ipv6 nd managed-config-flag
cmd:
ipv6 nd managed-config-flag
no ipv6 nd managed-config-flag
default ipv6 nd managed-config-flag
desc: The ipv6 nd managed-config-flag command causes the managed address configuration flag to be set in IPv6 RA packets transmitted from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ns-interval
cmd:
ipv6 nd ns-interval period
no ipv6 nd ns-interval
default ipv6 nd ns-interval
desc: The ipv6 nd ns-interval command configures the interval between IPv6 Neighbor Solicitation (NS) transmissions from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd other-config-flag
cmd:
ipv6 nd other-config-flag
no ipv6 nd other-config-flag
default ipv6 nd other-config-flag
desc: The ipv6 nd other-config-flag command configures the configuration mode interface to send IPv6 RAs with the other stateful configuration flag set.

title: ipv6 nd prefix
cmd:
ipv6 nd prefix ipv6_prefix LIFETIME [FLAGS]
ipv6 nd prefix ipv6_prefix no-advertise
no ipv6 nd prefix ipv6_prefix
default ipv6 nd prefix ipv6_prefix
desc: The ipv6 nd prefix command configures neighbor discovery Router Advertisements (RAs) prefix inclusion for RAs sent from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra disabled
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra disabled [SCOPE]
no ipv6 nd ra disabled
default ipv6 nd ra disabled
desc: The ipv6 nd ra disabled command suppress IPv6 Router Advertisement (RA) transmissions on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra dns-server
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra dns-server ipv6_addr SERVER_LIFE
no ipv6 nd ra dns-server ipv6_addr
default ipv6 nd ra dns-server ipv6_addr
desc: The ipv6 nd ra dns-server command configures the IPv6 address of a preferred Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) for the command mode interface to include in its neighbor-discovery Router Advertisements (RAs).

title: ipv6 nd ra dns-servers lifetime
desc: The ipv6 nd ra dns-servers lifetime command sets the default value that the configuration mode interface uses for the lifetime of any Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) configured on the interface.

title: times the RA interval configured on the interface. A lifetime of zero seconds me
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra dns-servers lifetime period
no ipv6 nd ra dns-servers lifetime
default ipv6 nd ra dns-servers lifetime
desc: not be used for name resolution.

title: ipv6 nd ra dns-suffix
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra dns-suffix domain SUFFIX_LIFE
no ipv6 nd ra dns-suffix ipv6_addr
default ipv6 nd ra dns-suffix ipv6_addr
desc: The ipv6 nd ra dns-suffix command creates a DNS Search List (DNSSL) for the command mode interface to include in its neighbor-discovery Router Advertisements as defined in RFC 6106 .

title: ipv6 nd ra dns-suffixes lifetime
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra dns-suffixes lifetime period
no ipv6 nd ra dns-suffixes lifetime
default ipv6 nd ra dns-suffixes lifetime
desc: The ipv6 nd ra dns-suffixes lifetime command sets the default value that the configuration mode interface uses for the lifetime of any DNS Search List (DNSSL) configured on the interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra hop-limit
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra hop-limit quantity
no ipv6 nd ra hop-limit lifetime
default ipv6 nd ra hop-limit lifetime
desc: The ipv6 nd ra hop-limit command sets a suggested hop-limit value to be included in Router Advertisement (RA) packets.

title: ipv6 nd ra interval
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra interval [SCALE] ra_period [minimum_period]
no ipv6 nd ra interval
default ipv6 nd ra interval
desc: The ipv6 nd ra interval command configures the interval between IPv6 Router Advertisement transmissions from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra lifetime
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra lifetime ra_lifetime
no ipv6 nd ra lifetime
default ipv6 nd ra lifetime
desc: The ipv6 nd ra lifetime command specifies the value that the switch places in the router lifetime field of IPv6 Router Advertisements sent from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra mtu suppress
cmd:
ipv6 nd ra mtu suppress
no ipv6 nd ra mtu suppress
default ipv6 nd ra mtu suppress
desc: The ipv6 nd ra mtu suppress command suppresses the Router Advertisement (RA) MTU option on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd ra rx accept default-route
cmd: ipv6 nd ra rx accept default
desc: The ipv6 nd ra rx accept default-route command configures the interface to accept the default gateway information from the Router Advertisement (RA) in the interface configuration.

title: ipv6 nd reachable-time
cmd:
ipv6 nd reachable-time period
no ipv6 nd reachable-time
default ipv6 nd reachable-time
desc: The ipv6 nd reachable-time command specifies the time period that the switch includes in the reachable time field of RAs sent from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 nd router-preference
cmd:
ipv6 nd router-preference [RANK [high | low | medium]]
no ipv6 nd router-preference
default ipv6 nd router-preference
desc: The ipv6 nd router-preference command specifies the value that the switch enters in the Default Router Preference (DRP) field of Router Advertisements (RAs) that it sends from the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 neighbor cache persistent
cmd:
ipv6 neighbor cache persistent
no ipv6 neighbor cache persistent
default ipv6 neighbor cache persistent
desc: The ipv6 neighbor cache persistent command restores the IPv6 neighbor cache after reboot.

title: ipv6 neighbor
cmd:
ipv6 neighbor ipv6_addr PORT mac_addr
no ipv6 neighbor ipv6_address PORT
default ipv6 neighbor ipv6_addr PORT
desc: The ipv6 neighbor command creates an IPv6 neighbor discovery cache static entry.

title: ipv6 route
cmd:
ipv6 route dest_prefix NEXTHOP [DISTANCE][TAG_OPT][RT_NAME]
no ipv6 route dest_prefix [nexthop_addr][DISTANCE]
default ipv6 route dest_prefix [nexthop_addr][DISTANCE]
desc: The ipv6 route command creates an IPv6 static route.

title: ipv6 unicast-routing
cmd:
ipv6 unicast-routing
no ipv6 unicast-routing [DELETE_ROUTES]
default ipv6 unicast-routing [DELETE_ROUTES]
desc: The ipv6 unicast-routing command enables the forwarding of IPv6 unicast packets.

title: ipv6 verify
cmd:
ipv6 verify unicast source reachable-via RPF_MODE
no ipv6 verify unicast
default ipv6 verify unicast
desc: The ipv6 verify command configures Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) for inbound IPv6 packets on the configuration mode interface.

title: ipv6 dhcp snooping
cmd:
ipv6 dhcp snooping [remote-id option | vlan [$ | vlan-range]]
no ipv6 dhcp snooping [remote-id option | vlan [$ | vlan-range]]
default ipv6 dhcp snooping [remote-id option | vlan [$ | vlan-range]]
desc: The ipv6 dhcp snooping command enables DHCP snooping globally on the switch.

title: pim ipv6 sparse-mode
cmd:
pim ipv6 sparse-mode
no pim ipv6
no pim ipv6 sparse-mode
default pim ipv6
default pim ipv6 sparse-mode
desc: The pim ipv6 sparse-mode command enables PIM Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) and IGMP (router mode) on the configuration mode interface.

title: show ipv6 dhcp snooping
desc: The show ipv6 dhcp snooping command displays information about the DHCP snooping configuration.

title: show ipv6 dhcp snooping counters
desc: The show ipv6 dhcp snooping counters command displays counters that track the quantity of DHCP request and reply packets that the switch receives.

title: show ipv6 dhcp snooping hardware
desc: The show ipv6 dhcp snooping hardware command displays internal hardware DHCP snooping status on the switch.

title: show ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address
desc: The show ipv6 hardware fib aggregate-address command displays the IPv6 prefixes that are restricted from entry into the hardware routing table.

title: show ipv6 interface
desc: The ipv6 interface command displays the status of specified routed interfaces that are configured for IPv6.

title: show ipv6 nd dad proxy
desc: Display information about the IPv6 Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) feature configured on an interface.

title: show ipv6 nd ra internal state
cmd: show ipv6 nd ra internal state [INTERFACE_NAME]
desc: The ipv6 nd ra internal state command displays the state of the IPv6 Router Advertisement (RA) daemon for the specified routable interface.

title: show ipv6 neighbors
desc: The show ipv6 neighbors command displays the IPv6 neighbor discovery cache.

title: show ipv6 route
desc: The show ipv6 route command displays IPv6 routing table entries that are in the Forwarding Information Base (FIB), including static routes, routes to directly connected networks, and dynamically learned routes.

title: show ipv6 route age
desc: The show ipv6 route age command displays the IPv6 route age to the specified IPv6 address or prefix.

title: show ipv6 route host
desc: The show ipv6 route host command displays all host routes in the IPv6 host forwarding table.

title: show ipv6 route interface
desc: The show ipv6 route interface command displays routing table entries on a specified routed port.

title: show ipv6 route match tag
cmd: show ipv6 route ADDRESS match tag
desc: The show ipv6 route match tag command displays the route tag assigned to the specified IPv6 address or prefix.

title: show platform fap mroute ipv6
desc: The show platform fap mroute ipv6 command enables PIM Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) and IGMP (router mode) on the configuration mode interface.

title: show rib route ipv6
cmd: show rib route ipv6 [vrf vrf_name] [PREFIX][ROUTE TYPE]
desc: The show rib route ipv6 command displays a list of IPv6 Routing Information Base (RIB) routes.

title: hardware counter feature ip
cmd:
hardware counter feature ip in|out layer3
no hardware counter feature ip in|out layer3
default hardware counter feature in|out layer3
desc: Use the hardware counter feature ip command to enable ingress and egress counters at Layer 3.

title: ACL Types
desc: The switch supports the following ACL types: • IPv4 matches on IPv4 source or destination addresses, with L4 modifiers including protocol, port number, IPsec tunnel interfaces, and DSCP value.

title: ACL Structure
desc: An ACL is an ordered list of rules that defines access restrictions for the entities (the control plane or an interface) to which it is applied.

title: ACL Rules
desc: The switch compares an ACL rule's command list to inbound and outbound packet fields.

title: ACL Rule Tracking
desc: ACL rule tracking determines how ACL rules impact traffic on the interfaces where those rules are applied.

title: ACL Configuration
desc: You create and modify Access Control Lists (ACLs) in an ACL-configuration mode.

title: ACL Rule Tracking Configuration
desc: ACL Rules provide a log option that produces a syslog message about the packets matching a desired packet.

title: SNMP Server
cmd: snmp-server community community-name [view viewname] [ro | rw] acl_name
desc: Use the snmp-server community command to apply the SNMP Server Service ACLs to restrict which hosts can access SNMP services on the switch:

title: BGP
desc: Use the ip access-group (Service ACLs) and ipv6 access-group (Service ACLs) commands to apply Service ACLs for controlling connections to the BGP routing protocol agent: switch(config)# router bgp <asn>

title: OSPF
desc: Use the ip access-group (Service ACLs) and ipv6 access-group (Service ACLs) commands to apply Service ACLs to control packets processed by the OSPF routing protocol agent:

title: PIM
desc: Use the access-group command to apply Service ACLs for controlling packets processed by the PIM routing protocol agent: switch(config)# router pim switch(config-router-pim)# ipv4 switch(config-router-pim-ipv4)# access-group <acl_name>

title: IGMP
desc: Use the ip igmp access-group command to apply Service ACLs for controlling packets processed by the IGMP management protocol agent: switch(config)# router igmp switch(config-router-igmp)# ip igmp access-group <acl_name>

title: LDP
desc: Use the ip access-group (Service ACLs) to apply Service ACLs for controlling packets and connections processed by the LDP MPLS label distribution protocol: switch(config)# mpls ldp switch(config-mpls-ldp)# ip access-group <acl_name>

title: MPLS Ping and Traceroute
desc: Use the ip access-group (Service ACLs) and ipv6 access-group (Service ACLs) commands to apply Service ACLs for controlling connections accepted by the MPLS Ping agent: switch(config)# mpls ping switch(config-mpls-ping)# ip access-group <acl_name> [vr…

title: active
desc: Sub-interface ACLs Limitations The sub-interface ACLs feature contains the following limitations: • Egress IPv4 ACLs on subinterfaces are not supported when sharing mode is disabled for Egress IPv4 RACLs.

title: Route Maps
desc: A route map is an ordered set of rules that controls the redistribution of IP routes into a protocol domain based on criteria such as route metrics, access control lists, next-hop addresses, and route tags.

title: Route Map Description
desc: Route maps are composed of route map statements, each consisting of a list of match and set commands.

title: Route Map Configuration
desc: Route maps are created and modified in route-map configuration mode.

title: Route Map Creation and Editing
desc: Creating a Route Map Statement To create a route map, use the route-map command, including the map name and filter type (deny or permit).

title: Prefix Lists
desc: A prefix list is an ordered set of rules that defines route redistribution access for a specified IP address space.

title: Prefix List Configuration
desc: A prefix list is an ordered set of rules that defines route redistribution access for a specified IP address space.

title: IPv6 Prefix Lists
desc: Creating an IPv6 Prefix List The switch provides an IPv6 prefix-list configuration mode for creating and modifying IPv6 prefix lists.

title: Static Routes Redistribution into IGPs
desc: Use match ip next-hop to match against next-hops in a route-map.

title: is a configured next-hop for static route 10.20.30.0/24.
cmd: is a configured next-hop for static route 10.20.30.0/24.
desc: The following commands configure a route map: switch(config)# route-map routeMapName switch(config-route-map-routeMapName)# match ip next-hop prefix-list prefixListName For example, based on the route-map mentioned in the preceding command, to redist…

title: Port ACLs with User-Defined Fields
desc: Describes the support for specifying User-Defined Fields (UDF) in Port ACLs, including IPv4, IPv6, and MAC ACLs.

title: IPv6 Port ACL
desc: The following configurations create a new profile based on the default profile.

title: Port ACLs with User-Defined Fields Limitations
cmd: Port ACLs with User-Defined Fields Limitations
desc: User-defined fields consume a limited set of copy resources.

title: clear ip access-lists counters
cmd: clear ip access-lists counters acl_name scope
desc: The clear ip access-lists counters command sets ACL counters to zero for the specified IPv4 Access Control List (ACL).

title: clear ipv6 access-lists counters
desc: The clear ipv6 access-lists counters command sets ACL counters to zero for the specified IPv6 Access Control List (ACL).

title: continue (route map)
cmd:
continue next_seq
no continue next_seq
default continue next_seq
desc: The continue command creates a route map statement entry that enables additional route map evaluation of routes with parameters meeting the statement matching criteria.

title: counters per-entry
cmd:
counters per-entry
no counters per-entry
default counters per-entry
desc: The counters per-entry command places the ACL in counting mode.

no hardware access-list resource sharing vlan ipv4 out
process and impacts traffic forwarding.

cmd:
hardware access-list update default-result permit
no hardware access-list update default-result permit
default hardware access-list update default-result permit
series switches.
desc: The hardware access-list update default-result permit command configures the switch to permit all traffic on Ethernet and VLAN interfaces with ACLs applied to them while modifying the ACLs.

title: hardware counter feature acl out
cmd:

no hardware counter feature acl out [options [ipv4 | ipv6]
default hardware counter feature acl out [options [ipv4 | ipv6]
desc: The hardware counter feature acl out command enables egress ACL hardware counters for IPv4 or IPv6 and count the number of packets matching rules associated with egress ACLs applied to various interfaces on a switch.

cmd:

no ip access-group list_name [direction [in | out]]
default ip access-group list_name [direction [in | out]]

title: ip access-list
cmd:
ip access-list list_name
no ip access-list list_name
default ip access-list list_name

cmd:
ip access-list standard list_name
no ip access-list standard list_name
default ip access-list standard list_name

seq_number remark comment

ipv6 access-list list_name

ipv6 prefix-list list_name

title: match (route-map)

match condition

desc: The match command creates a route map statement entry that specifies one route filtering command.

seq_num permit source_addr dest_addr protocol log

title: set community (route-map)

title: show platform fap acl
