no vrrp group ipv4 ipv4_address
default vrrp group ipv4 ipv4_address
desc: The vrrp ipv4 command configures the primary IP address for the specified VRRP virtual router.

title: vrrp ipv4 checksum pseudo-header exclude
cmd:
vrrp group ipv4 checksum pseudo-header exclude
no vrrp group ipv4 checksum pseudo-header exclude
desc: This command excludes the pseudo-header in IPv4 VRRPv3 checksum calculation on the VRRP group on the configuration mode interface of the switch and supports IPv4 VRRPv3 interoperability.

title: vrrp ipv4 secondary
cmd:
vrrp group ipv4 ipv4_addr secondary
no vrrp group ipv4 ipv4_addr secondary
default vrrp group ipv4 ipv4_addr secondary
desc: The vrrp ipv4 secondary command assigns a secondary IP address to the specified virtual router.

title: vrrp ipv4 version
cmd:
vrrp group ipv4 version VERSION_NUMBER
no vrrp group ipv4 version
default vrrp group ipv4 version
desc: The vrrp ipv4 version command enables VRRP on the configuration mode interface and configures the VRRP version for the specified VRRP virtual router.

title: vrrp ipv6
cmd:
vrrp group ip ipv6_address
no vrrp group ip ipv6_address
default vrrp group ip ipv6_address
desc: The vrrp ipv6 command configures the IPv6 address for the specified VRRP virtual router.

title: vrrp mac-address advertisement-interval
cmd:
vrrp group mac-address advertisement-interval period
no vrrp group mac-address
default vrrp group mac-address
desc: The vrrp mac-address advertisement-interval command specifies the interval between advertisement packets sent by the master router to the VRRP group members.

title: vrrp peer authentication
cmd:
vrrp group peer authentication AUTH_PARAMETER
no vrrp group peer authentication
default vrrp group peer authentication
desc: The vrrp peer authentication command configures parameters the switch uses to authenticate virtual router packets it receives from other VRRP routers in the group.

title: vrrp preempt
cmd:
vrrp group preempt
no vrrp group preempt
default vrrp group preempt
desc: The vrrp preempt command controls a virtual routers preempt mode setting.

title: vrrp preempt delay
cmd:
vrrp group preempt delay [MINIMUM_INTERVAL] [RELOAD_INTERVAL]
no vrrp group preempt delay [DELAY_TYPE]
default vrrp group preempt delay [DELAY_TYPE]
desc: The vrrp preempt delay command specifies the interval between a VRRP preemption event and the point when the switch becomes the master VRRP router.

title: vrrp priority-level
cmd:
vrrp group priority-level level
no vrrp group priority-level level
default vrrp group priority-level level
desc: The vrrp priority-level command configures the switchs priority setting for a VRRP virtual router.

title: vrrp session description
cmd:
vrrp group session description label_text
no vrrp group session description
default vrrp group session description
desc: The vrrp session description command associates a text string to a VRRP virtual router on the configuration mode interface.

title: vrrp timers delay reload
cmd:
vrrp group timers delay reload [INTERVAL]
no vrrp group timers delay reload
default vrrp group timers delay reload
desc: The vrrp timers delay reload command delays the time for VRRP initialization after a system reboot.

title: vrrp tracked-object
cmd:
vrrp group tracked-object object_name ACTION amount
no vrrp group tracked-object object_name ACTION
default vrrp group tracked-object object_name ACTION
desc: The vrrp tracked-object command configures the VRRP client process on the configuration mode interface to track the specified tracked object and react when its status changes to down.

title: action drop (DirectFlow-flow mode)
cmd:
action drop
no action drop
default action drop
desc: The action drop command configures packets that match an entry to be dropped.

title: action mirror (DirectFlow-flow mode)
cmd:
action DIRECTION mirror INT_NAME
no action DIRECTION mirrorINT_NAME
default action DIRECTION mirror INT_NAME
desc: The action mirror command can be used to ingress or egress mirror traffic to a mirror destination.

title: action output (DirectFlow-flow mode)
cmd:
action output DESTINATION
no action output DESTINATION
default action output DESTINATION
desc: The action output command configures an Ethernet or port channel interface as the output of a specified port mirroring session.

title: action set (DirectFlow-flow mode)
cmd:
action set CONDITION
no action set CONDITION
default action set CONDITION
desc: The action set command allows you to configure a packet to be routed out a layer three interface using a DirectFlow entry.

title: directflow
desc: The directflow command places the switch in DirectFlow configuration mode.

title: flow (DirectFlow)
cmd:
flow flow_name
no flow flow_name
default flow flow_name
desc: The flow command places the switch in flow configuration mode.

title: persistent
desc: DirectFlow flows are persistent by default.

title: priority (DirectFlow-flow mode)
cmd:
priority priority_value
no priority
default priority
desc: The priority command sets the priority for the flow match rules.

title: show directflow
desc: The show directflow command displays summary information for DirectFlow.

title: show directflow flows
desc: The show directflow flows command displays the contents of the flow table, showing each entry with its match rules, actions, and packet counters.

title: timeout (DirectFlow-flow mode)
desc: The timeout command, in DirectFlow mode, command configures the connection timeout period for connection sessions.

title: Decap Groups
desc: These sections describe the Decap Groups features: • Decap Groups Overview • Configuring Decap Groups • Configuring Generic UDP Encapsulation (GUE) Decap Groups • Decap Groups Commands

title: Decap Groups Overview
desc: The Decap Group feature provides a data structure that receives encapsulated packets and extracts the payload.

title: Decap Groups Commands
desc: Decap Groups Configuration Commands • ip decap-group • tunnel decap-interface • tunnel decap-ip • tunnel type Decap Groups Show Command • show ip decap-group

title: ip decap-group
cmd:
ip decap-group group_name
no ip decap-group group_name
default ip decap-group group_name
desc: The ip decap-group command places the switch in decap-group configuration mode which permits the creation or modification of Decap Groups.

title: show ip decap-group
desc: The show ip decap-group command displays the IP decap groups available on the switch.

title: tunnel decap-interface
desc: The tunnel decap-interface command adds all locally configured IP addresses to the specific Layer 3 interface per Decap Group.

title: tunnel decap-ip
desc: The tunnel decap-ip command specifies the IP address of packets handled by the Decap-Group Configuration Mode for the Decap Group.

title: tunnel type
desc: The tunnel type command specifies the tunnel protocol for extracting payload from encapsulated packets that arrive on the IP address specified for the configuration mode decap group.

title: Nexthop Groups
desc: These sections describe the Nexthop groups: • Next-hop Group Description • Next-hop Group Configuration • Nexthop Group Commands

title: Next-hop Group Description
desc: Each routing table entry provides the next hop address to a specified destination.

title: Next-hop Group Configuration
desc: Next-hop groups are configured and modified in next-hop-group configuration mode.

title: Segment Security
desc: • Overview of MSS-Group • Configuring MSS-Group • Segment Security Considerations • Show Commands • Segment Security Commands

title: Segment Security Considerations
desc: • Multicast and Link-Local prefixes are not supported.

title: Segment Security Commands
desc: Global Configuration Commands • match-list input • router segment-security Match-List Input Configuration Commands • match (match-list input) Router Segment-Security Configuration Commands • segment policy policy-drop-all default

title: clear segment-security hardware counters
desc: The clear segment-security hardware command clears the MSS-Group (segment security) Hit and Drop counters for all hits, and the hits and drops for each separate segment's policy.

title: definition (segment)
cmd:
definition
no definition
default definition
match (segment definition)
desc: The definition command enters Router Segment-Security VRF Segment Definition Configuration mode.

title: from (segment policies)
cmd:
from segment_name policy [ policy-drop-all | policy-forward-all]
no from segment_name [policy [ policy-drop-all | policy-forward-all]
default from segment_name [policy [ policy-drop-all | policy-forward-all]
desc: The from command adds a policy to a segment in order to filter traffic from a specified segment, either the same segment or a different segment.

title: match-list input
cmd:
match-list input [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
no match-list input [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
no match-list input [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
desc: The match-list input command enters Match List Configuration mode for the specified match list, creating one if it does not exist.

title: match (match-list input)
cmd:
match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6]ip_address_prefix
no match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] ip_address_prefix
default match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] ip_address_prefix
desc: The match command adds an entry to a match list.

title: match (segment definition)
cmd:
match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
no match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
default match [prefix-ipv4 | prefix-ipv6] match_list_name
desc: The match command adds a match list to a segment definition.

title: policies (segment)
desc: The policies command places the switch in Router Segment Security VRF Segment Policies Configuration mode.

title: router segment-security
cmd:
router segment-security
no router segment-security
default router segment-security
segment
shutdown
vrf
desc: The router segment-security command enters Router Segment-Security Configuration Mode.

title: segment
cmd:
segment segment_name
no segment segment_name
default segment segment_name
desc: The segment command enters Router Segment-Security VRF Segment Configuration mode, creating a segment if one does not exist.

title: segment policy policy-drop-all default
cmd:
segment policy policy-drop-all default
no segment policy policy-drop-all default
default segment policy policy-drop-all default
desc: The segment policy policy-drop-all default command configures the switch to drop all traffic to all segments.

title: show match-list
desc: The show match-list command displays match lists of type IPv4 or IPv6.

title: show segment-security
desc: The show segment-security command shows the status and configuration of MSS-Group (segment security).

title: show segment-security hardware
cmd: show segment-security hardware [summary | detail | routes | counters][vrf vrf-name][segment segname]
desc: The show segment-security hardware command displays detailed information about the hardware resources used by the MSS-Group feature.

title: show segment-security hardware counters
desc: The show segment-security hardware counters command displays the counters for policies in each segment, including the default policies.

title: show segment-security hardware detail
cmd: show segment-security hardware detail [vrf vrf_name][segment seg_name]
desc: The show segment-security hardware detail command displays the hardware ID allocated to each segment, the prefixes programmed in hardware for each segment, and the adjacency index used by each prefix as determined from L3 hardware tables.

title: show segment-security hardware routes
cmd: show segment-security hardware routes [vrf vrf-name][segment seg-name]
desc: The show segment-security hardware routes command displays the route and type for each programmed prefix in hardware.

title: show segment-security hardware summary
cmd: show segment-security hardware summary [vrf vrf-name][segment seg-name]
desc: The show segment-security hardware summary command displays the hardware ID, number of prefixes, and number of successfully programmed prefixes for each VRF and segment specified.

title: VXLAN Control Service
desc: The VXLAN control service allows hardware VXLAN Tunnel End Points (VTEPs) to share state with each other in order to establish VXLAN tunnels without the need for a multicast control plane.

title: Hardware Switch Controller (HSC) Service
desc: Traffic between virtual machines which share a physical host (or between virtual machines and the rest of the network) is forwarded by virtual switches.

title: Network Topology Service
desc: The network topology service gathers information from CVX clients to provide a view of the physical topology of the network.

title: Static Topology Service
desc: Static Topology addresses cases where the deployment infrastructure in an OpenStack setup that manages Virtual Machines and Bare Metal servers does not enable LLDP on interfaces connecting hosts to switches.

title: and using IP address 10.24.24.1 as the source address for its outbound packets.
cmd: and using IP address 10.24.24.1 as the source address for its outbound packets.
desc: switch(config)# interface loopback 5 switch(config-if-Lo5)# ip address 10.

title: cvc1
desc: Configuring Network Topology Service

title: cvc2
desc: Configuring Network Topology Service

title: cvc3
desc: Configuring Network Topology Service

title: cvc4
desc: bcf6.85bd.8050 dsj14-rack14-tor1 • This command displays all connections in the topology. switch# show network physical-topology neighbors cvx287.sjc.aristanetworks.com Interface Neighbor Intf Neighbor Host

title: cvx
desc: CVX (CloudVision eXtension) aggregates and shares status across a network of physical switches running EOS.

title: heartbeat-timeout (CVX)
cmd:
heartbeat-timeout period
no heartbeat-timeout
default heartbeat-timeout
desc: The heartbeat-timeout command specifies the CVX timeout period.

title: management cvx
cmd:
management cvx
no management cvx
default management cvx
desc: The management cvx command places the switch in mgmt-CVX configuration mode to configure CVX client parameters.

title: manager
desc: The manager command configures the IP address of the OVSDB controller for the HSC service, allowing CVX to connect to the controller.

title: name-resolution force (CVX-OpenStack)
desc: The name-resolution force command initiates an OpenStack controller function that communicates with the OpenStack Keystone and Nova services to update names of VMs and tenants mapped by the local OpenStack instance.

title: ovsdb-shutdown
desc: The ovsdb-shutdown command shuts down the OVSDB server.

title: port (CVX)
cmd:
port port_number
no port
default port
desc: The port command specifies the TCP port number the CVX server listens on.

title: resync-period
cmd:
resync-period seconds
no resync-period
default resync-period
desc: The resync-period command configures the grace period for completion of synchronization between the VXLAN control service and clients after a CVX restart.

title: server host (Management-CVX)
cmd:
server host host
no server host
default server host
desc: The server host command configures the IP address or host name of the CVX server to which the CVX client device connects.

title: service hsc
desc: The service hsc command enters the CVX-HSC configuration mode where the HSC service is enabled and configured.

title: service openstack
desc: The service openstack command places the switch in CVX-OpenStack configuration mode.

title: service topology
desc: The service topology command configures the topology statically on CVX without running LLDP on the servers connected to switches.

title: service VXLAN
desc: The service VXLAN command enters the CVX-VXLAN configuration mode where the VXLAN control service is enabled and configured.

title: show cvx
desc: The show cvx command displays the enable status and current configuration of CVX.

title: show network physical-topology
desc: The show network physical-topology command displays the network topology discovered through CVX.

title: source-interface (Management-CVX)
cmd:
source-interface INT_NAME
no source-interface
default source-interface
desc: The source-interface command specifies the interface from where the IPv4 address is derived for use as the source for outbound CVX packets that the switch sends as a CVX client.

title: vtep (CVX-HSC)
cmd:
vtep flood list type all | any
no vtep flood list type
default vtep flood list type
desc: The HSC service sends flood lists to each VTEP through CVX.

title: vtep (CVX-VXLAN)
desc: The OVSDB management protocol includes provisions for control-plane MAC learning, which allows MAC addresses to be distributed among VTEPs without using the data plane.

title: RIP Conceptual Overview
desc: Routing Information Protocol (RIP) is a routing protocol typically used as an Interior Gateway Protocol (IGP).

title: RIP Commands
desc: Global Configuration Commands • router rip • router rip vrf Interface Configuration Commands • rip v2 multicast disable Router-RIP Configuration Mode • distance (RIP) • distribute-list (RIP) • metric default

title: distance (RIP)
cmd:
distance distance_value
no distance
default distance
desc: The distance command assigns an administrative distance to routes that the switch learns through RIP.

title: distribute-list (RIP)
cmd:
distribute-list [DIRECTION [in | out] [MAP [route-mapmap_name] [INTF [ethernet e_num]|[loopback
no distribute-list [DIRECTION [in | out] [MAP [route-mapmap_name] [INTF [ethernet e_num]|
default distribute-list [DIRECTION [in | out] [MAP [route-mapmap_name] [INTF [ethernet e_num]|
desc: The distribute-list command allows users to filter out routes that are received or sent out.

title: metric default
cmd:
metric default metric_value
no metric default
default metric default
desc: The metric default command specifies the metric value assigned to RIP routes learned from other protocols.

title: network (RIP)
cmd:
network NETWORK_ADDRESS
no network NETWORK_ADDRESS
default network NETWORK_ADDRESS
desc: The network command specifies which network the switch runs Routing Information Protocol (RIP), and also specifies which routes will be accepted into the RIP routing table.

title: redistribute (RIP)
cmd:
redistribute connected ROUTE_MAP
redistribute ROUTE_TYPE [ROUTE_MAP]
no redistribute connected ROUTE_MAP
no redistribute ROUTE_TYPE
default redistribute connected ROUTE_MAP
default redistribute ROUTE_TYPE
desc: The redistribute command enables the importing of routes from a specified routing domain to RIP.

title: rip v2 multicast disable
cmd:
rip v2 multicast disable
no rip v2 multicast disable
default rip v2 multicast disable
desc: The rip v2 multicast disable command specifies the transmission of Routing Information Protocol (RIP) Version 2 update packets from the configuration mode interface as broadcast to 255.

title: router rip vrf
cmd:
router rip vrf [RIP_INSTANCE]
no router rip vrf [RIP_INSTANCE]
default router rip vrf [RIP_INSTANCE]
desc: The router rip command places the switch in router-RIP configuration mode to configure an RIP routing instance in the non-default VRF.

title: show ip rip database
desc: The show ip rip database command displays information about routes in the Routing Information Base.

title: show ip rip neighbors
desc: The show ip rip neighbors command displays information about all RIP route gateways.

title: timers (RIP)
cmd:
timers [update_time | expire_time | deletion_time]
no timers
default timers
desc: The timers command configures the update interval, the expiration time, and the deletion time for routes received and sent through RIP.

title: OSPF Routes over GRE Tunnels
desc: This feature introduces support for OSPF routes over GRE tunnels under default and non-default VRFs.

title: Show Commands
desc: Hardware Acceleration Information To view whether hardware acceleration is running use the show bfd hardware acceleration command.

title: Redistribute Configuration
desc: Configuring the redistribute ospf command under the config-router-bgp mode with multiple OSPFv2 instances configured redistributes routes from all OSPFv2 instances into BGP.

title: OSPF Routes over GRE Tunnels Configuration
cmd: OSPF Routes over GRE Tunnels Configuration
desc: You may employ the following commands in the router OSPF mode: • tunnel routes • no tunnel routes • default tunnel routes To enable OSPFv2 over GRE tunnels, use the following commands: switch(config)# router ospf 6

title: auto-cost reference-bandwidth (OSPFv2)
cmd:
auto-cost reference-bandwidth rate
no auto-cost reference-bandwidth rate
default auto-cost reference-bandwidth rate
desc: The auto-cost reference-bandwidth command is a factor in the formula that calculates the default OSPFv2 cost for Ethernet interfaces.

title: adjacency exchange-start threshold (OSPFv2)
cmd:
adjacency exchange-start threshold peers
no adjacency exchange-start threshold
default adjacency exchange-start threshold
desc: The adjacency exchange-start threshold command sets the exchange-start options for an OSPF instance.

title: area default-cost (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id default-cost def_cost
no area area_id default-cost def_cost
default area area_id default-cost def_cost
desc: The area default-cost command specifies the cost for the default summary routes sent into a specified area.

title: area filter (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id filter net_addr
no area area_id filter net_addr
default area area_id filter net_addr
desc: The area filter command prevents an area from receiving Type 3 Summary LSAs and Type 4 APSR Summary LSAs from a specified subnet.

title: area not-so-stubby lsa type-7 convert type-5 (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id not-so-stubby lsa type-7 convert type-5
no area area_id not-so-stubby lsa type-7 convert type-5
default area area_id not-so-stubby lsa type-7 convert type-5
desc: The area not-so-stubby lsa type-7 convert type-5 command configures the switch to always translate Type-7 Link-State Advertisement (LSAs) to Type-5 LSAs.

title: area nssa (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id nssa [TYPE]
no area area_id nssa [TYPE]
default area area_id nssa [TYPE]
desc: The area nssa command configures an OSPFv2 area as a Not-So-Stubby Area (NSSA).

title: area nssa default-information-originate (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id nssa default-information-originate [VALUE][TYPE][EXCL]
no area area_id nssa default-information-originate
default area area_id nssa default-information-originate
desc: The default area nssa default-information-originate command sets default route origination for the Not-So-Stubby Area (NSSA), allowing the redistribute policy to advertise a default route if one is present.

title: area nssa no-summary (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id nssa no-summary
no area area_id nssa no-summary
default area area_id nssa no-summary
desc: The area nssa no-summary command configures the switch stop importing type-3 summary LSAs into the not-so-stubby area and sets the default summary route into the Not-So-Stubby Area (NSSA) in order to reach the inter-area prefixes.

title: area range (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id range net_addr [ADVERTISE_SETTING][COST_SETTING]
no area area_id range net_addr [ADVERTISE_SETTING][COST_SETTING]
default area area_id range net_addr [ADVERTISE_SETTING][COST_SETTING]
desc: The area range command configures OSPF Area Border Routers (ABRs) to consolidate or summarize routes, to set the cost setting routes, and to suppress summary route advertisements.

title: area stub (OSPFv2)
cmd:
area area_id stub [summarize]
no area area_id stub [summarize]
default area area_id stub [summarize]
desc: The area stub command sets the area type of an OSPF area to stub.

title: clear ip ospf neighbor
desc: The clear ip ospf command clears the neighbors statistics per interface.

title: compatible (OSPFv2)
cmd:
compatible rfc1583
no compatible rfc1583
default compatible rfc1583
desc: The compatible command allows the selective disabling of compatibility with RFC 2328.

title: default-information originate (OSPFv2)
cmd:
default-information originate [FORCE][VALUE][TYPE][MAP]
no default-information originate
default default-information originate
desc: The default-information originate command enables default route origination for normal areas.

title: distance ospf (OSPFv2)
cmd:
distance ospf [external | inter-area | intra-area]
no distance ospf [external | inter-area | intra-area]
default distance ospf [external | inter-area | intra-area]
desc: The distance ospf command specifies the administrative distance for intra-area, inter-area, or external OSPF routes.

title: distribute-list in
desc: A distribute list uses a route map or prefix list to filter specific routes from incoming OSPF LSAs.

title: dn-bit-ignore (OSPFv2)
desc: The dn-bit-ignore command results in the DN bit in Type 3 Summary LSAs to be ignored during the Shortest Path First (SPF) calculations.

title: interface Tunnel
desc: OSPF packets by default are sent with Time to Live (TTL) value 1.

title: ip ospf area
cmd:
ip ospf area area_id
no ip ospf area area_id
default ip ospf area area_id
desc: The ip ospf area command enables OSPFv2 on an interface and associates the area to the interface.

title: ip ospf authentication
cmd:
ip ospf authentication [METHOD]
no ip ospf authentication
default ip ospf authentication
desc: The ip ospf authentication command enables OSPFv2 authentication for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf authentication-key
cmd:
ip ospf authentication-key [ENCRYPT_TYPE] key_text
no ip ospf authentication-key
default ip ospf authentication-key
desc: The ip ospf authentication-key command configures the OSPFv2 authentication password for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf cost
cmd:
ip ospf cost interface_cost
no ip ospf cost
default ip ospf cost
desc: The ip ospf cost command configures the OSPFv2 cost for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf dead-interval
cmd:
ip ospf dead-interval time
no ip ospf dead-interval
default ip ospf dead-interval
desc: The ip ospf dead-interval command configures the dead interval for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf disabled
cmd:
ip ospf disabled
no ip ospf disabled
default ip ospf disabled
desc: The ip ospf disabled command disables OSPFv2 on the configuration mode interface without disrupting the OSPFv2 configuration.

title: ip ospf hello-interval
cmd:
ip ospf hello-interval time
no ip ospf hello-interval
default ip ospf hello-interval
desc: The ip ospf hello-interval command configures the OSPFv2 hello interval for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf message-digest-key
cmd:
ip ospf message-digest-key key_id md5 ENCRYPT_TYPE key_text
no ip ospf message-digest-key key_id
default ip ospf message-digest-key key_id
desc: The ip ospf message-digest-key command configures a message digest authentication key for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf network point-to-point
cmd:
ip ospf network point-to-point
no ip ospf network
default ip ospf network
desc: The ip ospf network point-to-point command sets the configuration mode interface as a point-topoint link.

title: ip ospf retransmit-interval
cmd:
ip ospf retransmit-interval period
no ip ospf retransmit-interval
default ip ospf retransmit-interval
desc: The ip ospf retransmit-interval command configures the link state advertisement retransmission interval for the interface.

title: ip ospf router-id output-format hostnames
cmd:
ip ospf router-id output-format hostnames
no ip ospf router-id output-format hostnames
default ip ospf router-id output-format hostnames

title: ip ospf transmit-delay
cmd:
ip ospf transmit-delay trans
no ip ospf transmit-delay
default ip ospf transmit-delay
desc: The ip ospf transmit-delay command configures the transmission delay for OSPFv2 packets over the configuration mode interface.

title: ip ospf priority
cmd:
ip ospf priority priority_level
no ip ospf priority
default ip ospf priority
desc: The ip ospf priority command configures OSPFv2 router priority for the configuration mode interface.

title: line system
desc: The line system command places the switch in the OSPF - Line System configuration mode.

title: log-adjacency-changes (OSPFv2)
cmd:
log-adjacency-changes detail
no log-adjacency-changes
default log-adjacency-changes
desc: The log-adjacency-changes command enables syslog messages to be sent when it detects OSPFv2 link state changes or when it detects that a neighbor has gone up or down.

title: maximum-paths (OSPFv2)

no maximum-paths
default maximum-paths

title: max-lsa (OSPFv2)
cmd:
max-lsa lsa_num [WARNING] [IGNORE_TIME][IGNORE_COUNT][RESET]
no max-lsa
default max-lsa

title: max-metric router-lsa (OSPFv2)
cmd:

title: show isis database

title: show isis dynamic flooding

title: show tunnel fib
desc: The show tunnel fib command that displays tunnels programmed in the tunnel FIB also includes the TILFA tunnels along with protected IS-IS SR tunnels.

title: BGP Conceptual Overview
desc: Using TCP sessions, the BGP protocol exchanges routing information among neighboring routers in different autonomous systems.

title: BGP Confederations
desc: BGP confederations divide an Autonomous System (AS) into subsystems (sub-ASs), each identified by a unique sub-AS number while appearing externally as a single AS.

title: BGP Convergence
desc: BGP supports convergence, which waits for all peers to join and receive all the routes from other peers.

title: BGP Communities
desc: A BGP community is a group of subnet address prefixes that share a common identifying attribute.

title: BGP Graceful Shutdown Community
desc: Autonomous System Boundary Routers (ASBRs) do not update all paths received from external BGP sessions and routers.

title: BGP Selective Route Download
desc: BGP Selective Route Download allows the learning and advertising of BGP routes without installing them in hardware.

title: BGP Route Reflector
desc: A BGP route reflector is a switch within an autonomous system that forwards route information learned from iBGP peers to other iBGP peers as an alternative to a full-mesh topology.

title: BGP Nexthop Resolution RIBs: EVPN and IPV4/6 Labeled-Unicast Support
cmd: BGP Nexthop Resolution RIBs: EVPN and IPV4/6 Labeled-Unicast Support
desc: Adds the BGP Nexthop Resolution RIBs feature for EVPN and labeled-unicast address families.

title: BGP Logical OR of Multiple Community Lists in the Same Match Command
cmd: BGP Logical OR of Multiple Community Lists in the Same Match Command
desc: In the multi-agent routing protocol model, the BGP agent now supports matching community lists with a logical OR via the route map match community or-results command (same applies for extended and large communities with match extcommunity and match large-community).

title: BGP Additional Paths Send Optimization
desc: BGP Add-Path TX, or send, allows for a BGP speaker to advertise multiple paths (instead of a single bestpath) for a prefix towards a peering BGP speaker.

title: BGP Flowspec
desc: The EOS Release 4.

title: BGP IPv4-mapped IPv6 Address Next Hops for IPv6 Labeled-Unicast Routes
cmd: BGP IPv4-mapped IPv6 Address Next Hops for IPv6 Labeled-Unicast Routes
desc: A BGP router in an IPv4 network may need to receive or send labeled-unicast routes to and from IPv6 networks.

title: BGP Monitoring Protocol for a Multi-agent Model
cmd: BGP Monitoring Protocol for a Multi-agent Model
desc: BGP Monitoring Protocol (BMP) allows a monitoring station to connect to a router and collect all BGP announcements received from the BGP peers of the router.

title: bfd (Router-PIM Sparse-mode)

bfd echo
no bfd echo
default bfd echo
desc: The bfd echo command enables the BFD echo function on the configuration mode interface.

cmd:
bfd interval transmit_rate min-rx receive_minimum multiplier factor
no bfd interval
default bfd interval
desc: The bfd interval command configures the BFD control packet transmission rate, minimum control packet receive rate, and the number of missed packets that will signal that the session is down.

cmd:
bfd local-address [address]

default bfd local-address [address]

no bfd neighbor [address]
default bfd neighbor [address]

cmd:
bfd slow-timer receive_minimum
no bfd slow-timer
default bfd slow-timer
desc: The bfd slow-timer command configures the minimum reception rate for BFD control packets which will be used if the BFD echo function is enabled.

title: bfd qos dscp
desc: The bfd qos dscp allows the configuration of the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) for BFD traffic originating from the local switch to take advantage of Quality of Service (QoS) on the network.

title: hardware acceleration disabled
desc: Hardware acceleration is enabled by default on supported platforms and no explicit configuration is needed.

title: neighbor bfd
cmd:
neighbor NEIGHBOR_ID bfd
no neighbor NEIGHBOR_ID bfd
default neighbor NEIGHBOR_ID bfd
desc: The neighbor bfd command enables Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) for use as a failure detection mechanism for Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) connections to the specified BGP neighbor or peer group.

title: pim ipv4 bfd

pim ipv4 bfd
no pim ipv4 bfd
default pim ipv4 bfd
desc: The pim ipv4 bfd command enables Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) on the configuration mode interface as a failure detection mechanism for Protocol-Independent Multicast Sparse-Mode (PIM-SM).

desc: The show ip as-path access-list command displays BGP filters on the switch.

title: address-family
cmd:
bgp [ipv4 | ipv6]
no bgp [ipv4 | ipv6]
default bgp [ipv4 | ipv6]
desc: The address-family command places the switch in address-family configuration mode to configure the address family setting of addresses configured as BGP neighbors.

title: address-family flow-spec
cmd:
address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] route-target redirect vrf lpm
address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] route-target redirect vrf lpm
address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] route-target redirect vrf lpm
desc: Use the address-family flow-spec command to filter or redirect DDoS traffic on edge routers.

title: address-family flow-spec neighbor
cmd:
address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] neighbor ip_address [activate | sendcommunity extended]
no address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] neighbor ip_address [activate | sendcommunity extended]
default address-family flow-spec [ipv4 | ipv6] neighbor ip_address [activate |
send-community extended]
desc: The address-family flow-spec neighbor command in the BGP Router Configuration Mode enables Flowspec on a BGP configuration to filter or redirect DDoS traffic on edge routers.

title: aggregate-address
cmd:
aggregate-address AGGREGATE_NET [AS_SET][SUMMARY][ATTRIBUTE_MAP][MATCH_MAP]
no aggregate-address AGGREGATE_NET
default aggregate-address AGGREGATE_NET
desc: The aggregate-address command creates an aggregate route in the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) database.

title: authentication-key
desc: The authentication-key command adds security to the TCP session to the BMP monitoring station.

title: bgp advertise-inactive
cmd:
bgp advertise-inactive
no bgp advertise-inactive
default bgp advertise-inactive
desc: By default, BGP will advertise only those routes that are active in the switch’s RIB.

title: bgp always-compare-med
cmd:
bgp always-compare-med
no bgp always-compare-med
default bgp always-compare-med
desc: The bgp always-compare-med command configures the switch to always consider Multi-Exit Discriminator (MED) values (also known as “metric”) in best-path selection.

title: bgp bestpath as-path ignore
cmd:
bgp bestpath as-path ignore
no bgp bestpath as-path ignore
default bgp bestpath as-path ignore
desc: The bgp bestpath as-path ignore command configures BGP to ignore the length of the Autonomous System (AS) path when comparing routes.

title: bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax
cmd:
bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax
no bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax
default bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax
desc: The bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax command allows multiple eBGP routes to a destination to be considered equal in ECMP if their AS paths are the same length despite having different autonomous systems in those paths.

title: bgp bestpath d-path
cmd:
bgp bestpath d-path
no bgp bestpath d-path
default bgp bestpath d-path
desc: The bgp bestpath d-path command configures BGP to use the domain path length in bestpath route selection.

title: bgp bestpath ecmp-fast
cmd:
bgp bestpath ecmp-fast
no bgp bestpath ecmp-fast
default bgp bestpath ecmp-fast
desc: By default, within an ECMP group the BGP best-path selection process prefers the active path (the first path received by the switch) unless a relevant tie-breaker is enabled.

title: bgp bestpath med confed
cmd:
bgp bestpath med confed [missing-as-worst]
no bgp bestpath med confed [missing-as-worst]
default bgp bestpath med confed [missing-as-worst]
desc: By default, paths originating within the same confederation as the switch and received from confederation peers do not have their Multi-Exit Discriminator (MED) values compared as part of the best-path selection

title: bgp bestpath med missing-as-worst
cmd:
bgp bestpath med missing-as-worst
no bgp bestpath med missing-as-worst
default bgp bestpath med missing-as-worst
desc: By default, BGP best-path selection considers a missing MED value to be 0, so paths with missing MED values will be preferred.

title: bgp bestpath tie-break cluster-list-length
cmd:
bgp bestpath tie-break cluster-list-length
no bgp bestpath tie-break cluster-list-length
default bgp bestpath tie-break cluster-list-length
desc: The bgp bestpath tie-break cluster-list-length command causes the best-path selection process to prefer the multipath route with the shortest CLUSTER_LIST length in case of a tie in step 10.

title: bgp bestpath tie-break router-id
cmd:
bgp bestpath tie-break router-id
no bgp bestpath tie-break router-id
default bgp bestpath tie-break router-id
desc: The bgp bestpath tie-break router-id command causes the best-path selection process to prefer the multipath route with the lowest ROUTER_ID in case of a tie in step 10.

title: bgp client-to-client reflection
cmd:
bgp client-to-client reflection
no bgp client-to-client reflection
default bgp client-to-client reflection
desc: By default, routes received from a route reflector client and selected as best routes are propagated to all BGP peers, including other route reflector clients.

title: bgp cluster-id
cmd:
bgp cluster-id ID_NUM
no bgp cluster-id
default bgp cluster-id
desc: When using route reflectors, an AS is divided into clusters.

title: bgp confederation identifier
cmd:
bgp confederation identifier as_number
no bgp confederation identifier
default bgp confederation identifier
desc: The bgp confederation identifier command configures the confederation identifier.

title: bgp confederation peers
cmd:
bgp confederation peers as_range
no bgp confederation peers as_range
default bgp confederation peers as_range
desc: The bgp confederation peers command configures a confederation consisting of sub-ASs.

title: bgp convergence time
cmd:
bgp convergence time timeout_range
no bgp convergence time
default bgp convergence time
desc: The bgp convergence time command configures the time BGP waits before declaring all BGP sessions as converged, whether or not a EOR message has been received.

title: bgp convergence slow-peer time
cmd:
bgp convergence slow-peer time timeout
no bgp convergence slow-peer time
default bgp convergence slow-peer time
desc: The bgp convergence slow-peer time command configures the idle peer time to wait for the slow peers to establish a session in a BGP convergence state.

title: bgp default
cmd:
bgp default ADDRESS_FAMILY
no bgp default ADDRESS_FAMILY
default bgp default ADDRESS_FAMILY
desc: The bgp default command configures the default address family activation level of all addresses configured as BGP neighbors.

title: bgp enforce-first-as

bgp enforce-first-as
default bgp enforce-first-as
no bgp enforce-first-as
desc: The bgp enforce-first-as command causes a forced comparison of the first Autonomous System (AS) in the AS path of eBGP routes received from BGP neighbors to the configured remote external peer Autonomous System Number (ASN).

title: bgp listen range

bgp listen range NET_ADDRESS [PEER-ID include router-id ] peer-group group_name [remote-as
as_number | peer-filter filter_name]
no bgp listen range NET_ADDRESS peer-group group_name
default bgp listen range NET_ADDRESS peer-group group_name
desc: The bgp listen range command identifies the BGP peering request from a range of IPv4 or IPv6 address, and names the dynamic peer group to which those peers belong to.

title: bgp log-neighbor-changes
cmd:
bgp log-neighbor-changes
no bgp log-neighbor-changes
default bgp log-neighbor-changes
desc: The bgp log-neighbor-changes command configures the switch to generate a log message when a BGP peer enters or exits the established state.

title: bgp monitoring
desc: The bgp monitoring command enters the Router BGP Monitoring Configuration Mode on the switch, and configures BGP Monitoring Protocol on the switch

title: bgp redistribute-internal (BGP)
cmd:
bgp redistribute internal
no bgp redistribute internal
default bgp redistribute internal
desc: The bgp redistribute-internal command enables the redistribution of iBGP routes into an Interior Gateway Protocol (IGP).

desc: The bgp rib bestpaths monitoring globally enables or disables exporting all Local Routing Information Bases (Loc_RIB) for all BGP VRFs in a BGP VRF configuration.

cmd:
bgp rib received routes monitoring disabled
no bgp rib received routes monitoring disabled
default bgp rib received routes monitoring disabled
desc: The bgp rib received routes monitoring disabled allows filtering of Adjacency-RIB-In (AdjRIB-In) exports for BGP Monitoring Protocol and prevents a VRF from exporting the Adj-RIB-In.

desc: The bgp route install-map command enables BGP Selective Route Download on the switch and allows the learning and advertising of the BGP routes without installing them in hardware.

title: bgp route-reflector preserve-attributes
cmd:
bgp route-reflector preserve-attributes [always]
no bgp route-reflector preserve-attributes
default bgp route-reflector preserve-attributes
desc: The bgp route-reflector preserve-attributes command configures the switch, when operating as a BGP route reflector, to preserve the BGP attributes of re-advertised routes.

title: clear bgp history

desc: To clear all messages for a peer or group of peers, use the clear bgp history command .

title: clear ip bgp
desc: The clear ip bgp command removes learned BGP routes from the routing table, reads all routes from designated peers, and sends routes to those peers as required.

title: clear ip bgp counters
desc: The clear ip bgp counters command resets general statistics of peers.

title: clear ip bgp errors
desc: The clear ip bgp errors command resets the error statistics and history of peers.

title: clear ip bgp neighbor
desc: The clear ip bgp neighbor command clears BGP neighbors belonging to the IPv4 transport address family.

title: clear ipv6 bgp
desc: The clear ipv6 bgp command removes learned BGP routes from the routing table, reads all routes from designated peers, and sends routes to those peers as required.

title: clear ipv6 bgp counters
desc: The clear ipv6 bgp counters command resets general statistics of peers.

title: clear ipv6 bgp errors
desc: The clear ipv6 bgp errors command resets the error statistics and history of peers.

title: clear ipv6 bgp neighbor
cmd: clear ipv6 bgp neighbor [*] [vrf vrf_name] [reason message]
desc: The clear ipv6 bgp neighbor command clears BGP neighbors belonging to the IPv6 transport address family.

title: connection address

title: connection keepalive
cmd: connection keepalive [idletime seconds | probe-interval seconds | probe-count num]

cmd: connection mode [[active port port_number] | [reconnect-timer timer_value] | [connection mode passive]]
desc: The connection mode command configures TCP connection parameters for a BMP monitoring station.

title: distance bgp

distance bgp external_dist [INTERNAL_LOCAL]
no distance bgp
default distance bgp
desc: The distance bgp command assigns an administrative distance to routes that the switch learns through BGP.

title: dynamic peer max

dynamic peer max maximum
no dynamic peer max
default dynamic peer max
desc: The dynamic peer max command limits the number of dynamic BGP peers allowed on the switch.

title: export-policy received routes

title: graceful-restart stalepath-time
cmd:
graceful-restart stalepath-time interval
no graceful-restart stalepath-time
default graceful-restart stalepath-time
desc: The graceful-restart stalepath-time command specifies the maximum time that stale routes from a restarting BGP neighbor will be retained after a BGP session is re-established with that peer.

cmd:
graceful-restart helper long-lived
no graceful-restart helper long-lived
default graceful-restart helper long-lived
desc: The graceful-restart helper command enables BGP graceful restart helper mode on the switch for all BGP neighbors.

default ip as-path access-list list_name

default ip as-path regex-mode

no ip community-list list_name
default ip community-list list_name

ip community-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
no ip community-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
default ip community-list regexp list_name
desc: The ip community-list regexp command creates and configures a BGP access list based on BGP communities.

cmd:
ip extcommunity-list list_name {deny | permit} COMM_1 [COMM_2...COMM_n]
no ip extcommunity-list list_name
default ip extcommunity-list list_name

title: ip extcommunity-list regexp
cmd:
ip extcommunity-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
no ip extcommunity-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
default ip extcommunity-list regexp list_name
desc: The ip extcommunity-list regexp command creates an extended community list to filter VRF routes or for link bandwidth (LBW) advertisement.

title: ip large-community-list regexp
cmd:
ip large-community-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
no ip large-community-list regexp list_name {deny | permit} reg_exp
default ip large-community-list regexp list_name
desc: The ip large-community-list regexp command creates and configures a BGP access list based on BGP large communities.

cmd:
no match as-range [as_number1] [as_number2] result {accept | reject} group_name
default match as-range [as_number1] [as_number2] result {accept | reject} group_name

default maximum-paths

monitoring bgp rib bestpaths
monitoring bgp rib bestpaths disable

desc: The monitoring port command configures a port number for stations in passive mode.

title: monitoring qos
desc: The monitoring qos command configures the DSCP value for BMP.

title: monitoring received
cmd:
monitoring received routes [pre-policy | post-policy]
no monitoring received routes [pre-policy | post-policy]

title: neighbor

neighbor neighbor_ID as-path remote-as replace out
no neighbor neighbor_ID as-path remote-as replace out

title: show ip bgp regexp

title: show ipv6 bgp summary

title: Router Control Functions Commands

title: code

desc: The compile command compiles all modified code units from the pending buffer and unmodified code units from the running configuration.

desc: The pull command allows the population of the pending buffer for the specified code unit with the contents of the file located at the specified URL.

title: push
