desc: • IGMP • IGMP Snooping

title: IGMP Snooping L2 Report Flooding
desc: L2 report flooding is an IGMP snooping feature that forwards membership report messages to specified ports.

title: IGMP Snooping Filters
desc: IGMP snooping filters assign IGMP profiles only to Layer 2 interfaces, and for Layer 3 interfaces use multicast boundary filters to control the multicast groups that the interfaces can join.

title: v2
desc: Po1

title: IGMP Host Proxy
desc: Interfaces on the switch can be configured to serve as IGMP host proxies.

title: IGMP Host Proxy Configuration
desc: Enabling IGMP Host Proxy Enable PIM MBR on the interface using the pim ipv4 border-router command.

title: or Later
desc: ASU switch N/A Set the Interface Restart Query to 5000: switch(config)# ip igmp snooping interface-restartquery 5000 Action - Configure on ASU switch just prior to initiating ASU.

title: and Later
desc: No losses within a one second window.

title: IGMP and IGMP Snooping Commands
desc: IGMP Configuration Commands (Interface Configuration Mode) • igmp query-max-response-time • ip igmp last-member-query-count • ip igmp last-member-query-interval • ip igmp query-interval • ip igmp router-alert

title: clear ip igmp group
cmd: clear ip igmp group [ gp_addr][ interface INT_ID]
desc: The clear ip igmp group command deletes IGMP cache entries as follows: • clear ip igmp group all entries from the IGMP cache.

title: clear ip igmp snooping counters
desc: The clear ip igmp snooping counters command resets the snooping message counters for the specified interface.

title: clear ip igmp statistics
desc: The clear ip igmp statistics command resets IGMP transmission statistic counters for the specified interface.

title: igmp query-max-response-time
cmd:
igmp query-max-response-time period
no igmp query-max-response-time
default igmp query-max-response-time
desc: The igmp query-max-response-time command configures the query-max-response-time variable for the configuration mode interface.

title: ip igmp
desc: The ip igmp command enables IGMP on a routed interface or on SVI (VLAN interface) without enabling PIM.

title: ip igmp host-proxy
cmd:
ip igmp host-proxy [GROUP_ADDRESS [SOURCE_ADDRESS]]|[access-list acl]
no ip igmp host-proxy [GROUP_ADDRESS [SOURCE_ADDRESS]]
default ip igmp host-proxy [GROUP_ADDRESS [SOURCE_ADDRESS]]
desc: The ip igmp host-proxy command enables the IGMP host proxy service on the configuration mode interface.

title: ip igmp host-proxy report-interval
cmd:
ip igmp host-proxy report-interval period
no ip igmp host-proxy report-interval
default ip igmp host-proxy report-interval
desc: The ip igmp host-proxy report-interval command configures the period between unsolicited join reports that the switch sends as an IGMP host proxy from the configuration mode interface to a sender network after a (S,G) or (*,G) entry arrives in the multicast route (mroute) table.

title: ip igmp host-proxy version
cmd:
ip igmp host-proxy version version_number
no ip igmp host-proxy version
default ip igmp host-proxy version
desc: The ip igmp host-proxy version command configures the version number to be used in unsolicited reports when the interface is serving as an IGMP host proxy.

title: ip igmp last-member-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp last-member-query-count number
no ip igmp last-member-query-count
default ip igmp last-member-query-count
desc: The ip igmp last-member-query-count command specifies the number of query messages the switch sends in response to a group-specific or group-source-specific leave message.

title: ip igmp last-member-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp last-member-query-interval period
no ip igmp last-member-query-interval
default ip igmp last-member-query-interval
desc: The ip igmp last-member-query-interval command configures the switch's transmission interval for sending group-specific or group-source-specific query messages from the configuration mode interface.

title: ip igmp profile
cmd:
ip igmp profile profile_name
no ip igmp profile profile_name
default igmp profile profile_name
desc: The ip igmp profile command places the switch in the IGMP-profile configuration mode to configure an IGMP profile.

title: ip igmp query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp query-interval period
no ip igmp query-interval
default ip igmp query-interval
desc: The ip igmp query-interval command configures the frequency at which the configuration mode interface, as an IGMP querier, sends host-query messages.

title: ip igmp router-alert
cmd:
ip igmp router-alert DISPOSITION
no ip igmp router-alert
default ip igmp router-alert
desc: The ip igmp router-alert command configures the switch disposition of inbound IGMP packets to the configuration mode interface based on the presence of the router-alert option in the IP header.

title: ip igmp snooping
cmd:
ip igmp snooping
no ip igmp snooping
default ip igmp snooping
desc: The ip igmp snooping command enables snooping globally.

title: ip igmp snooping filter
cmd:
ip igmp snooping filter profile_name
no ip igmp snooping filter [profile_name]
default ip igmp snooping filter [profile_name]
desc: The ip igmp snooping filter command applies the specified IGMP snooping profile to the configuration mode interface.

title: ip igmp snooping interface-restart-query
cmd:
ip igmp snooping interface-restart-query query_time
no ip igmp snooping interface-restart-query
default ip igmp snooping interface-restart-query
desc: The ip igmp snooping interface-restart-query command configures the interface startup initial query time used for IGMP query spoofing.

title: ip igmp snooping proxy
cmd:
ip igmp snooping proxy
no ip igmp snooping proxy
default ip igmp snooping proxy
desc: The ip igmp snooping proxy command enables snooping proxy globally.

title: ip igmp snooping querier
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier
no ip igmp snooping querier
default ip igmp snooping querier
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier command enables the snooping querier globally, which controls the querier for VLANs that are not configured with a snooping querier command.

title: ip igmp snooping querier address
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier address ipv4_address
no ip igmp snooping querier address
default ip igmp snooping querier address
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier address command sets the global querier source IP address, which specifies the source address for packets transmitted from VLANs for which a querier address (ip igmp snooping vlan querier address) is not configured.

title: ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-count number
no ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-count
default ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-count
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-count command configures the global igmp snooping querier last member query count (LMQC) value.

title: ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-interval period
no ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-interval
default ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier last-member-query-interval command sets the global IGMP snooping last member query interval.

title: ip igmp snooping querier max-response-time
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier max-response-time resp_sec
no ip igmp snooping querier max-response-time
default ip igmp snooping querier max-response-time
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier max-response-time command specifies the global maxresponse-time value.

title: ip igmp snooping querier query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier query-interval query_sec
no ip igmp snooping querier query-interval
default ip igmp snooping querier query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier query-interval command sets the global query interval.

title: ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count number
no ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
default ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count command configures the global startup query count value.

title: ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-interval period
no ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
default ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-interval command configures the global startup query interval value.

title: ip igmp snooping querier version
cmd:
ip igmp snooping querier version version_number
no ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
default ip igmp snooping querier startup-query-count
desc: The ip igmp snooping querier version command configures the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping querier version on the configuration mode interfaces.

title: ip igmp snooping report-flooding switch-port
cmd:
ip igmp snooping report-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
no ip igmp snooping report-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
default ip igmp snooping report-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
desc: The ip igmp snooping report-flooding switch-port command specifies Ethernet ports or port channels that can forward IGMP membership report messages for all VLANs where L2 report flooding is enabled.

title: ip igmp snooping restart query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping restart query-interval query_sec
no ip igmp snooping restart query-interval
default ip igmp snooping restart query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping restart query-interval command sets the query interval for all VLANs during an IGMP snooping restart.

title: ip igmp snooping robustness-variable
cmd:
ip igmp snooping robustness-variable robust_value
no ip igmp snooping robustness-variable
default ip igmp snooping robustness-variable
desc: The ip igmp snooping robustness-variable command configures the robustness variable for snooping packets sent from any VLAN.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan command enables snooping on the specified VLANs if snooping is globally enabled.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan fast-leave
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range fast-leave
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range fast-leave
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range fast-leave
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan fast-leave command enables fast-leave processing on specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan max-groups
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range max-groups quantity
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range max-groups
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_rangemax-groups
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan max-groups command configures the quantity of multicast groups that the specified VLAN forwarding table can contain.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan member
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_num member ipv4_addr interface STATIC_INT
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_num member ipv4_addr interface STATIC_INT
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_num member ipv4_addr interface STATIC_INT
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan member command adds ports as static members to a multicast group.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan multicast-router
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range multicast-router interface STATIC_INT
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range multicast-router interface STATIC_INT
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range multicast-router interface STATIC_INT
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan multicast-router command adds a multicast router as a static port to the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan proxy
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan [ID | range]proxy
no ip igmp snooping vlan [ID | range] proxy
default ip igmp snooping vlan [ID | range] proxy
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan proxy command enables snooping proxy on individual VLAN.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier command controls the querier for the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier address
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier address ipv4_address
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier address
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier address
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier address command sets the source address for query packets sent from specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier last-member-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier last-member-query-count number
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier address
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier address
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier last-member-query-count command specifies an IGMP snooping querier last-member-query-count (LMQC) value for the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier last-member-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier last-member-query-interval period
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier last-member-query-interval
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier last-member-query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier last-member-query-interval command configures last-member-query-interval for packets sent from the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier max-response-time
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier max-response-time resp_sec
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier max-response-time
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier max-response-time
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier max-response-time command configures max-responsetime for packets sent from the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier query-interval query_sec
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier query-interval
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier query-interval command sets the query interval for the specified VLAN.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier startup-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-count number
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-count
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-count
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier startup-query-count command specifies the startup query count value for the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier startup-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-interval period
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-interval
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier startup-query-interval
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier startup-query-interval command specifies the startup query interval value for the specified VLANs.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan querier version
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier version version_number
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier version
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range querier version
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan querier version command configures the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) snooping querier function on the VLAN.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan report-flooding
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range report-flooding
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range report-flooding
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_range report-flooding
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan report-flooding command enables L2 report flooding on the specified VLANs if report flooding is globally enabled.

title: ip igmp snooping vlan report-flooding switch-port
cmd:
ip igmp snooping vlan v_range report-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
no ip igmp snooping vlan v_range report-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
default ip igmp snooping vlan v_rangereport-flooding switch-port INTERFACE
desc: The ip igmp snooping vlan report-flooding switch-port command configures Ethernet ports or port channels to forward IGMP membership report messages for a specified VLAN range where L2 report flooding is enabled.

title: ip igmp startup-query-count
cmd:
ip igmp startup-query-count number
no ip igmp startup-query-count
default ip igmp startup-query-count
desc: The ip igmp startup-query-count command specifies the number of query messages that an interface sends during the startup interval defined by ip igmp startup-query-interval.

title: ip igmp startup-query-interval
cmd:
ip igmp startup-query-interval period
no ip igmp startup-query-interval
default ip igmp startup-query-interval
desc: The ip igmp startup-query-interval command specifies the configuration mode interface IGMP startup period, during which query messages are sent at an accelerated rate.

title: ip igmp static-group
cmd:
ip igmp static-group group_address [SOURCE_ADDRESS]
no ip igmp static-group group_address [SOURCE_ADDRESS]
default ip igmp static-group group_address [SOURCE_ADDRESS]
desc: The ip igmp static-group command configures the configuration mode interface as a static member of a specified multicast group.

title: ip igmp static-group acl
cmd:
ip igmp static-group acl list_name
no ip igmp static-group acl
default ip igmp static-group acl
desc: The ip igmp static-group acl command configures the configuration mode interface as a static member of the multicast groups specified by an IP Access Control List (ACL).

title: ip igmp static-group range
cmd:
ip igmp static-group range GROUP_ADDR[SOURCE_ADDR]
no ip igmp static-group range GROUP_ADDR[SOURCE_ADDR]
default ip igmp static-group range GROUP_ADDR[SOURCE_ADDR]
desc: The ip igmp static-group range command configures the configuration mode interface as a static member of multicast groups specified by an address range.

title: ip igmp version
cmd:
ip igmp version version_number
no ip igmp version
default ip igmp version
desc: The ip igmp version command configures the Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) version on the configuration mode interface.

title: permit / deny
desc: The permit command configures the configuration mode IGMP profile as a permit list.

title: range
cmd:
range init_address [UPPER_RANGE]
no range init_address [UPPER_RANGE]
default range init_address [UPPER_RANGE]
desc: The range command specifies an address range for the configuration mode IGMP profile.

title: show igmp snooping querier
desc: The show igmp snooping querier command displays snooping querier configuration and status information.

title: show igmp snooping querier counters
desc: The show igmp snooping querier counters command displays the counters from the querier, as learned through Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP).

title: show igmp snooping querier membership
desc: The show igmp snooping querier membership command displays the membership from the querier, as learned through Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP).

title: show ip igmp groups
desc: The show ip igmp groups command displays multicast groups that have receivers directly connected to the switch, as learned through Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP).

title: show ip igmp groups count
desc: The show ip igmp groups count command displays the number of multicast groups that are joined across the specified interfaces.

title: show ip igmp host-proxy config-sanity
desc: The show ip igmp host-proxy config-sanity command displays diagnostic information about an IGMP host proxy configuration.

title: show ip igmp host-proxy interface
desc: The show ip igmp host-proxy interface command displays per-interface IGMP host-proxy configuration information, including the IGMP groups joined on the interface.

title: show ip igmp interface
desc: The show ip igmp interface command displays multicast information about the specified interface.

title: show ip igmp profile
desc: The show ip igmp profile command displays the contents of the specified IGMP profile.

title: show ip igmp snooping
desc: The show ip igmp snooping command displays the switch IGMP snooping configuration.

title: show ip igmp snooping counters
desc: The show ip igmp snooping counters command displays the number of IGMP messages sent and received through each switch port.

title: show ip igmp snooping counters ethdev-pams
cmd: show ip igmp snooping counters ethdev-pams
desc: The show ip igmp snooping counters command displays the number of dropped IGMP packets messages sent and received through each switch port at the kernel level.

title: show ip igmp snooping groups
cmd: show ip igmp snooping groups proxy [VLAN_ID][PORT_INT][GROUPS][DATA local]
desc: The show ip igmp snooping groups command displays IGMP snooping statistics.

title: show ip igmp snooping groups count
cmd: show ip igmp snooping groups [VLAN_ID][PORT_INT] count [DATA]
desc: The show ip igmp snooping groups count command displays the number of multicast groups on the switch.

title: show ip igmp snooping mrouter
desc: The show ip igmp snooping mrouter command displays the status of dynamic and static multicast router ports.

title: show ip igmp snooping report-flooding
desc: The show ip igmp snooping report-flooding command displays IGMP snooping L2 report flooding configuration and status information.

title: show ip igmp static-groups
desc: The show ip igmp static-groups command displays information about all configured IGMP multicast static groups.

title: show ip igmp static-groups acl
desc: The show ip igmp static-groups acl command displays information about the IGMP multicast static groups that are configured by the specified Access Control List (ACL).

title: show ip igmp static-groups group
desc: The show ip igmp static-groups group command displays information about all specified IGMP multicast static groups.

title: show ip igmp statistics
desc: The show ip igmp statistics command displays IGMP transmission statistics for the specified interface.

title: Protocol Independent Multicast
desc: Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) distributes multicast data using routes gathered by other protocols.

title: PIM Sparse Mode
desc: In PIM-SM, each host (sender or receiver) is associated with a Designated Router (DR) that acts for all directly connected hosts in PIM-SM transactions, and trees are unidirectional.

title: PIM Commands
desc: PIM Configuration Commands (Global) • anycast-rp • candidate • fast-reroute • group-expiry-timer • holdtime • ip pim dr-notify-delay • log neighbors • register local-interface • router pim bidirectional

title: anycast-rp
cmd:
anycast-rp rp_addr peer_addr [REGISTER]
no anycast-rp rp_addr [peer_addr]
default anycast-rp rp_addr [peer_addr]
desc: The anycast-rp command configures the switch as a member of an anycast-RP set and establishes a communication link with another member of the set.

title: candidate
cmd:
candidate INTERFACE [HASHMASK_LENGTH][INTERVAL_PERIOD][PRIORITY_NUM]
no candidate [priority][interval]
default candidate[priority][interval]
desc: The candidate command configures the switch as a Candidate BSR router (C-BSR).

title: fast-reroute
cmd:
fast-reroute acl_name
no fast-reroute acl_name
default fast-reroute acl_name
desc: The fast-reroute command enables Multicast only Fast Re-Route (MoFRR) to minimize traffic loss in a network when a link or node failure occurs.

title: group-expiry-timer
cmd:
group-expiry-timer value
no group-expiry-timer value
default group-expiry-timer value
desc: The group-expiry-timer command sets the group-expiry-timer in seconds after which a group with no activity gets deleted from the PIM Rendezvous Point (RP) tree.

title: holdtime
cmd:
holdtime period
no holdtime
default holdtime
desc: The holdtime command specifies the value the switch inserts in the holdtime parameter field in BootStrap Messages (BSM) that it sends.

title: ip pim dr-notify-delay
cmd:
ip pim dr-notify-delay notify_delay_time
no ip pim dr-notify-delay
default ip pim dr-notify-delay
desc: The ip pim dr-notify-delay command sets the designated router (DR) notification delay.

title: ipv4
desc: The ipv4 command places the switch in the IPv4 submode for the PIM configuration mode in which it is entered.

title: log neighbors
cmd:
log neighbors
no log neighbors
default log neighbors
desc: The log neighbors command configures the switch to generate a log message when a neighbor entry is added or removed from the PIM Neighbor table.

title: pim bsr ipv4 border
cmd:
pim bsr ipv4 border
no pim bsr ipv4 border
default pim bsr ipv4 border
desc: The pim bsr ipv4 border command prevents the switch from sending BootStrap router Messages (BSMs) over the configuration mode interface.

title: pim ipv4 bidirectional
cmd:
pim ipv4 bidirectional
no pim ipv4
no pim ipv4 bidirectional
default pim ipv4
default pim ipv4 bidirectional
desc: The pim ipv4 bidirectional command enables PIM bidirectional and IGMP (router mode) on the configuration mode interface.

title: pim ipv4 border-router
cmd:
pim ipv4 border-router
no pim ipv4 border-router
default pim ipv4 border-router
desc: The pim ipv4 border-router command configures the configuration mode interface as a PIM Multicast Border Router (MBR).

title: pim ipv4 dr-priority
cmd:
pim ipv4 dr-priority level
no pim ipv4 dr-priority [level]
default pim ipv4 dr-priority [level]
desc: PIM-SM uses these criteria for electing a Designated Router (DR): • If at least one router does not advertise a DR priority value, then PIM-SM elects the router with the highest IP address as the DR.

title: pim ipv4 hello count
cmd:
pim ipv4 hello count [multiple]
no pim ipv4 hello count [multiple]
desc: The pim ipv4 hello count command sets the PIM hello count for the interface being configured.

title: pim ipv4 hello interval
cmd:
pim ipv4 hello interval period
no pim ipv4 hello interval [period]
default pim ipv4 hello interval [period]
desc: The pim ipv4 hello interval command specifies the transmission interval between PIM hello messages originating from the configuration mode interface.

title: pim ipv4 join-prune count
cmd:
pim ipv4 join-prune count count_value
no pim ipv4 join-prune count count_value
default pim ipv4 join-prune count count_value
desc: The pim ipv4 join-prune count command configures the number of times a join or prune messages can be missed before the upstream neighbor time expires.

title: pim ipv4 join-prune interval
cmd:
pim ipv4 join-prune interval [period]
no pim ipv4 join-prune interval [period]
default pim ipv4 join-prune interval [period]
desc: The pim ipv4 join-prune interval command specifies the period between join or prune messages that the configuration mode interface originates and sends to the upstream RPF neighbor.

title: pim ipv4 neighbor-filter
cmd:
pim ipv4 neighbor-filter access_list
no pim ipv4 neighbor-filter
default pim ipv4 neighbor-filter
desc: The pim ipv4 neighbor-filter command configures the configuration mode interface to filter PIM control packets on the basis of neighbor addresses listed in a specified standard access list.

title: pim ipv4 sparse-mode
cmd:
pim ipv4 sparse-mode
no pim ipv4
no pim ipv4 sparse-mode
default pim ipv4
default pim ipv4 sparse-mode
desc: The pim ipv4 sparse-mode command enables PIM IPv4 Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) and IGMP (router mode) on the configuration mode interface.

title: register checksum
desc: The register checksum command in the Router PIM Sparse-Mode IPv6 Configuration Mode configures a checksum to use for PIM IPv6 register messages.

title: register local-interface
cmd:
register local-interface INT_NAME
no register local-interface
default register local-interface
desc: The register local-interface command programs the switch to fill the source field in all outbound PIM SM register packets with the IP address of a specified interface or the incoming interface of the group specified by the message.

title: router pim bidirectional
desc: The router pim bidirectional command places the switch in the router-pimbidirectional configuration mode.

title: router pim bsr
desc: The router pim bsr command places the switch in the router-pim bsr configuration mode.

title: router pim sparse-mode
desc: The router pim sparse-mode command places the switch in the router-pim sparse-mode configuration mode.

title: rp address
cmd:
rp address rp_addr [MULTICAST_SUBNET][HASHMASK_LENGTH][BSR_OVERRIDE]
no rp address rp_addr [MULTICAST_SUBNET]
default rp address rp_addr [MULTICAST_SUBNET]
desc: The rp address command configures the address of a Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) static Rendezvous Point (RP) for a specified multicast subnet.

title: rp allow
cmd:
rp allow
no rp allow
default rp allow
desc: The rp allow command accepts and allows PIM (*,G) join message with an RP address that is different from the configured RPs for that particular (*,G).

title: rp candidate
cmd:
any order.
rp candidate INTERFACE [GROUP_ADDR][PRIORITY_NUM][INTERVAL_PERIOD]
no rp candidate [INTERFACE][GROUP_ADDR]
no rp candidate [INTERFACE] interval
no rp candidate [INTERFACE] priority
default rp candidate [INTERFACE][GROUP_ADDR]
default rp candidate [INTERFACE] interval
default rp candidate [INTERFACE] priority
desc: The rp candidate command configures the switch as a Candidate Rendezvous Point (C-RP).

title: rp hash algorithm modulo
cmd:
rp hash algorithm modulo
no rp hash algorithm modulo
default rp hash algorithm modulo
desc: The rp hash algorithm modulo command configures the load-balancing scheme across available Rendezvous Points (RP).

title: rp-candidate advertisement-filter
cmd:
rp-candidate advertisement-filter access-list access-list_name
no rp-candidate advertisement-filter access-list access-list_name
default rp-candidate advertisement-filter access-list access-list_name
desc: The rp-candidate advertisement-filter command filters the RP candidate advertisements from certain IP addresses.

title: sg-expiry-timer
cmd:
sg-expiry-timer period
no sg-expiry-timer
default sg-expiry-timer
desc: The sg-expiry-timer command configures the (S, G) expiry timer interval for PIM-SM (S, G) multicast routes.

title: show ip pim bsr
desc: The show ip pim bsr command displays the switchs BootStrap Router (BSR) information.

title: show ip pim config-sanity
desc: The show ip pim config-sanity command displays diagnostic information about the switch's PIM configuration.

title: show ip pim interface
desc: The show ip pim interface command displays information about interfaces configured for PIM.

title: show ip pim neighbor
desc: The show ip pim neighbor command displays information about Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) neighbors discovered by hello messages.

title: show ip pim protocol counters
desc: The show ip pim protocol command displays statistics about Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) control messages sent and received by the switch.

title: show ip pim register-source
desc: The show ip pim register-source command displays the name of the interface from where the switch derives the IP address that it uses to fill the source field in all outbound PIM SM register packets.

title: show ip pim rp
desc: The show ip pim rp command displays the status and multicast group of each cached Rendezvous Point (RP).

title: show ip pim rp-candidate
desc: The show ip pim rp-candidate command displays the Rendezvous Point (RP) that is used for a specified multicast group.

title: show ip pim rp-hash
desc: The show ip pim rp-hash displays the group to RP-hash mapping for the specified group and the list of qualifying candidate RPs.

title: show ip pim upstream joins
desc: The show ip pim upstream joins command displays the join messages that the switch is scheduled to send.

title: spt threshold
cmd:
spt threshold {0 | infinity}[match list acl_name]
no spt threshold {0 | infinity}[match list acl_name]
default spt threshold{0 | infinity} [match list acl_name]
desc: The spt threshold command configures Shortest Path Tree (SPT) threshold actions for IPv4 multicast groups.

title: ssm range
desc: The ssm range command defines the Source Specific Multicast (SSM) range of IP multicast addresses.

title: MSDP Introduction
desc: Arista switches support the following MSDP features: • Basic MSDP speaker functions • MSDP peer configuration - description, connect-source interface, keepalive time, and hold time.

title: MSDP Description
desc: The Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) defines a topology connecting Protocol Independent Multicast sparse mode (PIM-SM) domains.

title: MSDP Speakers
desc: An MSDP speaker is a router in a PIM-SM domain that has MSDP peering sessions with MSDP peers in other domains.

title: Network Configuration
desc: The TCP connections between RPs are defined either through an underlying unicast routing table or by configuring a default MSDP peer.

title: Default MSDP Peers
desc: The default peer is the MSDP peer from which the MSDP speaker accepts SA messages.

title: MSDP Exchange Processes
desc: • Control Information Exchange • MSDP Data Exchange

title: MSDP Data Exchange
desc: This sequence describes the exchange of multicast data across PIM domains through MSDP: 1.

title: MSDP Configuration
desc: These sections describe the configuration of the switch as an MSDP speaker and the establishment of MSDP peering sessions.

title: MSDP Speaker Configuration
desc: The switch is configured as an MSDP speaker when MSDP is enabled.

title: originator-id local-interface
cmd:
originator-id local-interface INTERFACE
no originator-id local-interface INTERFACE
default originator-id local-interface INTERFACE
desc: The originator-id local-interface command configures an originator ID to replace the Rendezvous Point (RP) address in Source-Address (SA) messages that it originates as an MSDP speaker.

title: peer
desc: The peer command configures the specified address as an MSDP peer, enables MSDP on the switch if it was not previously enabled, and places the switch in Router MSDP Peer Configuration Mode for the specified peer.

title: sa-filter in
cmd:
sa-filter in list list_name
no sa-filter in
default sa-filter in
desc: The sa-filter in command assigns an IP Access Control List (ACL) as a filter for inbound Source-Active (SA) messages from the configuration-mode MSDP peer connection.

title: sa-filter out
cmd:
sa-filter out list list_name
no sa-filter out
default sa-filter out
desc: The sa-filter out command assigns an IP Access Control List (ACL) as a filter for outbound SourceActive (SA) messages to the configuration-mode MSDP peer connection, after which the permit statement allows matching SAs to be advertised outbound to the peer.

title: sa-limit
cmd:
sa-limit quantity
no sa-limit
default sa-limit
desc: The sa-limit command specifies the maximum number of Source-Active messages from a specified MSDP peer that the switch allows in the SA cache.

title: show ip msdp peer
desc: The show ip msdp peer command displays information about specified MSDP peers.

title: show ip msdp pim sa-cache
desc: The show ip msdp pim sa-cache command displays the SA cache for the local PIM domain configured on the switch.

title: show ip msdp sa-cache
desc: The show ip msdp sa-cache command displays contents of the Source-Active (SA) message cache.

title: show ip msdp sanity
desc: The show ip msdp sanity command performs a consistency check between the multicast routing table and the MSDP Source-Address (SA) caches.

title: show ip msdp summary
desc: The show ip msdp summary command displays a list of peer addresses, the operational status of the peer, and the number of Source-Active messages in the SA cache from that peer.

title: Listen 0
desc: Up

title: show msdp mesh-group
desc: The show msdp mesh-group command displays the mesh group membership of MSDP peers that are configured on the switch.

title: MSDP Commands
desc: MSDP Configuration Commands (Global) • connection retry interval • forward register-packets (MSDP) • group-limit • ip msdp rejected-limit • originator-id local-interface • peer • router msdp MSDP Peer Configuration Commands

title: clear ip msdp sa-cache
desc: The clear ip msdp sa-cache command removes contents of the Source-Active (SA) message cache.

title: connection retry interval
cmd:
connection retry interval connect_retry
no connection retry interval connect_retry
default connection retry interval connect_retry
desc: The connection retry interval command specifies the period that the switch waits after an MSDP peering session is reset before trying to reestablish the session.

title: default-peer
desc: The default-peer command configures the specified MSDP peer connection as a default peer on the switch.

title: description (MSDP)
cmd:
description description_string
no description
default description
desc: The description command associates descriptive text with the configuration-mode MSDP peer.

title: disabled (MSDP)
desc: The disabled command closes the peering session with the specified MSDP peer by terminating the TCP connection between the switch and the peer.

title: forward register-packets (MSDP)
cmd:
forward register-packets
no forward register-packets
default forward register-packets
desc: The forward register-packets command enables the encapsulation of multicast data packets on the sending MSDP peer and the decapsulation and forwarding on a receiving MSDP peer.

title: group-limit
cmd:
group-limit quantity sourcesrc_subnet
no group-limit quantity source src_subnet
default group-limit quantity source src_subnet
desc: The group-limit command specifies the maximum number of Source-Active (SA) messages that the switch allows in the SA cache for a specified multicast group address.

title: ip msdp rejected-limit
cmd:
ip msdp rejected-limit quantity
no ip msdp rejected-limit
default ip msdp rejected-limit
desc: The ip msdp rejected-limit command specifies the maximum number of rejected Source-Active messages that the switch allows in the SA cache.

title: keepalive (MSDP)
cmd:
keepalive keep_alive hold_time
no keepalive
default keepalive
desc: The keepalive command configures the MSDP keep-alive and hold time intervals for a specified MSDP peer connection.

title: local-interface
cmd:
local-interface interface
no local-interface
default local-interface
desc: MSDP peering sessions are established over a TCP connection.

title: mesh-group
cmd:
mesh-group group_name
no mesh-group group_name
default mesh-group group_name
desc: The mesh-groupcommand configures the configuration-mode MSDP peer connection as an MSDP mesh group member.

title: show msdp rpf-peer
desc: The show msdp rpf-peer command displays MSDP information for the peer from which the switch accepts SA messages for a specified Rendezvous Point (RP).

title: gPTP

title: VXLAN Introduction

title: VXLAN Architecture
desc: The VXLAN architecture extends a Layer 2 network by connecting VLANs from multiple hosts through UDP tunnels called VXLAN segments.

title: EVPN Overview
desc: Ethernet VPN (EVPN) is a standards-based BGP control plane to advertise MAC addresses, MAC and IP bindings and IP Prefixes.

title: EVPN Terminology
desc: The EVPN standard, in the context of an NVO environment, defines the functionality for delivering multitenant Layer 2/3 VPN services using either VXLAN, NVGRE, or MPLS over GRE encapsulation across a common physical IP infrastructure.

title: EVPN Service Models
desc: An EVPN Instance (EVI), can contain, one or more Layer 2 broadcast domains (VLANs).

title: VLAN Based Service Interface
desc: In the VLAN-based service there is a one-to-one mapping between the VLAN-ID and the MAC-VRF of the EVPN instance.

title: VLAN Bundle Service Interface
desc: In the VLAN bundle service, there is a many-to-one mapping between the VLAN-IDs and the MAC-VRF of the EVPN instance.

title: VLAN Aware Bundle Service Interface
desc: In the VLAN aware bundle service, there is a many-to-one mapping between the VLAN-IDs and the MACVRF of the EVPN instance.

title: EVPN MPLS LAYER 3 VPN (Type-5 Route)
desc: Ethernet VPN (EVPN) is an extension of the BGP protocol introducing a new address family: Layer 2 VPN (address family number 25) / EVPN (subsequent address family number 70).

title: EVPN VXLAN IPv6 Overlay
desc: Beginning with EOS Release 4.

title: iBGP as PE-CE Protocol for BGP and EVPN L3 VPNs
cmd: iBGP as PE-CE Protocol for BGP and EVPN L3 VPNs
desc: EOS supports iBGP as the Provider Edge (PE) - Customer Edge (CE) as an extension to BGP EVPN Layer 3 (L3) VPNs.

title: EVPN Layer 3 Core Operations
desc: The EVPN standard defines a number of operations and functionality to allow the dynamic learning of MAC and IP bindings, management of MAC moves (VM/host mobility), ARP suppression, automated discovery of

title: ARP Suppression
desc: Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) suppression can be supported on the remote VTEPs, providing the option to advertise the MAC and IP binding in the type-2 route.

title: IP VPN
desc: RFC 4364 allows Service Providers and Enterprises to use their backbone infrastructure to provide services to multiple customers, or internal departments, while performing the following functions: • Maintaining privacy.

title: TAP Aggregation Introduction
desc: Ethernet-based switches are commonly deployed in dedicated networks to support Test Access Point (TAP) and mirror port traffic toward one or more analysis applications.

desc: These sections describe TAP aggregation, timestamps, and keyframes: • TAP Aggregation • Timestamps and Keyframes

title: TAP Aggregation
desc: Test Access Point (TAP) aggregation is the accumulation of data streams and the subsequent dispersal of these streams to devices and applications that analyze, test, verify, parse, detect, or store data.

cmd: show flow tracking sampled ipfix template [data | options | tracker]
desc: The show flow tracking sampled ipfix template command displays information about the exported IPFIX data templates and options templates.

title: template interval
cmd:
template interval interval
no template interval
default template interval
desc: The template interval command configures the interval at which templates are exported for a specific exporter.

title: tracker
cmd:
tracker tracker_name
no tracker tracker_name
default tracker tracker_name
desc: The tracker command configures a sampled flow tracker for a device.

title: sFlow
desc: This chapter describes Arista's implementation of sFlow, including configuration instructions and command descriptions.

title: sFlow Conceptual Overview
desc: Sampled Flow Tracking Commands

title: sFlow Technology
desc: Sampled Flow (sFlow) provides a multi-vendor sampling technology that continuously monitors application level traffic flow at wire speed simultaneously on all interfaces.

title: sFlow Agents
desc: The sFlow agent runs a software process as part of the network management software within an Arista switch.

title: sFlow Collector
desc: An sFlow collector runs software that analyzes and reports network traffic.

title: sFlow Data
desc: The sFlow Agent uses two forms of sampling: statistical packet-based sampling of switched flows and timebased sampling of network interface statistics.

title: sFlow and Mirroring
desc: sFlow and Mirroring supports using the same interface for a mirroring session and sFlow at the same time.

title: sFlow Subinterfaces
desc: The sflow sample [input | output] subinterface command configures ifIndex values for subinterfaces on input and output ports to be included in the sFlow sample.

title: sFlow Commands
desc: Global Configuration Commands • sflow agent • sflow datagram • sflow destination • sflow extension bgp • sflow extension router • sflow extension switch • sflow extension vpls • sflow polling-interval

title: clear sflow counters
desc: The clear sflow counters command resets the global sFlow statistics, which includes the number of samples and sample pool.

title: sflow agent
cmd:
sflow agent address [ipv4_address | ipv6_address]
no sflow agent address
default agent address
desc: The sflow agent command allows you to specify a specific type of address used by sFlow to identify the datagrams exported to the collector and uniquely identifies the origin of the datagrams.

title: sflow datagram
desc: The sflow datagram command configures the size of the sFlow datagram from 200 to 1500 bytes to help avoid fragmentation of the datagram.

title: sflow destination
cmd:
sflow destination dest_addr [UDP_PORT]
no sflow destination dest_addr [UDP_PORT]
default sflow destination dest_addr [UDP_PORT]
desc: The sflow destination command specifies an sFlow collector IPv4 or IPv6 address and, optionally, a UDP port (the default is 6343).

title: sflow enable
cmd:
sflow enable
no sflow enable
default sflow enable
desc: The sflow enable command enables sFlow on the configuration mode interface.

title: sflow extension bgp
cmd:
sflow extension bgp
no sflow extension bgp
default sflow extension bgp
BGP sFlow export behaves differently on different switch platforms as follows:
use software simulation to determine the egress port.
from the first path of the ECMP route and a BGP next hop of 0.
but identifies the BGP next hop as 0.
use software simulation to determine the egress port.
desc: The sflow extension bgp command enables BGP export to sFlow.

title: sflow extension evpn mpls
desc: The sflow extension evpn mpls command adds support for providing information related to the bridging domain in sFlow packet samples for traffic forwarded through Layer 2 EVPN MPLS.

title: sflow extension router
cmd:
sflow extension router
no sflow extension router
default sflow extension router
desc: By default, the switch provides extended router information in sFlow packets, including the IP version and address of the next-hop router and source and destination mask lengths.

title: sflow extension switch
cmd:
sflow extension switch
no sflow extension switch
default sflow extension switch
desc: By default, the switch provides extended switch information in sFlow packets, including source and destination VLANs and priorities.

title: sflow extension switch vlan
cmd:
sflow extension switch vlan forwarding
no sflow extension switch vlan forwarding
default sflow extension switch vlan forwarding
desc: The sflow extension switch vlan command in the Global Configuration Mode feature supports providing the VLAN from the bridged packet instead of decoding it from the Ethernet frame and reports the input and output VLAN fields.

title: sflow extension vpls
cmd:
sflow extension vpls
no sflow extension vpls
default sflow extension vpls
desc: The sflow extension vpls command enables VPLS exporting to sFlow configuration.

title: sflow interface
desc: The sflow interface command enables the sampling of egress packets from an interface on the switch.

title: sflow polling-interval
cmd:
sflow polling-interval interval_period
no sflow polling-interval
default sflow polling-interval
desc: The sflow polling-interval command specifies the counters polling interval.

title: sflow qos
cmd:
sflow qos dscp dscp_value
no sflow qos dscp
default sflow qos dscp
desc: The sflow qos command configures Quality of Service (QoS) for sFlow datastreams by specifying the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value for sFlow export datagrams.

title: sflow run
cmd:
sflow run
no sflow run
default sflow run
desc: The sflow run command globally enables sFlow on the switch.

title: sflow sample
cmd:
sflow sample sample_rate
no sflow sample
default sflow sample
desc: The sflow sample command sets the packet sampling rate.

title: sflow sample input
desc: The sflow sample input command enables sFlow sampling on Switched Virtual Interfaces (SVIs) or subinterfaces, and explicitly targets the input direction.

title: sflow sample output interface
desc: By default, sFlow samples include information about the output interface of the sampled packet.

title: sflow sample rewrite
desc: The sflow sample rewrite command configures sFlow to modify the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value of the sampled packet IP header as included in the sFlow datagram.

title: sflow sample truncate
desc: The sflow sample truncate command configures the maximum length of the sampled packet header included in the sFlow datagram exported to a collector.

title: sflow sample vxlan
desc: The sflow sample vxlan command explicitly configures sFlow to intelligently sample and parse VXLAN encapsulated traffic.

title: sflow source
cmd:
sflow source source_addr
no sflow source
default sflow source
desc: The sflow source command specifies the IP address used in the Agent address filed of the IPv4 or IPv6 sFlow datagram that the switch sends to the collector.

title: sflow source-interface
cmd:
sflow source-interface INT_NAME
no sflow source-interface
default sflow source-interface
desc: The sflow source-interface command specifies the source IP address set to the IP address of the specified interfaces that the switch sends to the collector.

title: sflow vrf
desc: The sflow vrf command configures sFlow parameters on Virtual Routing Forwarding instances, and enables sFlow functionality to be established specific VRFs.

title: show sflow
desc: The show sflow command displays configured sFlow parameters, operational status, and statistics.

title: show sflow interfaces
desc: The show sflow interfaces command displays the interfaces with sFlow enabled.

title: SNMP
desc: This chapter describes the Arista switch SNMP agent and contains these sections: • SNMP Introduction • SNMP Conceptual Overview • Configuring SNMP • SNMP Commands

title: SNMP Introduction
desc: Arista Networks switches support many standard SNMP MIBs, making it easier to integrate these platforms into existing network management infrastructures.

title: SNMP Conceptual Overview
desc: Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) is a protocol that provides a standardized framework and a common language to monitor and manage network devices.

title: SNMP Structure
desc: The SNMP framework has three parts: • SNMP manager: The SNMP manager controls and monitors network host activities and is typically part of a Network Management System (NMS).

title: SNMP Notifications
desc: SNMP notifications are messages, sent by the agent, informing of an event or a network condition.

title: SNMP Versions
desc: Arista switches support the following SNMP versions: • SNMPv1: The Simple Network Management Protocol, defined in RFC 1157.

title: SNMP Authentication and Encryption Methods
desc: The following are the SNMP stronger Authentication and Encryption methods: • Authentication • MD5 • SHA-1 • SHA-224 • SHA-256 • SHA-384 • SHA-512 • Encryption • AES • DES • AES-192 • AES-256 Note: Use the following minimums when using the stronger SN…

title: octet
desc: OctetString hexadecimal string opaque Opaque hexadecimal string string OctetString ascii string SET Requests For SET requests, script is passed three lines on stdin: the command (set), and the requested OID, and the

title: SNMP IP Address ACL Support
desc: SNMP IP address ACL support provides the ability to add access-lists to limit the source addresses that can be used to query the SNMP server.

title: Show Commands
desc: Use the show snmp ipv4 access-list summary command to display an abbreviated output of an IPv4 access-list.

title: SNMP Commands
desc: Global Configuration Commands • no snmp-server • snmp-server chassis-id • snmp-server community • snmp-server contact • snmp-server enable traps • snmp-server engineID local • snmp-server engineID remote

title: no snmp-server
desc: The no snmp-server and default snmp-server commands disable Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) agent operation by removing all snmp-server commands from running-config.

title: show snmp community

title: show snmp engineID
desc: The show snmp engineID command displays the local SNMP engine information configured on the switch.

title: show snmp group
desc: The show snmp group command shows the names of configured SNMP groups along with the security model, and view status of each group.

title: show snmp local-interface
desc: The show snmp local-interface command displays the interface whose IP address is the source address for SNMP traps.

title: show snmp mib

title: show snmp notification

title: show snmp notification host
desc: The show snmp notification host command displays information for Simple Network Management Protocol notification.

desc: The show snmp user command shows information about Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) users.

desc: The show snmp v2-mib contact command displays the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) system contact string configured by the snmp-server contact command.

title: show snmp v2-mib location
desc: The show snmp v2-mib location command displays the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) system location string.

title: show snmp view
desc: The show snmp view command displays the information of a Simple Network Management Protocol configuration and the associated MIB.

desc: To display information about all sham-links configured for a provider edge (PE) router in the Virtual Private Network (VPN) backbone, use the show ip ospf sham-links command in EXEC mode.

title: show ip ospf statistics
desc: To display Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) shortest path first (SPF) calculation statistics, use the showipospfstatisticscommand in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.

title: show ip ospf summary-address
desc: To display a list of all summary address redistribution information configured under an Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) process, use the showipospfsummary-addresscommand in EXEC mode.

title: show ip ospf timers rate-limit
desc: To display all of the link-state advertisements (LSAs) in the rate limit queue, use the showipospftimersrate-limitcommand in privileged EXEC mode.

title: show ip ospf traffic
desc: To display parameters and the current state of Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) virtual links, use the showipospfvirtual-links command in EXEC mode.

title: show ipv6 ospf
desc: To display general information about Open Shortest Path First ( OSPF) routing processes, use the show ipv6 ospf command in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.

title: show ipv6 ospf traffic
desc: To ensure compatibility with the showiptraffic command, only checksum errors are displayed.

title: show ospfv3 multi-area
desc: To display information about the Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) multiarea interfaces, use the show ospfv3 multi-area command in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.

title: show ospfv3 sham-links
desc: To display parameters and the current state of Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) sham links, use the show ospfv3 sham-links command in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.

title: show tech-support ospf
desc: To run show s that display OSPF information that is useful to Cisco Technical Support personnel in resolving issues, use the show tech-support ospf command in the privileged EXEC mode.
